Very Comfy with Stretch but causes excessive wrinkling on my legs. I have somewhat thin legs but the pants still bunched up. Its worse when I've been sitting and stand up. If I pull the pants up so that they sit very high just above my belly button, the stretch isn't as bad but as you can see in the second photo, that will cause a camel toe, so I need to wear a long top over them. Even with all that, I have to say they are very comfortable and will wear them around the house and with tall boots. I'm 5'3" about 138lbs and purchased a regular medium and the length and waist are otherwise perfect.

I absolutely love, love, love these pants! I am 5'6, weigh about 140lbs and I ordered these pants in a size large with a 30.5 inch inseam and in the color black and they fit very well. They are form fitting and hug your butt and hips nicely and just really accentuate your curves. The fabric is soft like a brushed cotton and they are thin and lightweight. They are high waisted and have good tummy control. They have 2 front pockets and 2 back pockets. The front pockets are surprisingly wide and deep and I can easily fit my phone in there. These pants look great, feel great and are everything I look for in a pair of leggings. 5 out of 5 stars for sure!

I was skeptical about these because I don't love the slight kick flare to them but then I put together a pretty cute fit with them and for the price I think I will keep them. I probably could have gone with my usual size 30 but I sized up to a 31 and they fit comfortably with extra room in the thighs. Pros: comfortable, good length for a 5'4" girlie looking for a crop hitting just above the ankle, nice wash, value for money, quality denim with a moderate stretch and decent drape Cons: run a bit small, wish they were straight leg instead of slightly flared

I'm pleasantly surprised with these jeans. They run slightly small but they have a lot of stretch so that's probably for the best. I think they are curve hugging in a flattering way. I am a pear-shaped size 10. They arrived a little wrinkled but other than that they look really nice. The patch pockets are a nice touch. They're just slightly cropped on me, and I have about a 28" inseam. I wasn't sure about the oversized cuff trend at first but for a fairly reasonable price I'm enjoying the look.

Just received the pants today, and I'm OBSESSED. These jeans are very close perfection in my opinion! For starters, I love the distressed look without having holes or looking TOO distressed. They look like something that would say Ralph Lauren on the label tbh. The fabric is soft (not a stiff denim), not too thin or too thick, and they have a nice amount of stretch to them. I'm usually a size 10 (but sometimes 12 depending on the brand). For reference, I'm 5'6" and 170 lbs. I ordered a size 12 because I don't like anything tight around my waist and I was concerned with them running small. The 12 fits nicely and I DO think that if I ordered a size 10, they may have pulled around the waist just enough to be able to see some of the white interior of the pockets (I hope that makes sense). The legs have just the right amount of "barrel" to them (not as barrel shaped as FP jeans). These jeans are PERFECT for anyone looking to try out the barrel jeans trend without committing to a full on barrel jean. I'd rate these jeans 10/10 and ended up ordering 2 more in other colors!!! WORTH EVERY PENNY!!!

Comfortable stretchy jeans with a great cuff bottom. My only issue is the washed out spots in the front and back - just not what I was looking for but a great fit! High waisted and straight leg. These are a medium and I am 5’6”.

I ordered 36” inseam, color Blue, size L. I am 6’1” and 175 pounds. These fit WONDERFULLY and are so conformable. They hit the floor but don’t puddle which is just what I wanted. I don’t know how why they say 36” is for much taller women unless they want shorter pants - I do not want them any shorter. Or longer. They do stink and need to be aired out, like others have commented. They are lightweight and very stretchy, as others have said, too. One star off because I feel they are too expensive for the style (or lack of style), but there sadly is not a lot of options so they’re doable. They do seem good quality. I long for a high-waisted and/or tummy control version of these! I saw one comment calling out over-fading on the backside for this same color iirc. Mine are not overly faded at all, thankfully.

These arrived over the wknd. The size I ordered def needed to size down. I’m normally between 6/8 and I’m 135ibs 5’1-2ish. These jeans didn’t feel bad material wise but def they were not skinny jeans at all. The bottoms were def a little wider. Would have been okay if that is what I thought I was buying. Returning because the size 8 is too big for me. I went with the recommendation size. Also returning because it’s not what was advertised.

This pair of elastic waist with button/zipper fly and a drawstring are easily the most comfortable pair of jeans I own! The fabric is of a medium thickness and is quite soft feeling, inside and outside. The fabric has a good bit of stretch in it. Enough that I would say they could even be worn for lounge pants! They are well made and I found no flaws or loose threads when they arrived. There are 2 large/deep patch pocket on the front and 2 normal sized pockets on the back of them. The drawstring can be easily removed if it is not needed or wanted. They have belt loops for those who would like to add a belt to their attire. The bottom hems are a little wider than most, but I think that adds to the cute style of these jeans. The waist has sort of a ruffled look to it on the upper band part where the belt loops are. The elastic is a loose, easy feeling fit that does not bind or pinch at all. They sort of remind me of a Daisy Mae look, from the 'Lil Abner comics of old, except they are longer. I think the look makes them fun and the comfort makes them a favorite for me! I ordered them in a size XL, which is my regular size for pants of any type. They fit super comfy on me with perhaps a little room to spare due to the loose fitting elastic waistband. I usually never tie drawstrings and just let them hang, but this pair I think I will need that drawstring tied for the extra snugness. The length is also perfect for my shortness! This is a pair I will not need to wear heels or platforms, but can wear my "barefoot" type shoes with them. For reference: I'm 5' 4" tall, 33-ish waist, 44" hips, and 182 lbs. These are good quality made jeans and I highly recommend them. They are fun and so easy to wear for any occasion. The current price (12-26-2025) of $36.99 is a fair value in my opinion. I would buy these again, without hesitating.

The color is close to the picture. I’m concerned about the zipper quality. But if it holds up I will definitely buy more. I’m 5’4” and weigh 160lbs. I bought size 10 regular.

I bought 2 colors of otherwise identical jeans, sassafras and orzo. I love the fit of both on my waist and legs. I am 5'2" and 115lbs. These jeans are insanely comfortable. BUT... they are both size 2S and the orzo jeans are 2" longer than the sassafras. I don't understand why b/c they are the same style and size. Additionally, I'll need to return/replace the sassafras because one of the eyelets is malformed and quite sharp and I keep cutting my fingers on it!

Everyday jeans for work etc. nice quality and fit. I could’ve gone to an 8 but the ten is comfy. Great value and I love the distressing. You must make these just a tad longer is all. I’ve got long legs !! Will buy another ! Love them. I’m 5’6. 145 pounds and over 60 years old.

I ordered these jeans in size small horizon blue. I wear a size 4 in most pants, I'm 5'4" 134lbs. They are a little roomy for me but I'm old and still wear skinny jeans! The stitching and the fabric are nice quality. I think I will get a lot of use out of these when the weather cools off here in Florida!
I struggle with gapping in the back - not with these. I am 5’8 210 48 hips - they are stretchy, comfortable, and flattering.
Fit really cute and are comfortable. I’m 5’3 and 180lbs and got a size Medium. They aren’t as wide leg as I’d hoped but still fit great.
Fit really cute and are comfortable. I’m 5’3 and 180lbs and got a size Medium. They aren’t as wide leg as I’d hoped but still fit great.
Very comfortable and stretchy. I am 5'8 and they are a little short hence the 4 stars. Overall love the pants can dress up or down.

Super comfy and stretchy. I ordered the 10 and it’s a 30” waist. Too big so I ordered the 8 and it’s a 29” (it says on the tag inside the pants). I could’ve even gone smaller but I like how they fit now. Only downside is that odd smell some pants come with from the factories.

These Vetinee barrel jeans looked so cute in the listing photos, and I really wanted them to work. Unfortunately, once I put them on, the fit just wasn’t flattering on my body (my husband immediately agreed), even though the sizing felt true to size. For reference, I ordered a size XL. The denim itself is a nice weight and the stretch is comfortable—honestly, it’s on the “very flexible” side, which helps because the fit is a little snug through the hips. I also like how the high waist sits. That said, the front pockets tend to gap open, which exposes the pocket lining and looks a little sloppy. Pocket-wise, the front pockets are well placed, and the back pockets are functional, but they sit lower than I expected. After washing, the top edges of the back pockets folded over, which I didn’t love (though everything else seemed to wash fine). For length reference, I’m 5’6” with long legs, and the 26” inseam hits around the tops of my shoes. It’s a little longer than what I’d consider truly cropped—more of an ankle-length fit on me. Also, I have noticed that the inseam seems very wide at the bottom, meaning that there’s a lot of fabric between my ankles. Overall, I think these could be really cute on the right body shape, but on me the barrel/balloon leg was a miss. If you’re considering them, I’d recommend thinking carefully about your proportions and how you like this silhouette to fit before ordering.

I’m picky. I’m used to skinny or tight styled pants, wanted to try the loose fit. I’m 5’4 and about 145 pounds, and I would say a pretty typical white girl body. I ordered a size six and these fit exactly how I wanted them to. I’m going to order a couple more because they’re perfect for a casual night out or even the office. Recommend! And I agree with many of the other reviewers regarding paying attention to the sizing. I made my sizing decision based on the reviews.
Love these jeans. I'm 5'9 and this hit just perfect. I bought the 32"inseam, but I'm more long torsoed. They have some elastic give in them so they're very flattering.

I haven't worn jeans in ten years. I had these in my cart for months and rly happy I decided to finally get them! They fit true to size. For reference, I’m 5’6”, 175lbs. 32 waist, 45 hips. Usually I’m a size 12/14, but I ordered these in 16 for a looser fit and glad I did! The front pleats are well placed for a flattering fit, especially if you’re curvier. The material is fairly thick, but also stretchy. Surprisingly comfortable! They hold up well in the wash & don’t shrink when dried. The rise hits at just the right spot — although the waist is a little baggy on me, so I have to wear a belt. Very pleased with the quality. I never spend more than $20 on pants, but these are worth the money for sure. 10/10 recommend. If you’re questioning getting them, do itttt:)

These jeans are cute and quite comfortable because of the slight stretch to the denim. And they're not too stretchy to the point that they feel stretched out by the end of the day. The style is cute, I love the pockets on the front and the seams above the back pockets, this adds a nice stylish touch. My one qualm with these jeans is that they are not full length as pictured in the listing. I am just under 5'6" and my legs are proportionate to my torso. I do not normally need to wear 'long' or 'tall' sized jeans. These could've definitely been longer. The cuff is sewn, so if I want to uncuff them to make them longer, I'll have to rip open the stitching, which isn't a huge stitch, but the pants could've just been a bit longer. I like the color and the fit overall. Again, they are comfortable too.

I already have so many pairs of jeans but I fell in love with the large front pockets. The only thing that gave me pause was selecting the size. I wear a women's size 4 or 6 depending on the brand but the measurements listed for these jeans make them look like they run a size small. I decided to go with the 6 and I'm glad I did because it fits perfectly. There is a good bit of stretch and they feel very comfortable on. They are very well made for the price point - $35.99 at the time of this review. For reference I'm wearing the Size 6. My hips are 38", my waist is 29" and I'm 5"4.

I think these jeans are cute and they are crazy comfortable! Based on the comfort alone, I would keep them if they were half the price ($20-$25 would be reasonable.) However, for $40, I won’t be able to keep them since I will need that money to keep shopping around for the look that I want. Cons: They run really small and they don’t fit like they do in the product photos. I normally wear an 8-10 in jeans, depending on the brand, and I sized up to a 12 because I wanted these to fit nice and loose like they did on the model in the first product photo. In person, they’re much shorter so I can’t roll them up at the ankle like I wanted to. I think the size 12 fits me like most size 10 jeans normally would. A little loose in the waste with some wiggle room. Pros: would be cute on someone shorter and very skinny, the material seems like decent quality and there is a comfortable amount of stretch to them If you want to achieve the look in the product photos, I would recommend trying to go up by 3-4 sizes. For reference, I am 5’5”, a pear shape with a short torso, and I was 165 lbs when this photo was taken.
New favorite pants! I will buy in other colors. These are very comfortable and professional for the classroom. I am 5'4" and 150lbs. Size 10 short is a perfect fit.

As others have said its good to get a larger size, than you normally wear in jeans. I normally wear a 10/12 so I got a 14 short. I think regular length would have been too long and I think the petite/short size is a little too short. I’m 5’3 160lbs.
Bought this for my daughter for school. She’s 120lbs and 5’2. It fits her perfectly. I got a size 8 for her. It’s roomy and very stretchy. I bought more for her
I sized up for a baggier fit and love them! They are my new favorite jeans. I am 135lbs, 5'6", apple shape, I ordered an XL.

The jeans fit well. I’m in between sizes right now and got the 28x32. I’m 5’4” and 142lb. They are a bit snug in the hip area but they are comfortable in the waist. I wish I got the 28x30 but they didn’t have that choice. I’m keeping them. I usually have problems finding jeans that fit my hip/thigh area since my pelvis is kind of wide, but these look very nice.
I wanted to love these but length on the long were even too short. I’m 5’7” for reference. Love the fit but sadly will return because of length

I am pleasantly surprised by these jeans. The stretch makes them extremely comfortable. I am 5'4" and the length is great. The light color is as pictured. The fit is flattering. I would highly recommend!

I ordered these Sidefeel Women's Wide Leg Baggy Jeans in the (6, Dark Blue) version... and I like them, but they don't fit me exactly how I want them to yet. I sized down because I am on a weightloss journey, thinking that because they have an elastic waistband and wide legs that they would work. While they are passable, I will be letting my daughter wear these until I am ready. For reference, I am 5'5ish, 160ish pounds, 34D bust and 28" waist. Here's what I know about these baggy jeans: •Excellent quality garment, free of defects •Made of soft, stretchy, denim-like material that is medium weight •Elastic waistband with drawstring is extremely comfortable •The legs are baggy, but not super sloppy looking •Dark blue color is rich and vibrant •Versatile-- can be styled a variety of different ways •Wash up well •I choose to hang dry these and them fluff them in the dryer to remove the wrinkles Overall, these are fun jeans! At the time ordering they were a little less than $37-- because they are well made and on trend, I think they are a fantastic value for the money. MOM APPROVED! RECOMMEND!!!

These jeans are perfect for fall. I am sure excited to wear them. They are a nice dark wash, with a stretchy waisted band. The material is very stretchy and it’s a thinner jean. I am 5’2 and the length is perfect. Also they are very high waisted.
Love these jeans. I’m 5”3’ around 135lbs and the short size is perfect for when I’m wearing sneakers or flats. They’re also very comfortable.

I am surprised to find I love these jeans! Shopping for pants as a short, curvier (particularly in the thighs) woman can be difficult. I am also postpartum and finding jeans that fit and are comfortable is tricky at the moment. The elastic wasteband is fantastic. I ordered a medium since I am postpartum. I am 5'2" and 145 lbs (if I were my usual weight of 130-135 lbs I'd have ordered a small). The waist is very comfortable and the length is perfect. That said, the length may be short if you are average height or taller. My two gripes: the sewing of the elastic band in the front of the pants seems like a mistake -- the band ends in a different place on the right side and the left side. And the shoestring lace is just stylistically not something I like. It may work better with a lighter wash, but I'll likely remove the string and wear without.

Nice and stretchy. Good fit a little long for a petite person I'm 180 5'1. I would have given a better score but they are too long i purchased a size 14
I love these jeans! It’s hard to find a good pair of maternity jeans. I am 5’4” and about 130lbs at 20 weeks. These jeans fit perfectly!
I love these jeans! It’s hard to find a good pair of maternity jeans. I am 5’4” and about 130lbs at 20 weeks. These jeans fit perfectly!

I had low expectations for the price, but these are surprisingly flattering and comfortable. One caveat is that you need to size up. I usually wear a size 4 jeans so I ordered a size 6. They fit comfortably but are tighter in the waist and hips than my barrel jeans from Anthro. So if you want more of a baggy look at the top, you'll want to go up two sizes. I am 5'2" and the length is just right for me, so for a taller person they might be too short. The fabric is super stretchy and comfortable, color is good. They are cheaper looking than the Antro/Free People versions, but also $125 less. :-) Updated: after a couple of washings and frequent wear, the jeans are pilling. I have never seen denim pill before, so it must be from the quantity of viscose that makes them soft? I'm glad I have a pill shaver but just thought I'd mention it.

I wish that I could convey to you how soft these bad boys are. You know when you have a pair of jeans that are getting almost threadbare in some places because they’re so worn in they don't even feel like the thick jeans material anymore? That’s what these feel like - only they aren’t threadbare, just soft. These don’t even feel like jeans to me at all. They’re stretchy from the top to your bottom, then they get loose and comfy from the legs down. If they feel a bit tight at first, just give it a minute, because they will conform to you. The fit is a little higher on the waist than I typically like, and I kept trying to pull them down more on my hips, but they’d just creep back up 😆. They have real pockets on the back AND front, which is nice because we sometimes have fake pockets on the front. There IS a fake zipper since these are pull on pants. I am 5’1,” about 120 and I ordered the XS. It’s possible that I should’ve ordered the small, but these still fit really well. They are a bit tighter on my bottom and around the thighs in the back than I’d like, but my husband thought they looked really good. When I ordered these, the petite wasn’t an option, nor was the dark color - both of which I would normally get. I purchased the blue, which is a bit lighter than any other jeans that I own, and which also is a bit more unforgiving in my opinion. However, I’m really glad I got them. I think once I get to my usual weight of about 107-110, I will, personally, feel more comfortable in them. They will still be a tad long, though, which I’m totally used to. 😁. However, if you are the same size as me, they will absolutely fit you - it’s more a “me” thing than a “not fitting” thing.

These jeggings are so comfortable! The fabric is heavier than most legging fabric, and has just the right amount of "give" to keep the leggings in place. Even after bending over, reaching, stretching, twisting - I didn't have to pull up or readjust the waistband. There is the typical jeans contrasting stitching along the pockets (2 rear, 2 side), the leg hems, and the fake zipper on the front. I machine washed and lined dried these with no issues. For reference, I am 5'4" and weigh about 150 lbs, ordered Large, and they are the right length and fit. Well made, comfortable, at a good price - very pleased with these jeggings.

I liked how these looked online, but in person they definitely fit different. First, they are much longer than expected, I was thinking these would be slightly cropped. I am 5'7" so typically I have trouble finding pants long enough and when I'm actually looking for a cropped leg I get these that hit just at my ankle. The front pockets are so small that they are unusable, and look like they are on backwards. The back is not flattering. It is stretchy fabric but seems to suck in to the wrong curves. Wouldn't be able to size up because it'd then be too big in the waist, so I'm not sure what body type this is designed for, but mine isn't it!

I’ve got these skinny Jeans in size 8. At 5’1”, the size 8 fits true to the size chart, with the ankle-length cut hitting perfectly at my ankles. The dark blue color is true to the product images. The stitching is well-executed, with no loose threads. The fabric, a blend of 72% cotton-very soft, breathable, and comfortable with just the right amount of stretch and no weird smell. The designer front and back pockets add a unique, chic touch. Easy to care for with machine washing, these jeans are a practical, stylish choice for everyday wear or date nights. I paired them with a t-shirt for casual outings and a blazer for a more dressed-up look for a great price. Great value--a must have.

UPDATE: Size 6 fit great. Could maybe even do a 4 but I carry my weight in my waist so I'm sticking with the 6. These have a true wide, baggyish leg so if you're looking for a slim leg, keep looking. I hung out on these on a weene afternoon and they were super comfy. The black and the grey are barely indistinguishable - the black is a washed black, and the grey is pretty dark. I took a pic along with some of my other grey jeans. Original review: These are made of a thicker but still quite stretchy denim. I'm 5'6" 145-150 lbs and they're ankle length, hitting just a smidge above my ankle bones. The size 8 was a loose in the waist - I will probably return and reorder in a size 4 or 6 (probably 6) since they're likely to stretch out during the day. Waist is comfy though! The black is a washed black, not true black.

Fit me well. I was skeptical after reading they were short for taller girls, but I am 5'6" and they are a good length on me. I am also 6 months pregnant and 157 lbs and they fit pretty good! I definitely like and want in other colors now.

I am 5’4 and 107 lbs. Ordered a size 4 and the fit is generous. The hem is almost to the floor. I like the fit and it is very flattering. I no complaints about anything.
I am 5’ 9” 150Ibs and can never find the right jeans to fit with the right length. Love these light colored jeans, 34” length, size medium.
I am 5’ 9” 150Ibs and can never find the right jeans to fit with the right length. Love these light colored jeans, 34” length, size medium.

I ordered a size large which fits perfectly. They’re very stretchy and comfortable. The jeans are not heavy which is a good thing. The length is somewhat long on me but I’m 5’4. But I’m very satisfied with the purchase. I love ❤️

I am always afraid to buy clothing online, but these are genuine so they fit perfect and the fabric has a little forgiving room but fix so perfectly. The come down a little past my ankles, which I love. I’m 5 foot three 127 pounds (photo is to show my shape) and I got the size 28 regular and yes I got the size a tiny bit bigger on me because I get nervous that if the fabric is hard that it will give me muffin tops but these are so soft and comfortable. They look great.
So comfy and really great quality! Tall girl friendly -5’7 and perfect length maybe a little on the longer side

I am usually in between a medium and a large pants. These are a medium and are form fitting. Fabric is thick yet has ample stretch. Not see through and good quality. Comfy and my length is perfect, I am 5ft 6 inches, 152 pounds. Loving this color!

I am usually in between a medium and a large pants. These are a medium and are form fitting. Fabric is thick yet has ample stretch. Not see through and good quality. Comfy and my length is perfect, I am 5ft 6 inches, 152 pounds. Loving this color!

These jeans are well made, and have a thicker material - but they're still stretchy. The main complaint that I have is that the sizes are kind of weird. They just have small, medium, large, etc., but girls come in a lot more sizes, and it's kind of difficult to tell which size will be best. There is a size guide, but most people aren't going to be the exact porportions for one of these sizes. My 15-year-old is tall-ish (almost 5' 8") and slender, and the small size fits her waist almost perfectly; however, the length is super short, and really doesn't look great. If she pulls them down low, then the length looks alright, but it really doesn't look good either. Then my 13-year-old daughter is much shorter (probably about 4' 10" or so), and the length is about perfect, but the waist is huge on her - there's no way to even alter them, or cinch them up to make them fit. It's kind of sad, because these are the 'trendy' jean style right now, but you really have to be a specific body type for them to work for you.

I am 5’4, 36DD 150 lbs and ordered a medium. Very flattering fit! I’ve lost a lot of weight over the past few years, since my son was born. And (in my opinion) I have a case of elephant butt. From the back, my butt looks like an elephant’s face into the trunk area. Some saggy wrinkles and not much contouring. My husband insists I am wrong but… when you know you know 🤷‍♀️ It’s not what it used to be! Well ladies - these are a bit snug and I wish they were a different Jean fabric - but they make my butt look FABULOUS again! Perky and like I have a booty, not a sad saggy old lady butt. Finally!!!! Very cute, easy to pull on. Overall 5 stars. Very happy, flattering things can be hard to find for my shape!

These size XL jeggings were selected for a woman 5'8" tall, 170 lb., with 44" hips. They fit quite comfortably....true to size. They have a denim appearance with real front and back pockets. The fabric is soft and stretchy. I like them.

Hybrid & Company Women's Slim Boot Cut Stretch Pants So let me start off by saying that the first impression quality is great on these. There were a few loose threads in the zipper area, but most of those were easily pulled free and its no big deal. Moving on from that, I got the size 18 in grey twill. I usually buy 16A jeans. These jeans are very long. I admit I didnt pay attention to the inseam length as that isn't usually a problem for me being about average height (5' 5 or so). These are like 5 or 6 inches too long. See my pic, my feet are flat. I dont normally wear heels so that is an issue. Next, I think the fabric might almost be too stretchy. Or perhaps just a little too thin. I have quite a bit of a pudge in front of my stomach. Most of my jeans, even the super stretchy skinny jeans, even it out. These don't. And its very obvious. Finally these fit super well around the waist but got a bit too loose around the knees. Not much an issue but there you are. As for a huge plus! There's front pockets!🎉🎉 Im not sure if I'll return yet as they're not bad pants, just not quite right for me which is a shame because they're such a good price.

Edit* I bought the green pair and they are very green, unlike what most reviews said! I was hoping they were more grey green, which a lot of reviewers didn’t like. But I love them. Don’t size down! If you’re plus size, with a fupa, don’t size down. They are borderline too small but I’m going to keep them because I’m steadily losing weight. Love these freaking pants! They fit like a glove and I feel so confident. Being a plus size woman, I have tried on thousands of jeans in my life. I LOVE jeans. I’m very picky and I don’t wear anything else. I almost ordered the olive colored pair because I’ve always wanted some like that, but I have big calves and when I’ve tried similar pants they pool around my knees and accentuate the parts of my body I hate the most. But as soon as I tried these jeans on, the first thing I wanted to do was order the olive pair and another pair of the jeans. So I will be doing so ASAP. I love the button fly, and the fit is like a glove. They’re a little long, and I’m shocked because I’m 5’8” but I like to cuff my pants at the ankles anyway so it’s cool. I don’t really like how white the distress holes are in contrast to the dark denim. I feel like it’s a little too loud. But I still like the jeans so I’m keeping them and ordering as many as I can find in my size. Thanks for being all inclusive with your sizing and appropriate with your prices! That’s a big deal. 🙌🏻🖤 P.S. For reference I’m 298lbs 5’8” and wear a 22 in most jeans. I got the 22 because my waist is very round and I have a THE fupa from hell. These fit perfectly around my middle. So for those of you who have a heavy middle like me, go for your actual size, don’t size down like so many reviews say.

Having packed on the Covid 15, I was scared to get my actual size. I went with a 22, but now wish I had gone for the 20. At 6 foot, 265 pounds, it’s nearly impossible to find jeans long enough. I’ve been looking for the perfect pair of jegging‘s for over a year. These pants are thicker than leggings, but terrific. They don’t lift my bum, but at 55 I don’t care. They are super stretchy, Maybe if I had gone a size smaller, they would hold my stomach in more, but they don’t feel super strong. They are very comfortable. AND LONG ENOUGH! I will be keeping these for my fatty days, and will order another one in size 20, hoping for more tummy support. I love that they are truly high waisted (over my belly button), have four real sized pockets, and come below my ankles. I highly recommend. Don’t judge me on my pics, LOL.

Lucky Brand Women's High Rise Bridgette Skinny Jeans Love these jeans—first Lucky Brand purchase I’m 5’4, 37-28-40. I also ordered the darker pair in a 29W/27L. The dark pair is not an ankle length as these are despite the same “length”. The 29 was too big. The dark color is great though—wish they were ankle length. I think the jeans fit true to size if you’re shaped like me. I sometimes find that in addition to the waist being too big on jeans, the legs are too tight. These were perfect! I think my thighs measure about 23.5in Really love this pair and didn’t have to pull them up after walking around a bit.
reviewer_name_raw: Great for petite and hourglass shape

user_comment: I am 5’9” 130lbs. And usually a size 6-7 is women’s /juniors jeans. I bought the Jeans in a small at first. Those were wayyyy too small like size zero small. So I bought a medium. Even though according to the size chart that’s an 8-10 and I am at max a 7 when I buy jeans. Anyways the length and the flare of the jeans at the bottom was fantastic. But the fit in thighs, waist and hips was too snug for my comfort. Made things bulge that I didn’t know would. They came way too high for me as well. I’m used to hip huggers. But they fit my butt weird and had to really suck in my gut to button them. Not comfortable.

Lee Women's Sculpting Slim Fit Skinny Leg Pull on Jean I'm 5'10, I ordered a size 6-long. The length is great! It's just below my ankle, the inseam is probably around 32. Only issue I have is that I had to order a size 6 because they didn't have size 4-long so they're a little big. I'll deal with it, it's worth it to have pants long enough!

Lee Women's Relaxed Fit All Cotton Straight Leg Jean I bought these for work. I love them for work! I needed 100% cotton jeans and these did the trick. They’re not super flattering but boy are they comfortable! So great for lounging, running errands, they’d have FANTASTIC for gardening, and they are great contract/construction work. I wore them when I visited power plants for work and I will wear them again for gardening and other outdoor adventures when it’s hot out and I need long pants. I love these pants!! I wish they were a little more stylish so I could wear them more often... I mean... I could... but I don’t have that lien or courage... yet. BUT, I will wear these every opportunity I get and maybe I can find a way to make them stylish. I think it’s my body. Some women could definitely make these stylish. I’m a little shorter and more petite. I think for these to be stylish you probably need longer legs. I have to to roll these at the bottom and I look like a little girl. I also got the smallest size they offered and they get a little roomy... they may be made to be that way though. Anyway, I seriously do love these. Comfiest and most breathable jeans that I have ever worn. I want MORE 100% cotton jeans. Please!! They truly are amazing. GET THEM. They may look great on you, just not as much on me. If the way don’t look “cute,” keep them for fun and rugged activities/gardening. P.S. I got a size 4. This was the lowest offered when I bought them. They stay up because of where they sit on my hips though. I’m something like a 27in waist, 34-35inch hips?? Waist size doesn’t matter as much for these... or they didn’t for me... I’m 5’3”

I first reached out to the company on the 6th of January, to see if this could be rectified before I left a review using the email they had on the happy/unhappy card with my purchase. No reply. I bought 3 pairs, all the same style in a size 1. So, the very first pair I received are Amazing! The fit was excellent, the length, love love loved it. Those were the medium blues. Was so stoked to get the other two being delivered the following day. Thought I had finally found “the jeans” you just love to wear. Wow, just completely shocked at the inconsistency in sizing. The dark wash was fitting like a 0, a bit tight on my hips but I could still put them on. The light blue acid ones. Omg. I couldn’t even get them past my hips. These white cropped barrel jeans instantly make getting dressed feel easy and fun again. Just add a tee and sandals and suddenly you look styled, intentional, and ready for sunshine. The fit is excellent with a true high waist, classic 5-pocket design, and a flattering barrel leg that’s wide without being overwhelming. The 28-inch cropped length hits just above my ankle at 5’6”, which is perfect for showing off summer shoes. They’re true to size, but I sized up for a relaxed, lived-in feel and love the slightly looser look. What really makes these special is the subtle design detail. The double-stitched side seams add structure, and the long, arched front seam creates that gentle barrel shape that gives these jeans personality. It’s flattering and quietly stylish. The cotton blend fabric has light stretch and feels sturdy yet comfortable. The cream white color is crisp and bright, and the silver hardware gives them a clean, polished finish. They washed beautifully with no shrinkage and came out looking just as good as new. Overall, these are the kind of jeans you reach for on repeat. Comfortable, flattering, easy to style, and a great value. Already eyeing another color.

These black denim jeggings were a little big on me before washing, which is what I expected ordering a Large when I'm usually a Medium. I am 5'6", a long-torso-ed 140ish pounds and 62 years old. I washed them on hot and dried them fully, and now they fit much better. I prefer clothing I can toss into the washer and dryer, and these are perfect for that. I imagine the Medium runs a tiny bit small, otherwise my normally size 8 body would have had these leggings hanging off her. As for the material, it's thinner than average cotton denim, and stretchy. The material is soft and the proportions are nice.

roswear Wide Leg Jeans Elastic Waist with Drawstring, MEDIUM, BLACK: I’m 5’2”, 174 lbs and normally wear a large or 10/12 in pants. These medium jeans fit very well. The only area of ‘constraint’ is in the lower belly/upper thigh/pooch area… It’s where I hold extra weight. I can feel the tightness when I do certain movements, but it’s not uncomfortable, or even noticeable. These jeans are very long on me, which is my norm. Photos show with and without shoes. I am big fan of the roswear brand and these jeans do not disappoint. I really like the style and find these to be flattering, with the right shoes. Although these have an elastic waist, the jeans themselves are not stretchy, they feel like jeans material but lighter. In my photos the black appears lighter, but it’s truly black. Only downside thus far is that these jeans, or at least the black jeans, do pick up lint very easily. The pockets are full-sized which is amazing! Good value. I’m very happy and would recommend!

Fit: I’m 5'10" and ordered a small — the length is a bit short on me, which I expected, but otherwise the fit is spot-on. The waistband is snug but comfortable, and the long drawstrings make it easy to adjust. Comfort & Warmth: These are exceptionally cozy. The fleece is thick, soft, and warm, perfect for lounging, running errands, or wearing around the house on cold mornings. They keep their shape well and don’t feel flimsy like some budget sweats do. Quality: For the price, these are a steal. I grabbed them on a Lightning Deal for $14, but they easily feel like a pair twice that cost. The material feels durable, stitching is solid, and they’ve held up great after washing. Overall: If you want affordable sweats that feel premium — thick, warm, and comfortable — these are an easy win. I’d definitely buy another pair (maybe a medium next time for extra length).

These are accurately shown and described and the color was true to what's shown. I'm 5'2" 140 lbs and mine fit fine, love the length. There are two buttons on the waist band and I have to remember to button them bottom up because if I button top first I can't get to the second one afterwards, lol.

Levi's Women's Ribcage Straight Full Length Jeans Me and Levi’s have had a love hate relationship for a few years now. I used to fit them fine until I put on some adult weight. Either the waist was gappy but they fit my bum or the waist fit and the jeans would push in my bum and make me look awkward. However, THESE JEANS, are now what I consider the holy grail of Levi’s. The waist isn’t stiff and nips in my waistline ever so slightly. The jeans hug my bum and thighs just as they should and have a nice modern look. I was worried about the length but I am 5’4” and I think the length is perfect. I like when jeans lay slightly on my sneakers/shoes. For reference, I purchased 26x32 and I am between 25-26 waist and 36 hips. (25 curve love in Abercrombie) If you’re considering these, you won’t regret them!

These jeans my pear-shaped body perfectly. They run true to size. I’m 5’4” and needed a 14 short, but they didn’t have one so I ended up with the 14-regular which I will hem up. (Inseam was 32in) I only wish they came in black.

I really love these! Here is what you need to know:The color is isn't a white/white. It is more of a cream, or off/white. The fabric is 98% cotton and 2% Elastane. It feels like thin, lightweight denim with a good amount of stretch. The stitching is the same color of white too, and all the pockets are real.The cut is somewhere between a barrel and a wide leg. They don't have the rigid, well, barrel shape of the higher-end jeans that bear that name, but they do have the extra fabric insert, and are nicely wide legged. This is the easiest kind of "barrel jean" to wear IMHO.Size notes: I am 5'8", proportionally long in the leg, and usually in jeans a 4/6 or 27. I ordered a medium. The fit on me is great. They are loose and slouchy, and very comfy. The crop on me is a little high, above the ankles, but I can make it work. The waist is high but not too high.I am not usually a fan of white jeans, but I may have been converted by these.

Be prepared to size up if you prefer a looser fit. At 5'7" and 155 pounds, I typically wear a size 12. Based on the sizing chart, I ordered a size 14, and they still feel a bit snug. Since the shorts have belt loops, I removed the drawstring because I didn’t like the way it looked. The material has just enough stretch to be comfortable while still maintaining that classic “jean short” aesthetic. My biggest complaint is that the right leg is sewn unevenly and constantly rides up at my inner thigh (see picture), even with minimal movement. Unfortunately, this is a deal breaker for me, and I will most likely never wear them—which is a shame, because they are otherwise flattering.

This review is for the “ Tall Women's 34"/36"/38" Inseam Curvy Bootcut Jeans Mid-Rise Stretchy Denim Pants Classic Bell Bottom Trouser“ These jeans are amazing!.It is a very rare occasion that i am lucky enough to find tall enough jeans that also fit in all other places. These fit me like a second skin with a boot cut leg opening. They stretch in all the right areas and they do not get baggy. They washed well and I dried them on low and hung them up while still damp. I am used to paying over $100 for long enough jeans and there is only a handful of retailers (all online) that sell tall lengths and often they stop at the 34” inseam. The one thing that is strange is the size chart with women’s heights. It does not take into consideration that you can have very long legs and a short torso. According to their chart I should be 6 feet to 6 feet 4 to need a 34” inseam. For reference, I am 5’10” , 157 pounds, I ordered the 14 since i have a tummy and wide hips. My regular length is 34-35”. I had just reviewed a pair of 34” work pants from this seller and they were extremely long, so I got the jeans in 34” and not in 36” and i am glad I did. At $43 these are a good value.

Ok, these look pretty good on. The waist is a bit tight. But I don’t like baggy at my age so I ordered a small. They did shrink in length after the dryer. I wouldn’t order small if you are over 5’7”. For reference I’m 5’6 1/2” and weigh 132.

THE GYM PEOPLE Women's Wide Leg Pull on Jeans are pretty great, but they do have a few drawbacks for me. They are machine wash, which is one of the first things I look for with clothing. They also say the pants can go in the dryer, but I chose to hang dry. The color is a nice, solid black and they did not fade or leave anything on the color catcher I put in the washer. I ordered Large, as suggested, and believe that is the right size for me. These are definitely not jeans. They are yoga pants with jeans style riveted pockets. Me: 5'2" 162 L/XL or 12/14 42DD Now, I'm short, so I'm used to pants being a little long on me. These are A LOT long and should be labeled TALL. Normally, I would just roll them up and carry on, but the super wide legs are flappy and the stretchy yoga pant material won't stay folded. Other than the length, the fit of these pants is comfortable, but the waist feels small, maybe a half size smaller than the pants? The front pockets are pretty decent. I'm not sure what phone they used in the video, but my Samsung 24+ sticks out slightly. Like... just the top edge and corner. The back pockets are technically functional, but they're on the small side. My phone only goes in 2/3 of the way and I wouldn't trust the back pocket to keep it safe and secure. All in all, these pants are super comfy, I just wish they weren’t so long and waist was a little bigger. The waist feels fine, unless I gain an ounce or get bloated for breathing wrong. iykyk

I’ve ordered more jeans than I can count from Amazon and hate to say it, but have returned every single pair… they NEVER fit or look like what is pictured or described. BUT… I have finally found a pair I shockingly love! I’m 5’9, 150 lbs and I always have the hardest time finding jeans that are long enough and fit the way I like. I have to admit I’m honestly surprised at how perfect these jeans fit! The overall fit and length is very accurate (I’m got size 10, 34” inseam in light blue). The material looks like real denim, but feels much softer and more comfortable to wear. The material also has some stretch to it and it’s just enough to help with comfort and doesn’t gradually stretch out through out the day like other denim jeans do! I did end up retuning these because the light blue color was a little different than what is pictured. It’s more like a faded or washed out light blue and that’s not really my style. I did reorder them in the regular blue so hopefully the color will be as pictured because I truly am obsessed with the fit! For all the long legged tall girlies, you won’t be disappointed!

The fit of this tank is not what I expected. I like the length. It hits right below my waist so could easily be worn tucked in or untucked. Unfortunately, I won't be comfortable wearing this shirt without a cardigan or blazer on top given how generous the top is. I wear a size 32DD bra, but the chest on this shirt is still too large and the arm holes gap open quite a bit.The shirt is currently selling for just under $15 which seems like a great price given the quality of the material. If not for being overly large in the chest, I would have ordered more.

Nice shirt. Sleeves are a tiny bit short but not bad. I ordered the apricot in XS. I weigh 130lbs and it fits fine. Still loose but not sloppy. Soft material, nice color. Might order the black too since these are the only two colors available in this size.

Really cute wide leg jeans. I got the red in size 8, based on the sizing chart. The measurements on the chart are more accurate than what you think your size is. I’m a 6 in Banana and Crew but 8 fit me better in this pant. I am 5’5 135# for reference. LOVE the wide leg and unfinished hem, about 30” inseam, I should be able to wear a low or mid heel boot or sandal with these. I do love the red but it has a lot of pink in it and not a true RED red, like the photos might suggest.
Great jeans! Well made! I’m 5 foot 6 and a half inches and weigh 135 pounds. Ordered the size medium. Very comfy and cute!

I was very surprised how well these jeans fit! They are so stretchy and comfy. Waist fits comfortably. I am 5’0 and ordered a 4 (s). They are a bit too long, but with platform shoes they are fine! Love the design. Might get a lighter color as well

I am an avid jeans hater, I don’t wear them because they’re usually uncomfortable. But these jeans are so comfortable I forget I’m even wearing jeans! They fit me perfectly and for reference I weight 210 lbs and I’m 5’4. The quality is excellent as well as the durability. I love the bootcut look, it goes great with boots and sneakers. I think this is a great addition to your closet and worth the affordable price.

Im 5’6 and ordered a size 6 They fit TTS with a bit of stretch! I’m usually in between sizes (6/8 or a M), depending on the amount of stretch the denim has. These fit perfectly! Will definitely be ordering in more colors

Comfortable, great fit. I'm 5'3" so these are a bit longer than shown on the model, but I don't mind that. I need to wear a belt to keep them from sliding down, but otherwise the fit is great.

I know it says “with patch pockets” but like they don’t show a difference in color on the preview pictures so I’m pretty disappointed. I also feel like there’s too much fabric in the crotch area, not a fan. Maybe a size smaller would help that because they are a tad big on me but I trusted reviews that said to size up. I’m 5’2 150lbs & I got a large

Comfy fleece-lined, high-waisted, distressed black jeans. Stretchy, zip and button closure, straight wide leg. The leg hems are folded and have that frayed look. But my daughter didn't care for that and so our seamstress is hemming them up with a cuff. She loves them. They're a great pair of jeans! The soft fleece lining the inside provides warmth, almost like a pair of sweats. Perfect for when the weather starts turning cold and we need to run around doing our errands. You can dress them up with a slouch sweater and boots. Or dress them down with a hoodie and some sneakers. Really, they work for all seasons. The material feels like good quality. It's a cotton and poly blend, very breathable. The price is amazing for a lined, distressed jean. My daughter is wearing a size 16. She is 5'4 and 185 lbs.
reviewer_name_raw: Soft fleece lining, stretchy and comfy.

I would say these pants seem sized pretty accurately, plus there's a little stretch to them, so that makes them more comfortable. The slight stretch to the fabric is also nice for days that I'm a bit more bloated. I like the material of these pants. The material seems pretty decent. No heavy odors from the dye, but any slight odor washes away after the first wash. These pants really didn't smell like much to begin with.I'm just shy of being 5' 3" tall, so these appear longer on me, but they still give me a bit of that cropped/capri look.The back pockets are cute and I like the embroidered design. The front pockets don't seem overly small, like some jeans do.

First I noticed weird smell after opening the bag. It might be the dye? I washed the pants using liquid softener and sheet softener besides detergent right after taking the photos but still it stinks... The pants are soft and comfortable. The color is quite vivid. (Another reason I washed it is I worried about color transfer... The pocket liners are now light blue lol *To be fair, I don't remember if it was its original color) I'm 5' 3" with short legs. Unfortunately these pants are a bit too long for me. Otherwise size is good. I'll need to shorten the pants. The smell really makes me down

These jeans are super comfortable and look good as well. I wasn’t sure about them because they’re unconventional with no front buttons or front zipper, instead they have just a side zipper. So far while wearing them the side zipper stays up well, but I feel like it should have a button or even a small clasp at the top of the zipper to secure it. I got a size 8 and I am 5’5” 150lbs and the size 8 is quite roomy. They do fit well but I like my jeans to be a little more snug. They look cute on and I like the wide leg. They do have two pockets which is a must, so they didn’t change that concept. The jeans have tons of stretch, so I really could have sized down but I am still happy with the ones I got. The quality seems to be good, the seams all line up and there are no stray threads. If you’re looking for wide leg jeans that have a different look these might catch your eye.

I’m only 5’3 and they barely touch the floor. In the picture the models foot is completely covered by the jeans. It’s also not as tight around the knee so it doesn’t give the same vibe as the picture at all. I got a medium and I’m 150. The color is correct. They would be nice if they were longer like the picture.

The pants look accurate to the product photos and arrived in perfect condition. Stretchy jeans tend to have a strong smell. These pants only have a faint smell. The waist does have belt loops. I love the amount of pockets on the pants. There are 6 pockets all together and each one can comfortably fit a phone. The ankles of the pants can be cinched in by pulling the drawstring. I'm 5'5", about 215lbs and have 47in hips. I usually wear a size 16-18 or 2XL in pants, so I ordered these pants in a 2XL. They go past my ankles. They fit me comfortably, but I wish I had actually sized down. I ordered my regular size, but I've actually lost weight recently. According to the tag, the pants are 70% cotton, 28% polyester and 2% spandex. The quality of the material and construction are both great. The material feels comfortable on the skin. They are quite stretchy, so they're very easy to move in. I think they're even comfy enough to sleep in them if you need to. I already washed the jeans with the water temperature set to cold and I didn't have any issues. At the time of me making this review, the pants are selling for about $39 and they're well worth the price.

These are so cute but so poorly made! I am very curvy, with a 32 inch waist, and a 45 inch hip. I typically wear between an 8 and a 12 depending on the brand and I purchased a medium. The fit was spot on and the style is adorable, very vintage pin-up; however, my 8 year old daughter who is just learning to sew could have constructed these better. There is weird, uneven topstitching across the front and tons of long, loose threads. The buttons don’t really lay properly from the side (see photo). And I prob would have kept them if I wasn’t planning to wear them with a bodysuit, since the front button panel is really meant to be seen. Sigh…back they go. 🫤

Got a few pair that were similar to these but they were still not long enough. I am 5'9 and 196 lbs with Super long legs. I received these at the same time as the other ones... sent the other one's back and then bought two more pair of these. I also suggested these two a church member because she's tall like me. I wish there were more colors in these. They fit very well. I ordered an XL. I would suggest these to any tall long legged woman. Thank you so much!

I love these jeans! They were perfect for Valentine’s Day at school. I am 5’ 6” and got the medium. They were a great fit. I wore them with platform sneakers and they were still long enough. The front pockets have little gold buttons (I wasn’t sure I would like this but it ended up looking cute with gold jewelry).

The jeans are really cute, but they are low waisted not high like the pic. Also, like other reviews said, the jeans are super short. I’m 5’6” and they needed to be 2 inches longer. Otherwise super cute. I wish they were high waisted like the pic!! Material is soft and nice and they are flattering in. I debated returning them overnight but I know I won’t wear jeans that are too short añd low. Oh well!! If you are 5’4” añd under you’d be super happy with length. Also some ppl said go size down so I did. I got a medium añd they were pretty snug in the waist. I normally wear a size 12 and I like how they fit in medium. Large probably would have been fine.

These pants surprised me in the best way. They feel like the softest stretchy denim but look like actual trouser jeans. My teenager even thought I bought new jeans which says everything. For sizing reference I am 5 foot 8 and 190 pounds with an hourglass shape and the XL worked great. If someone is more apple shaped I would suggest sizing up because the waistband is the most fitted part. Length was perfect for me which I did not expect at all. There was a slight chemical smell when opening the package but it faded fast. I would absolutely wear these out to run errands because they look like I tried but they feel like yoga pants. The fabric stretches comfortably and does not go sheer. The wide leg is cute. The waistband design is the only part I do not love and I think it would look better with a faux button detail. The pockets being usable is a win. I would like to try these again in the black denim as well.

These pants are stretchy, but the sizing is all wrong and runs small. I originally got these for my daughter who normally wears a Large, but we got a medium since the size chart said it would fit her. It was wrong. They were too tight. I normally wear a medium and they were a little tight on my as well. I am 5'3" about 145 lbs and the pants are a little tight around the hip and thigh area. They are super cute pants and there are a few nice jeans colors available. I went ahead and order and XL for my daughter since she really liked the style (she is 5'7" and about 170 lbs-normally wears a L) and the XL fit pretty well...only a little loose. So I would recommend sizing up if you want a looser fit. The fabric also has a funky smell to them and it still persisted after washing once. They are comfortable and a nice style, but I will probably try to find a similar style with a different vendor.

I’m 5’9, 155 lbs. I normally wear a size 6 or 8. The waist is very tight. Also shorter than I would like. I got a size medium, I wish I would have gotten a large. I have very narrow hips , but not a lot of give in that area either. They seem like very good quality. Also I am writing this review, because I couldn’t find another review with the same body type as me. Your welcome.

These jeans are SUPER soft and stretchy. The material is light, fairly thin but not in a negative way - the quality of the fabric still seems decent. Overall they are just a very smooth, soft, light pair of denim (or faux-denim?). They feel more like wearing a pair of stretchy leggings than they do a pair of jeans, but they wear more like jeans given the bootcut design and real pockets. The color is ok, I would prefer just a touch darker or more variations in the color so it doesn't look like such a uniform bright blue - but this is a style preference and I'm sure others would be fine with this color.I am not thrilled with the fit of these, however. They have a high waist which hits me in an odd place and is a little loose (though the fit of the legs is a bit snug, so I wouldn't want to down-size). This means the waistband slides down naturally, which actually makes it sit in a more comfortable place above my hips; but then also makes them sag at the crotch-seam in an unflattering way. The other issue is when they are pulled all the way up as intended, they get slightly short on me, hitting right at the mid-ankle; when they are sagging down, the length is perfect, right at the center of my heel. So ultimately I wish these were cut with a slightly lower rise and I think they would have been perfect. For reference I am 5'9" around 160lb, and got a Large which is my typical size for pants.

This are super cute and flattering, considering I’m only 5’2” and 130. I didn’t think I could wear wide legged jeans or high waisted. The only issue was that the bottom of the left front pocket was stitched to the waistband. I had to cut it so now there’s a hole in the bottom of the pocket but not a big deal.

I am 5'7" and 178 lbs. These jeans fit me perfectly, and I think they are slimming. They are sooooo comfortable, even when sitting down and hunched over looking down at my phone. I stopped wearing jeans for over a decade because the waist band would dig in painfully in that position. These jeans solve that! I'm very happy! They are thin enough for summer and super stretchy. The seam quality is good. The pockets are big enough to hold something, but I just use a purse or backpack. Sorry about the fuzzy mirror in the photos. Lol

A bit mad I so hastily took off the tag on these, but my main issue is that they keep sliding down! I’m 25 weeks, 5’6, 190 lbs pre-pregnancy and usually a size 10-12 with 41-42 inch hip, so I ordered a large. Having worn under-the-bump jeans and finding that they were hitting in an uncomfortable spot, I wanted a pair of pants that go over the bump with front pockets so I can hook my work badge. Maternity pants with front pockets appear to be a rarity. These pants keep slipping down! The legs feel fine, although they run long so I need to roll/cuff them, but the waist is not staying in place. I feel like the bottom of the panel is constantly falling down to my crotch instead of staying above it. There’s no belt loops either, so it’s awkward to try to “hike up” the pants constantly. I’m not sure if sizing down would work, given that the legs are perfectly fine for me in the large, but these pants were not the 10/10 I was hoping for. Maybe they’re best for mid to late third trimester. Included a pic when they are first hiked up and another once I’ve been walking around to show the slump!

I am a 5'8" 150 lb female and ordered the size 10. Fit is true to size. I love the color and quality of these jeans. While I don't normally look great in natural waist jeans due to my diastasis recti, and while I'm still getting used to this new style of jean, these jeans looked better than I expected. The quality is really great. The interior is a softer texture and will be warmer for winter which is nice. They are cotton, poly and elastane. The pictures are after I washed them and I didn't notice any significant shrinkage.

These are very comfortable stretchy jeans. They hug your torso and don't leave that annoying gap between your lower back and bottom that sometimes jeans can do. I am 5"2 and 150 lbs. The length is PERFECT and they fit me like a glove in the Petite Large. I took one star away because the stitching along the front waistline could be better. It's bumpy and uneven. Other than that, they are nice and warm without feeling unbreathable. They'll be for around the house when I don't want tight leggings or skinny jeans but still want to feel put together and comfortable.

Fit Reference: 5’2”, ~123 lbs, slim build, usually wear size Small Use: Casual outfits, styled everyday wear I really wanted to love these wide-leg jeans because I like the overall style and the black color is very versatile. The paper bag waist and patch pocket detail give them a trendy, modern look. However, at 5’2”, this cut just wasn’t the most flattering on my frame. The cropped wide-leg style combined with the high waist felt a bit overwhelming on my height, and the proportions didn’t quite work for me personally. The denim itself has some stretch and feels comfortable, and the quality seems good overall. I think these would look great on someone taller or with a different body proportion. While they weren’t the best fit for me, the design and construction are nice. Best For: Someone taller or who loves a bold wide-leg silhouette and structured paper bag waist style.

user_comment: I am 5’0, 122 lbs, athletic build. I purchased an XS in the jeans. I am a true size 25 in Abercrombie jeans and a 24 in their midrise barrel Jean (which I have and love also). I actually really love how these fit! I don’t have to have them cut off or altered in the length which is huge for me. They are high waisted and hit right at the belly button on me. I like the quality of denim for the price and the size of the back pockets are spot on. The barrel shape is subtle, less obvious than the abercrombie barrel jeans which are the only other barrel jeans I have to compare them to. The button closure is weird, I wish it was a simple silver or normal color. It’s white and says JEANS around the circle if that makes sense. I feel weird tucking things into them because of the button looking cheap/tacky so I might color it with a sharpie or something so it’s less noticeable. I will say, the jeans smell horrible like chemicals. I’ve washed and dried them and still smell it when just wearing them. I’m hoping that will wear off eventually. I may let them air outside for the day. Overall, really happy with the fit and style. I think the smell and the button closure are my only complaints!

I bought these at Kohls, but was looking on here to see if I can find more. I love them. I have a mom pouch and these help to smooth me out! I feel so confident in them! I am 5'5 and weight 165 and bought a size 12. I would love another pair when the budget allows!

Cute jeans that fit well. I really like the front patch pockets and the wide legs. The color is good, too. They aren't as light as the listing photo, and I'm happy with that. My only complaint is that the only way in and out of these pants is with a zipper in the side seam. There is no button or hook and eye at the top of the zipper, so it's necessary to hold the fabric together at the top of the zipper with one hand while zipping up with the other. The zipper catches where the pocket seam is close to the top, and it's difficult to zip it past that point. I also had to trim some loose threads to keep them from getting caught in the zipper. All the rest of the stitching and seams are great. The fabric weight is good and hangs well. I ordered a size smaller than I had planned because it was the only size available. If I'd ordered a size larger, it might be easier to zip, but the waist would have been too large as I have a tiny bit of a waist gap in the smaller size. The waist of these pants truly is tiny. That's great for curvy girls like me, but if you're more of an apple shape, definitely order based on your waist size. One other mention. The size chart lists numeric sizes, but the product I received is sized as a small instead of the size 4 I ordered. I'm 5'7", 145 pounds, and my measurements are 36-26-38. I ordered a size 4 which is labeled as a small.

user_comment: This pair of cropped, paper bag waist jeans are well made and fun. The fabric is 73% cotton, 25.4% polyester, and 1.6% elastane. They are closer to a medium thickness than a thin one, in my opinion. They are soft and have enough stretch that they are comfy to wear. There are 4 patch pockets that are functional, but a little small. Personally, I think the smaller pockets look nicer when doing patch pockets on jeans. The button and zipper work smoothly without any snags or being overly stiff. The Washed Black color looks good and does not arrive with an overpowering odor from the dye. They have no bad odor to them at all! The paper bag waist style is something I have not tried before. It does feel different, and at the same time it's very comfortable. I was trying them on and noticed the button was lower down from the top of the jeans than any others I have. It does make for a different feeling around my waist with that looser "bag-like" part above the elastic and button area. Not in a bad way. They're less constricting and more comfortable, actually. I ordered these jeans in a size XL, which is my regular size. The waist is snug, but comfortable without any pinching or binding. The length is almost a normal length on me due to my shortness. For reference: I'm 5' 4" tall, 33-ish waist, 44" hips and 182 lbs. These are cute, nice quality cropped jeans and I will recommend them. There are several shades of blue along with the washed black and cream white to choose from. The current price (1-25-2026) of $36.99 is a bit expensive for cropped jeans, in my opinion. However, they are offering a coupon at this time that brings the price down to make them a good value.

I'm 5'2" and weigh 138 lbs. My waist is 34" and my hips and thighs are very wide (45" at the widest point). I purchased a size large. I like the way they fit nicely on my waist (not too tight without the weird gap in the back), but they are very tight on my hips, which makes me worry that they will ware or tear in no time. The length is a tad long on my short stature, but manageable. Another reviewer mentioned that these are not meant for pear-shaped ladies, I would agree. I purchased these to try something other than skinny jeans for once. After testing about a half a dozen pairs of jeans, these were the most flattering. I plan to keep these and see how things go.

Wish they came in white! The metal buttons and zippers on regular jeans irritate my surgery scar tissue on my tummy. These pull on jeans solve this problem quite well! Can’t afford the Spanx jeans, and these are a wonderful dupe! I’m 5’4”, 155lbs and 32” waist. I’m wearing size 10.

I followed the advice of others regarding size: My normal jeans size is 2 Reg and I ordered 4 Petite. Other than a little gapping at the back waist they fit well. The length is good as long as they don't shrink too much. I am 5'2" and 105 lbs. (Despite being short I don't normally order petite pants) I usually get curvy jeans which these are not, hence the gap at the back waist - it's not too bad though so I'm keeping them. The waist is at my belly button, I would have liked a little higher since I'm long waisted but they are acceptable, however they are not true high waisted jeans. My feeling is, if you're going to wear pull on jeans they should be high waisted to avoid muffin top. I'm 60+ yrs old and they look decent on me, at my age that's the best one can hope for. I wish they were more curvy, otherwise a good fit.

Levi's Women's Ribcage Straight Full Length Jeans The fabric is of excellent quality, just right on point with a slight stretch. It's not stiff, nor is it super soft. It allows the garment to maintain its structure while being very comfortable at the same time. I ordered size 28 (inseam is 32). It's a little long for my height, but it will work well with heels or sneakers with a thicker sole too. It also looks great if you roll up the hem. There's no gap at the waist, which is very difficult to find in other models. For reference, my measurements are: Waist 26.5 Hips 37 - 37 1/2 Height 5’3”

My info: I am 5'9", 215 lbs, long legs, short torso, large tummy, normally a size 20 OR 2X. I had originally ordered the 20 Plus Long but they were baggy on me under the tummy and lower butt areas and the waist & legs areas were really loose, so I return those and ordered the 18 Plus Long. They are still a little baggy under the areas I mentioned above, which I would have to wear a long tunic to cover these areas. The waist & legs are more fitted but the length is PERFECT!! These are a true LONG as they reach exactly even with the bottom of my ankles. The fabric, IMHO, can be worn in ALL seasons; it's thick but not heavy thick, similar to jeans. The only negatives I have are, I wish there wasn't a zipper & buttons AND I wish they had belt loops. So in regards to belt loops, I will take these pants to a fabric store to match or closely match these fabrics so that I can sew belt loops on these pants.Edited to say: As you can see, these do not lift the butt!

Really comfortable and flattering jeans that are close to being perfect- especially at this price. The jeans are high waisted but very stretchy, which make them comfortable and worry free to wear (no adjusting after bending over needed!). They’re also flattering, slimming, and the length is perfect for me (I’m 5’3) to wear with both heels and flats. The only issues that prevent these from being five stars are the sizing and the closure. The sizing chart and ordering options are numerical, yet the jeans are letter sized. I ordered a 4 and was sent a Small, which worked out because they fit, but I think the manufacturer should update that info. Secondly, the closure might be an issue in the future. The jeans have an only have a side zipper to keep them together (no hook to secure them). While they stay up now, I can see the jeans stretching over time and that zipper eventually not being enough…easily solved with the addition of a cheap hook and clasp.

I just got these Tuesday low rise. I’m a short, curvy woman. 5’1” and 129lbs. These jeans are 27W x 31L. Out of the package they fit great. After one wash, even better. They are so confortable. Look closely at the denim and color options. Some denim is more distressed than others, some with intentional holes. I think they are great value for the money as I have an original pair from 10+ years ago.

These are super cute jeans! They are definitely wide legged and baggie. The pockets in the from and back are both nice and big, I can fit my whole hand in there and I have big hands! The white cord does help hold the pants up as it is an elastic waist. The cord loops are not going to be big enough to replace the cord with a belt, which is a bit of a bummer but not a deal breaker. I’m 5’6” 185 and can wear a 12 or 14. I sized up to a 14 and they are a bit loose so I’d say they run true to size.

i ordered this at night & it arrived on my porch the next morning! in fact i’m writing this review 20 mins after receiving bc i love them so much. i had actually just sworn off buying jeans off of amazon bc they’ve all been a thin jegging material with poor, cheap quality that i end up returning. i gravitated to these bc they remind me of circus print 🎪 & i have a love for unique clothes. i adore the sailor pockets as that’s something ive also been shopping for! i also bought these knowing that it will need extra care washing so it doesn’t bleed as i also have other red & white clothing or vintage that i have to hand wash , so if you're not the kind to want to deal with the tediousness of that i probably wouldn’t buy! i am a size 6 or 28 and went ahead and got a 8 which i’m glad i did bc it gives a relaxed fit on the legs if you have thicker thighs! the zipper and button are surprisingly sturdy and the jeans itself are light but not thin, i was pleasantly surprised with this purchase! i’m 5’2 and it’s at my ankles.

Y’all I’m not gonna lie that picture they have online is not it and I was extremely reluctant to buy it based off the stock photo however I was pleasantly surprised and I absolutely love these pants. They are now in my top three bottoms that I love to wear. The stretch is amazing considering I have a big bottom and a smaller ways for reference. I am 52 and 160 pounds. The medium works well and the drawstring is acute functional edition, even when it gets warm, I’m still going to find opportunities to wear it with maybe a crop top or a tube top. You do need to know that for my height, which is 5’2, these pants are the perfect life so if you are taller than 5’2 or 5’3, I would not purchase these they are not tall friendly. Whenever I wear them, I put them with flats or tennis shoes and they are the perfect length.

I’m at a loss for words. For years and years I’ve spent thousands and thousands on “tall” girl jeans. They’ve all only been “okay”. I mean I’ve even had pairs I’ve convinced myself were great. But these are in a different world. I’m between 5’11-6’- and both the 36 inseam and 38 inseam are amazing. The 36 is the length that covers almost the bottom of your shoes. The 38 is nearly touching the floor, but not in a flare or “skater girl” way. I have yet to wash a pair but I’d probably stick to the 38 JUST IN case, but honestly both are beautifully long. The best part- the FIT. The stretch fit is amazing. They don’t have that “stretch” jean look (like where it is overly fitting and looks like skin). They look like expensive high quality jeans, like QUALITY jeans. They’re not too heavy or light, they feel durable- you know they’re jeans you’ll wear for years and years. I’ve spent thousands over the years, between Nordstrom, fashion nova, American tall, buckle and more. I’m simply blown away. In my photos, the darker blue jeans are the 36 inseam. They’re light wash are the 38. Not to be dramatic, but these pants have changed my life.

These pants are almost perfect. The only issue I have with them is length. The pants feel like real denim, they are thick and not see-through. They are very comfortable and roomy, with a wide leg. Style is nice, with a tailored but casual look. The waistband is elastic with a drawstring. Had I not washed them before I wrote this review, I would’ve told you they were perfect. I washed them on cool, threw them in the dryer for just a few minutes, and then hung to dry. Unfortunately they shrunk up just a little on the length. I am 5’7.5”. Prior to washing them, they just met the floor. Now, they are about two fingers off the floor. I will continue to wear them, but will only be able to wear with flats. So if you are any taller than me, I probably would not recommend, but if you’re shorter they will probably work out perfect.

Wrangler Authentics womens Stretch Denim Jacket Purchased the jacket originally in a size medium, but decided to go for something more loose so I can wear it over almost anything and returned it for a size large. What I especially like is the flattering fit (the darts in the backside help create this look) unlike most boxy-style jackets. I'm looking forward to wearing this with dresses, jeans and shorts. Waist is loose and big through my butt and hips. Def need to try the XXS but looks like they are sold out.
