I am not usually successful in buying jeans online but these are a happy surprise. I followed the size chart and ordered size 8. I’m 5’7”, 135pounds and my chunkier parts are my thighs. There is a great amount of stretch in these, you can bend, squat easily. I like the waist height , it holds in any pooch. The length is just to my ankle, I think I’d wear a chunky heel boot in cooler weather with these. The 65% cotton is soft and thick….I LOVE a thick fabric, so many things are made with cheap, thin cloth. I highly recommend if you want an updated Jean for cooler weather.

I am surprised by how nice these baggy jeans are! I am trying to be more fashionable and on trend with the baggy jean trend and I gave these a try. They are perfect! They are baggy but too baggy where you are swimming in them. I LOVE the elastic waist that allows for easy on and off and doesn't feel constricting on the stomach. The drawstring works to synch the waist even further if you need it. The weight of the denim is thick without being too thick and thus allows for the perfect draping of the jeans. Lastly, the length is also perfect and fell over my sneakers and even low enough to wear with heels. I am 5'6 and it hit the floor to where I would want the length of my jeans to me. The only con I would say is that I typically wear a size Medium and that is what I purchased, but it felt a little big on me. Also because it was a little big on me, the back pockets looked lower on me than it should. I wore them right out of the package and have yet to wash them so maybe it'll shrink a little in the wash but don't know yet. Still, would recommend but perhaps size down.

Loved these jeans. Couldn’t believe they weren’t too long for my 4’11” height (with wedges). I’m 112-115 and these jeans were just too big overall. Very stretchy! I was able to pull these on like sweatpants and while I absolutely love the style, length and color, they were just too big. If they had a XS, I’d buy them in every color! They went back.

So i was really looking forward to these jeans, the price was perfect and some of the reviews gave me hope. These jeans will be amazing for someone with a different body shape than me!! I wear a size 20 in torrid Jeans and even those are slightly too loose for my liking (ive been losing weight) I wear a size 20 in most jeans from ross aswell. So i bought a 20. I am 295lbs im 5'7 and i have an apron belly (Thank you to my 3 wonderful children LOL) I was looking for high waisted jeans that could hold IN my belly and thought these would be amazing... but as you can see in the photos it didnt work out that way. Yes the simple solution is to order a bigger size.... but i still dont think doing that will accomplish what i am looking for. Other than the top band being too tight, the pants fit great the legs are super comfy and the length is great and as you can see the apron belly is busting out the sides where the pockets are. I am going to return these and buy a bigger size and see if there is a difference, but i wanted to show you my version with my body shape in hopes it might help someone else. **Please excuse the dirty Mirror i was in the kids bathroom.** :)

These are nice and thick sweatpants that feel like good quality! Super impressed. They are a little bit high waisted which isn't my thing but I know it is for others! The length is nice (5'3 for reference) whether they're rolled or not rolled, I have them rolled because I don't like the high waisted which isn't.

I chose the Size: 16 in Color: Bluing but I probably should have gone with the 14 but went up for a looser fit. For reference I'm over 50 years old. Height 5'6", weight 170lbs measurements Bust: 43 Waist: 34 Hips: 43. These are a nice weight, stretchy denim that has a comfortable feel to wear. The cotton content says 65% so I'm hoping for some shrinking over time. But so far it's been through the washer and dryer with no shrinking. It also didn't bleed or pick up excessive lint. The general construction seems fine. The raw edge does have stitching so it wont fray beyond where it currently is. They didn't attach the cuff all the way up though. Just a couple spots overlaying the seams. So as you can see in one of my photos, I'll need to add some more stitches to keep it in place. Also what's up with the big ol faux leather patch sewn on the waist band. Thankfully that's easy enough to remove and it's unnecessary. It's not like they put a cute branding or anything on it. The main warning though is the back. Granted, I've got some junk in my trunk and these probably were not made for much curves back there. That's to say it gaps at the waistline, and if you bend over you'll look ready to do some plumbing. I took a pic in a light squat and you can tell we're getting close to the danger zone. At this age, aint nobody wanna be seeing that! ha! So it definitely needs a belt if not to hold them up, but to hold them in place. Otherwise they're comfortable and I'm sure I'll get plenty of wear from them. Peace

I bought this sweater in green to wear for Friendsgiving and holiday events this fall. I’m 5'4" and 125 pounds, ordered a small, and the fit is spot on. It’s not too tight or baggy, it's literally the perfect fit. The length is perfect for wearing as-is or doing a little tuck or twist. The material feels soft like a sweater but has the lighter weight of a shirt, which makes it great for warmer climates or indoor gatherings. It isn’t see-through, and the v-neck is just right, modest but still low enough to be flattering for an athletic build. (I personally don't like the v-necks with a higher cut.) The color is a bit more muted in person, leaning toward an earthy green rather than a vibrant one, but it’s still pretty and easy to wear with so many things. Pros: • Perfect fit, flattering on an athletic frame • Super soft and lightweight without being thin or sheer • V-neck is modest but flattering • Comfortable for warmer fall weather or indoor events • Versatile length that can be worn out or tucked Cons: • The color is more muted than expected, so it’s great if you like neutral tones Verdict: This sweater is a great staple piece that would look good on most body types. I like it so much I’m already thinking about ordering it in another color. And I scour reviews before buying things, so I hope I can pay it forward and help someone else!!

I got a small and I’m 5’6” and 130 lbs. I wish they were a few inches longer but I got them on flash sale for like $20. Thick and cozy material. Worth it if u get them on sale regardless though.

user_comment: I was skeptical to order these online, because I really wasn’t sure how they would fit. They arrived this morning and I tried them right on. They are so comfortable. 5’7” 115lbs size XS Love these and had tons of compliments. Only complaint is that I ordered khaki and they are much darker than the photos.

Lucky Brand Women's High Rise Bridgette Skinny Jeans I’m 5’3” 147lbs. I bought a size 31/12 regular. They fit prefect. I normally wear a size 10 or 12 in jeans. I’m happy I went for the 12. They also fit great in the length which I normally don’t even worry about since most jeans are too long for me. They are very well made and very comfortable. For $30 I might buy another pair.

I like these jeans, overall they are super comfortable and fit me well - though you should be aware they are a pretty baggy fit. I am 5'7 and 135lbs and I got the size medium. Another interesting thing to note about them is that they have what i consider to be a somewhat odd looking distressed pattern across the pelvic area in the front which, doesn't really bother me but, it's a somewhat questionable styling choice the way they did it, you can see it in my pictures. Pricing is on the higher end in my opinion at $36 dollars, and unfortunately I do not really feel like the quality of construction is quite up to that price point. That said, these jeans are extremely comfortable, they're a quite soft fabric and they're made baggy enough to just be easy to wear even if you're quite curvy which I am. You may want to be careful what you wash them with the first time through the washing machine as they do seem to leech out a bit of blue dye. In regards to pockets, the pockets here in the front are just about big enough to fully enclose a typical smart phone though depending on the phone your mileage may vary here, the back pockets are quite small, but at least they are actually functional pockets. There is a bit of stretch to the waist, but not too much I'd say just a few inches maximum.

Metietila Women’s Casual Pull-on Distressed Stretch Jeans Elastic Waist Jean Denim Joggers Pants The fabric is stretchy but has a good weight. They don't start tight and end up too loose at the end of the day which I love. Real pockets, cute wash, not too short- they go just below the ankle (I'm 5'6"). I feel like these cover the mom pooch pretty well. I got a large and I like the amount of room they have. Overall better than I expected.

A slim cut, comfortable denim capri. It sits nice on waist and stretch helps it move with the body. The leg opening could be a bit bigger for my calves but overall it fits well. Washed up well with a cold machine wash and low tumble dry. For reference - I am 5’11”, 175lbs and ordered a size 12.

I’m 5’8 and 125 pounds. I ordered the size 3 long and included 3 angles in my pics. These are so flattering. If you’re close to my weight and height, get the size 3 long girl. If you’re over 130 pounds, I would say size up cause these fit me JUST right at 125. There’s enough scrunch at the bottom of the pants too so someone who’s even taller than me would love them.

These capris jeans are very cute overall, and I will absolutely wear them. The weight of the jeans is perfect - not too thick and not too thin. There's plenty of stretch as well, which is great because I won't wear any jeans without it. I am 5'6" and the length is truly a capris length...so these actually hit slightly above my ankles and cannot be mistaken for regular length jeans. The leg shape is very flattering - they fit nicely in the thighs and start to bell out as you get toward the calves/bottoms. I do have a few constructive notes: 1) I ordered classic blue thinking these are regular old denim color...they are much darker. I still like them but wish they were the medium/light blue color I thought; 2) unless you are tiny waisted like the model these jeans will be comfortable at the waistband for you, but won't look quite as pictured; 3) wish the front pockets were normal pockets or a bit bigger. Overall - these look super cute with a fitted'ish top that hangs slightly over the waist/hip area and some wedges/sandals.

I really love the look and feel of these jeans. The material is well made and a good quality, and there’s a decent amount of stretch to them so they’re not too stiff. The sizing is great, so I’d recommend ordering your usual size. I’m 5’4” and 135lbs and ordered a size 6, and they fit great. The style of these jeans is really flattering, and even my high schooler thinks I look good in them, which is saying something, lol.

I’ve seen the trend of barrel jeans/pants and thought to my old millennial self that that trend would never be something I’d put in my wardrobe. But I saw these animal print barrel pants on someone at a theme park and thought they looked really cute on the older woman wearing them and so I figured I’d find me a pair and give it a shot. I’m 5’7” and weigh about 172lbs. I ordered a large in “brown” as I wasn’t sure what the other colors would look like. These pants fit great and honestly are a subtle barrel cut so they aren’t so in your face like some styles I’ve seen. I highly recommend these if you’re looking to try out the barrel jeans and want something subtle but still trendy!

At first i bought a size 14 based on the product size chart, when my normal size is a 12, some places a 10.... Go with your usual size!! They at first sent me a size 16 and i tried them on and they were so big i returned and went with a 12. They fit adequately. But it's a weird fit. They are big in the waist and butt, tight in the thighs and knees and a little too short. it's just not a comfortable fit. I will keep them because they have been washed and are ok as a backup pair but not the great pair of jeans they are marketed as. the material is very thin and stretchy in all the wrong places. I am not real happy with this purchase but not so upset that I won't wear them once and try them. For reference i am 5'8" and 190 lbs. usually 33-34 inch waist. 32 inch inseam. I would try a different pair of jeans if you're looking for a thick or structured pair of jeans that don't hug the wrong places. Actually i have been wearing them about an hour and i think i will have to change!!

I almost exclusively wear Levi's jeans because they fit everytime. The brand of these pants had such great reviews that I figured I'd try them out. I struggle with jeans fitting me here, except Levi's. So, I'd recommend these because I do think they're nice, they just dont fit me.

These jeans are super cute. I love the design of the pocket. They are a perfect pair of medium wash wide leg jeans. The material is a thinner jean &super stretchy. I have 5’2 and they hit the floor, so I do have to wear slightly elevated shoes.

Listen!! Ruuun don’t walk 😫 these are such great jeans. Slight stretch fits great round the tummy! I’m 5’2 it just hits the floor looks great in flats or heels easy to dress up for an elevated look!!!! Color literally perfect!!! Bought a size M

user_comment: Levi's Women's Ribcage Straight Full Length Jeans Cute jeans, definitely run small compared to other brands. They’re shorter than I’d like. For reference: I’m 5’10” and 160-165 lbs, athletic/fit build. I normally wear a size 8, or 28/29 waist. I ordered a size 30 and they are snug and have little give. Don’t stretch out too much, thick material. I have the wedgie jeans as well by Levi’s and I think I like that fit better overall than these but I am keeping them.

These jeans are so cute! They run a little small; I'm a 4 in most pants and got a 4 in these, which is a tiny bit snug in the waist. I guess sizing up would be wise if you want a looser fit. They're long on me; I'm 5' and these are the perfect length with wedges but too long for flats. The rope belt is cute and summery, but it's not attached so you can remove it if you want. There are functional belt loops and pockets. Even though the fabric is thick and not super stretchy, the design is flattering. The worst thing about them was the horrific chemical smell when they were opened--I've never smelled anything like it on clothing. The fabric is listed a a cotton/synthetic blend, which is pretty common, so maybe it was a finish used in the factory. They smelled fine after a machine wash and dry though, so it wasn't a big deal for me. Overall, these jeans are adorable for the price, run nearly true to size, and have a fun unique look that I love.

Lee Women's Relaxed Fit All Cotton Straight Leg Jean I spent about 40 min reading everyone’s reviews and after buying the jeans and the size Amazon and the size chart recommended here it is. These jeans are a “mom” style jean. Is small at the waist flares out in the hips then tapers back in at the ankles. In theory this should work for curvy women TTS but the waist size becomes a concern for some. This is not a common body type. Thats why you’ll need to size up to find a good fit. I only found about 2-3 reviews of girls in my size (2-4) and they stated true to size or ran big and this is absolutely the case for me. I have a 34 hip and 28 waist and the four was big but doable to wear. This will stretch out when you wear them so eventually I’d need to keep pulling these up. I have a very straight frame and that’s why I can size down or go with TTS. I wish they had a 2. The material is thinner cotton but cotton none the less. It will not be a warm Jean. I was expecting a thick cotton/denim like the older 2000s styles but you cannot come by those new anymore. Overall this Jean is a good price for the material but I’m not blown away with the fit. This doesn’t compliment my body type. If I had a small waist and bigger hips then I’d buy this style forever. I’m still deciding on keeping for a chore type jean but do I really need them?

These Eytino Plus Size Wide Leg Jeans are all the rage! Oh my stars are these pants comfortable. They have a stretch to them, but they still shape and support, and don't feel like leggings. They are weighted, so they didn't rise up while walking, they are a really nice quality material. I wear a sizd 2x and these fit perfectly around the hips and thighs. They did come up the leg a little too high, but then again, I am 5'11 and most of my jeans are too short. Excellent jeans, they just need to come in talls.

I have a 32-in inseam and m 5 ft 8 in tall making it rather difficult for me to find pants that fit my legs. I'm pretty thrilled by these . I actually like wearing my pants slightly longer so I ended up ordering a 34-in inseam size 14 . I typically wear a size 14 to 15 in pants and these fit perfectly even after washing and warm water and drying on warm . Naturally they fit a bit long on me because they're 2 inches longer than my inseam, but I like them this way.Overall, the quality is equal to any brand name you might find in department stores. The seams are even and finished with minimal hanging threads. The fabric is a softer more stretchy style cotton blend that offers a little bit as tummy support without them being uncomfortable even when you sit or crouch. I love the fact that these give and allow me to move around without restriction. I was surprised that the pockets are actually fairly deep. These are more straight-legged rather than boot cut, but do offer plenty of room around the ankle. These washed well and don't appear to have shrunk.These are distressed and I had to laugh when I found a small piece of pumice stone (what they used to distress the pants with) in my pocket from factory. I don't mind where they've distressed the pants at. Another customer mentioned that it looked like you'd peed your pants and I'm not quite sure where they came up with that. I work outside a lot with my line of work and the distress areas are pretty darn close to where I normally wear down my pants.I honestly have to say this is one of my favorite pairs of jeans now and that says a lot, because as a fat girl with long legs pants are hard to find for me. At the present price of $46.99, I feel like these are within fair market value considering the comfort and good quality.

YES!! Size 3 Fits my 39” butt!! I usually wear a size 3 junior length and stomach about 27”waist size,30 belt.. but sometimes feel like my butt is just smashed in.. these DONT Smash they smooth right on over! Yay ! Cute pockets too.. not a”mom” Jean. One star away for the stitching could possibly come out after awhile seems like maybe…

I'm giving these jeans 5 stars because I think they look like they're supposed to look...I'm just not certain I like how I look in them. LOL! I do like the feel of the denim - it's a nice light/medium weight, soft, and has a good stretch to it. The waist fits my hourglass figure well. The jeans seem to be well made, with nice seams, etc. I like the Classic Blue color that I ordered. I'm just not sure about the shape...I'm 55 years old. Can I pull this off? I'm thinking maybe not. I haven't left the house in them yet. Maybe there are some trends that have an age limit? I feel a little like a bow-legged cowboy in them. They are more attractive from the side, in my opinion. That said, I think these are well made. If you like the look, you might like these jeans!

Wrangler Authentics womens Stretch Denim Jacket Love this jacket! I have a really hard time finding jean jackets to fit in the arm length. I have long arms. This one fits perfectly. I'm 5'4" 145 lbs. purchased a medium

Levi's Women's Ribcage Straight Full Length Jeans These are a 5-star win for me! For reference, I’m 5’4” and 118 lbs, and these fit my body type perfectly. I struggle to find jeans that are flattering without feeling stiff or awkward through the waist and hips, but these hit in all the right places. The high rise is super flattering and really defines my waist, and the straight-leg cut makes them easy to dress up or down. The denim feels structured but still comfortable enough to wear all day - Not rigid like some vintage-style jeans can be. I’ve always had great luck with Levi's, and these Ribcage Straight Full Length Jeans are easily my favorite pair I own. If you’re looking for a classic, flattering fit that actually feels good on, I can’t recommend these enough! 👖✨
Decent quality. They fall down constantly, I wish there were belt loops. They are short for me too im 5'9.

I ordered these because I really enjoy my gym people leggings. Overall, the jeans are very comfortable and they are well-made. They're stretchy and provide full movement with only a minimal restriction around the waist. I ordered a medium according to the size chart and they fit everywhere loosely other than the waist. It did cut into me a bit at the waist because of the elastic stitching but that was easy to hide with a T-shirt. My overall complaint would be the length. I am 5'5" and the pants were about 2 inches too long. Even after washing them I had to pull them up regularly so that they weren't dragging at the heels. If wearing these with heels or a higher shoe, this would not be an issue but I live my life in Converse and flip-flops. Color was exactly like the photo and they did not fade with the wash. Washed and dried with no issues and only slight shrinking

I really like these jeans but was hit with a strong chemical smell upon opening the package. In all fairness I do suffer from migraines with strong odors being one of my main triggers. I had to run them through the wash before I could even try them on. The good news is they washed up really well. Turn inside out to wash and tumble dry on low. The material feels really nice on your skin and they seem to be very well made. No turned up edges or crooked seams. I'm about 5'4" and the length is perfect for about a 2-3" heel or platform shoe. Sizing is accurate as I chose my usual size and they fit well. See pics. Overall very happy with these jeans but deducting 1 star for the initial odor. Certainly not a deal breaker for me but maybe for some.
reviewer_name_raw: Nice Material. Accurate Sizing. Washer Friendly.

user_comment: NYDJ Women's Marilyn Straight Denim Jeans I'm a high rise convert, these are perfection! I'm small but have the muscle tone of Wonder bread plus I'm a 62 Y O grandmother. But in these jeans? I don't look it. I'm 5 7 @ 127 lbs - 29" waist and 39" hips and I bought the size 4 with 32" inseam which is More than long enough. I sew so non issue. My problem area is heinous muffin top if jeans not "just right" and many times I wear a Spanx cami to compensate for this. Not comfy in the south east summers lol. No Spanx needed with these. I'd always thought if I avoid my waist all together I'd avoid muffin top hence low waist line pants, I was wrong! These fit excellent in crotch which can also be another issue with me. The dreaded CT lol. heavy enough fabric that these are not see through, wearing black under things in this picture. Levi's Women's Low Loose For ref I’m 5’1 110lbs & got 25 & they fit perfect. Little long so I will have hemmed but exactly what I was looking for! I could do a 24 but I think you wouldn’t get that “that” look. Love the wash!!

With the Vine program, sometimes you can only order certain colors & sizes. This was the case for these pants. I could only order Size: X-Large with 28" Inseam in Indigo. I really really love them but they are WAY too short & the color isn't my favorite. If I could have gotten a longer inseam, that would have been perfect. For reference, I am 5'6", 185lbs and normally wear L or XL. I am ordering another color and longer inseam so I will update my review as to the quality and fit. The XL is perfect, not too tight but great compression and smooths out everything. I love love love the crossover waist as it hides the mom pooch. It has back pockets but I do wish it had front pockets too. The fabric is thicker and looks a little more like regular pants vs activewear. Doesn't really look like jeans, but I think it's really just because the color is not exactly a jean color. I think the darker colors will be better. I can't wait to try another pair and I really hope the quality is the same!!

I’ve always been obsessed with leopard print everything, so when I found out FP had leopard print barrel jeans I was stoked until I realized they were out of stock so I opted for these instead. I absolutely love them! They aren’t as thick as FP but that’s okay. I’m normally a M - L (145lbs 5’4”) and didn’t want to risk them being too small so I got the L and I can’t wear them without a belt. So if you’re between sizes, go with the size down. The best and cheaper alternative with the exact same look and fit. I bought these months ago and they’re still holding out really well. The button piece above the zipper does seem to be loosening but that’s fixable.

I just wanna preface this written review and say that yes, I’m a dude, and believe that this is a great 90’s look jeans purchase regardless of your gender. The men’s ones out there are just horrendous and I’m tired of buying and then returning (Amazon and in-store like Levi’s or GAP), and the ones from Temu never fits me because of the horrible sizing. I’m 5’9 with a size 30 waist and an athletic build (I wear size L or XL depending on brand for men’s shirt). After numerous research, I find that there some dudes out there still that still swears by women’s jeans only. Since why not? I used to wear tight skinny women’s jeans back in my emo-phase days in the early 2000’s anyways, so I decided to check out this baggy version that is in-style nowadays as I really like the style. I ordered a large and they fit okay, I could’ve sized down to a medium though, but the large is still good with a belt on. These are stretchy, lightweight, and comfortable. However, just like the women’s jeans back in the day when I used to wear the skinny ones, I’m still disappointed that women’s jeans doesn’t have a big pocket like the men’s jeans do. Why is that? Oof. I wish that would change as some of the ladies out there have these mega tablet-like size phone, and with my iPhone Pro Max, it was sticking out and when I sit down, or shift to a weird angle, they slide off. Same thing for my wallet. Overall though, these are the exact jeans I wanted and highly recommend.

The pockets are nice and large, which is a big plus and actually functional for carrying a phone or other stuff. The overall fit is perfect: relaxed and baggy without looking sloppy. These are stylish, comfortable, and practical, and I’m very happy with how they look and feel. Definitely a great pair of jeans. I'm 5'7" and about 160 lbs; the large is a perfect fit. These are are good value for the quality of material and design.

I’m in love with this jeans,so beautiful and comfortable the only reason I will give you 4 stars is because I’m 5’1 and they are so long,I wish they would be a petite size and I’m 145 pounds regularly used a size 6 but in this jeans i have to order size 8 just in case and they fixed just wonderful

Dark blue size medium. 5’1” 146 lbs. They are just a tad too tight in the waist for me, but I have a fairly thick waist - working on it so hopefully in the next month they will be more loose in the waist. Ordered Med based on other reviews and I think Amazon was pushing extra large. They hit exactly where I hoped they would on my legs. They are strangely both very stretchy while also being delightfully heavier denim. High waist which I really like. My Waist 34” hips 42”. Lots of room everywhere, even in the waist I can put my hand easily down the back, I’m just so used to super comfy lounge wear or dresses. I’m going to keep them b/c tried with a variety of shoes and appreciated the fit with all of them.

Metietila Women’s Casual Pull-on Distressed Stretch Jeans Elastic Waist Jean Denim Joggers Pants Hand these in the light denim and absolutely love them! Finally broke down and bought the white pair for spring/summer. The martial of the white pair is slightly heavier than the light denim, but they still fit get! I'm 5'7 185lbs and ordered large.

I’m 5’6”, 138lbs and ordered the medium. They are very stretchy and not loose at all. More skinny jeans than boyfriend. Comes right under my bellybutton. A little tight in the rear end, but length is perfect. I included a picture/measurements because the reviews are all over the place. Size up, size down, too tight, too loose?? It’s hard to order jeans online! But these are super cute and comfortable.

These are super cute and stretchy. They’re comfortable and fit well. I’m 5’7, 175lbs and the size 10 fits well. The length is perfect for my long legs. The pockets are a nice size, which is pleasantly surprising for women’s shorts. The label on the back gives them a high end feel. They’re a great price. I can’t wait for summer and will definitely be getting more.

Pros: 1. The shape is cute. 2. They stretch 3. They are comfy Cons: 1. The seams are yellow unlike the picture 2. The fabric isn't quality jeans material. More like heavy stretch jeggings. Slightly rough. Too stretchy so they look baggy. (Maybe they will shrink after washing?) 3. The buttons aren't pressed on tightly. They jiggle. I'm 5'4" 128lbs and the small size was big on me. I usually wear a 3 but they fit like a 5. Overall I still like them. I with the fabric was more jean-like.

NOTHING I try on has been flattering lately. And no matter size, I do try. I am menopausal and on steroids, so it makes some body shifting changes that are hard to correct or get used to. Anyway, for reference: I am 5'6" (possibly lower now since clothes are fitting different), and 165 lbs. I have a large frame. More weight in lower body than upper. I love, love love these!! To anyone else, they may not flatter me, but I feel happier about myself in them. I have always loved lower to mid rise jeans, and altho these are higher, they have that feel and don't pinch my waist. I think the crotch comes a little low, even after washing, but its not bad. What do I LOVE: the waist not pinching, as I said; All the useable pockets; the length is great for almost any kind of shoes; I love that they can cinch in for a different kind of style or use; Very comfortable: Didn't shrink much at all after washer & dryer (Maybe just a tiny bit, but like all jean type pants do after drying); They aren't that really thin material you sometimes get with "cargo" pants - these scream durability, without being too thick. As, sort of, said earlier, with my frame, the waist to crotch area is a little long for my body, but it doesn't detract from how much I love these. for me, these are totally 5 stars(I apologize for not matching in pics. it looked ok in mirror. Maybe that atleast shows you the shade of green in tends more toward, if you get the army green)

For what I paid, I can’t really be that disappointed. The chocolate brown is definitely more like a weird muddled/muted calico color, if that makes sense. Not particularly flattering, but I’m going to keep it, because it’s definitely extremely soft. The shape and fit is as expected. I ordered a small and am 5’6” and 120lbs.

Lucky Brand Women's High Rise Bridgette Skinny Jeans I thought I was so excited to find a replacement for my favorite pair of Lucky Brand jeans that seem to have been discontinued by Lucky. Alas. Imagine my disappointment to find out that this pair of "Lucky Brand High Rise Bridgette" have indicators that they are not real Lucky Brand High Rise Bridgette when I compared it side-by-side. This pair has many differences from the genuine pair that I own: - Does not have the printing on the front left pocket liner - Does not have the same rivets, and lacks rivets in some places - The materials it is made of are different. This one is 48% cotton, 34% tencel, 14% polyester, 4% elastane. (the genuine pair of Bridgette that I have are 92% cotton, 6% elasterell, 2% elastane) - There is a typo on the tag that reads "...may have INTERNATIONAL rips or holes" (genuine Lucky Brand jeans say "intentional") - The tags have different appearances for size, brand, cut/style description from the genuine pair. The care tags are sewn in the waistband, where the genuine pair has the care tags sewn to the front pocket liner. - They are both 8/29a, but the size is smaller than the genuine pair and the cut/fit is slightly different.

These Vetinee Barrel Jeans with Cheetah print are very nice looking. The cut is great and they fit really well. The fabric is a thick denim but still soft and comfortable. They look quite stylish. My only issue is that they are too long. They claim to be 27 inches, but on me the hem touches the ground, normally a 27 inch inseam falls at my ankles. Other than that they are a really great pair of pants.

My kids love Carhartt hoodies, beanies, and jackets but this is the first Carhartt item that I have personally owned for myself and I can see why the brand is so popular. I did a little research before I ordered these jeans and it seems the consensus is that they run a bit small. I am 5 foot 4 137 pounds. Normally I am comfortable in a size 6 or 28 in the various brands I wear. I decided to go up to an 8 for these jeans and I’m glad I did, if it weren’t for the waistband, I could probably do a 6 but the 8 is incredibly comfortable as they are. They look just as in the picture on the listing, a nice dark rinse, no fading or destruction anywhere. They have great stretch and recovery. These jeans are a very high waist in the front, they go about an inch above my navel and they dip a little lower in the back but not a whole lot. There is a slight gap in the back waist, but not too terrible. The inseam is exactly 32” and it’s just how I like them, a slight bit lower than my heel when barefoot. The only thing that I find annoying about them, and this is minor, is with the front pockets. The front pockets are long enough to fit my whole hand in, however the cut of the pocket is tapered towards the out seam. This is kind of difficult to describe so I’m including a picture but that doesn’t help much either lol just know that it forces my hand into a less than natural position/feeling. I would absolutely buy another pair of Carhartt jeans, I’m only docking a star due to the fact that they run small.

Very stylish, comfy, and good quality. Did not have to hem I’m 5,2 135 pounds for reference. I got in medium. Fit just a bit loose but loved the fit so I’m keeping them . I will be ordering a different pair in small since they stretch. I really love the high waist feature.

NYDJ Women's Marilyn Straight Denim Jeans This denim is like butter. So comfy. Fits great. But i really need a 32" inseam. These are only 30". Ill give them a try in the wash but if they shrink at all I wont be able to wear them.

I'm 5'3 130 lbs and think these are just okay. They're super stretchy and comfortable. They feel more like flared leggings that look like jeans, than fake denim if that makes sense. I wish the fabric was thicker so it doesn't show everything. They do look like real jeans though from a distance. The worst part is the waist - it looks too silly if you wear it exposed. I'd wear it if you have a long top or sweater covering the waistband. Don't wear something cropped.

Ariat Womens R.e.a.l. Mid Rise Stretch Entwined Boot Cut JeanJeans These feel like a great pant and quality. They feel like they will keep their shape and not stretch like crazy. From just trying them on I feel they do run a touch small. I am 5’1” and 115lbs. I got size 26 short and they fit perfect for length- still a bit snug around the waist. I did order 25s and that was absolutely not happening. I wear a size 2 in Old Navy and American Eagle jeans.

I love Luvamia and I extra love these jeans. They are super soft with a slight stretch and a relaxed fit. Much of Luvamia’s denim is tight at first then relaxes during the first wear. But these are already relaxed and you can feel comfortable ordering your usual size. I am wearing a medium on my 5’5” 165# body. I am not getting any pulling or tugging nor are they too snug on my waist. The waist is “ruffled” like a bag waist. What’s nice is that with a loose shirt, this detail doesn’t show through. But if you want it to show, it looks great with a tucked in shirt. The only thing I wish they had done was to either add another button at the waist where the two sides of the ruffle meet or put a little extra material there. The way the ruffle gaps by the belly button makes it look like the jeans are too snug although they fit well. The price is at the upper range of what I would pay for these. If you can find them on sale or with a coupon, snatch them up! They are really fun, flattering jeans that are super comfortable to wear.

Really they should be a 4.5. Pros: Material is very comfy. Not stiff like traditional jeans, but not fake jegging material either. A thin, soft, real jean looking mix. Long enough not to look like high waters, which is a perpetual jeans problem. I got the size L and I'm 5'6.5" with long legs. I think they look like the '90's baggy jeans I wasn't cool enough to wear then. Cons: I'm basically a size 14 and I found the Large size a little too snug at the hips, even though the size chart said sizes 12-14. If I could order again, I would probably pick XL. The drawstring and elastic waist ultimately wasn't very comfy. It wasn't like sweat pants. The material is soft and pliable, but not stretchy. I like the material and the cut, I would just say order a size up.

These cropped barrel jeans have become my new go-to. I’m 5’2” and usually wear a size 16, so I ordered my usual size. They fit me perfectly. The cropped length hits just below the ankle, which I prefer. The mid-weight fabric with a medium amount of stretch makes them incredibly comfortable. The deep side pockets can hold all but the largest phones, and there are patch pockets in the back. The contrasting stitching gives them a classic jeans look. The stitching is straight and flawless, with no gaps, puckers, or hanging threads. I got them in the “darkness blue” color, which has minimal distressing. They look great dressed up or down. You can wear them with sneakers or pumps, a blazer, or a T-shirt, and they always look stylish. I’ve machine-washed them twice in cool water and dried them on low, and they’ve come out beautifully. I expected nothing less from a fabric blend of 75% cotton, 23% polyester, and 2% elastane. Overall, these are an excellent pair of jeans.
Love these I would buy them in every color! Length is perfect! I am a shorty (5'2") and the crop hits just right! So comfortable, love the placement of the front pockets and the high waist!

I’m 5’9” 300lbs. I bought the 3XL size of jeans and they’re too snug for my liking. They’re stretchy, material feels great, perfect leg length, great quality jeans, if only they were one size larger (which I don’t think they offer any bigger than 3X) they would have been perfect. I’ll be returning these unfortunately.

Lee Women's Sculpting Slim Fit Skinny Leg Pull on Jean The size Long is stated to have a 32 inch inseam. A previous purchase of size 14L was, but two subsequent purchases of size 12L had only a 30 inch inseam. Either there has been a massive mix up and the are labeling their medium length as Long accidentally or they are not stating inseam length accurately and think that loosing a few pounds make you legs get shorter, extremely disappointed.

It's always a gamble ( for me anyway) to order shorts online. I'm 5'10"132lbs usually a size 4. I decided to give these a try in a small and they fit good. There's alot of stretch so they feel fitted but not too tight. I ordered white and they are a decent thickness...not see through but not fully opaque, if that makes sense. I have long legs and I felt that they landed in an awkward spot right at my knee cap. I folded them up one more time and I liked that length better. If you have short legs these may look more like awkward length capris. I've included photos of them rolled up, then one showing where they land without the extra roll.

These are a fantastic invention! For those dress-and-boots girls who like to sit outside at restaurants, these tights are a great option. A few pros and cons: Pros: They really do look like they are tights. They are warm enough for nights where you will be sitting outside They are well made, thick tights that basically hide the fleecing Cons: The liner ends at the ankles, and this may bother you in regular shoes The back seams will leave lines under tight clothing At 5'7", these BARELY fit me, lengthwise. The crotch wants to dip down a few inches. That is uncomfortable to me. So taller women, you may prefer the Large size despite what the chart says. Perhaps they will stretch like normal tights tend to do, but so far, no go. It's still a bit hot here to test them out for long periods of time...but even with the cons, these tights will go to good use.
reviewer_name_raw: Tall Women Be Careful About Sizing

user_comment: These lightweight jeans are super stretchy. They're the kind of stretchy where they will stretch to fit you but they won't offer any shaping because they're too thin. The "denim blue" shade is a nice dark shade, not too cool-toned. Also something to know, they come with a little air freshener packet tucked into the folds. Thankfully, it's not too strong but that feels unnecessary and not great for anyone with fragrance issues. Now to the sizing. Like I said, the high rise is INTENSE. Like a foot-long rise in the front. Which is a bit excessive for me. I'm 5'8" and it doesn't feel like a natural place on my waist for the jeans to stop. An inch or two higher and it would hit the very smallest part of my waist but I'm not sure I'd want that. Thankfully the pockets in the back are low, so they don't look weirdly high. On the size small, the top of the waist measures about 26 inches (with plenty of stretch). I was able to get it over my hips with a bit of shimmying. The hips measure about 39 inches and the leg opening at the thigh is about 22". The inseam is around 29.5". So overall, pretty true to size. I'm not entirely convinced they're worth $36, but if you want a lightweight pair of jeans with a very high rise, they're probably worth trying.

user_comment: I ordered these Sidefeel Flare Jeans for Women in the (6, Dark Blue) version... and I like them. But they do have some quirks that I wish were a little different. They fit me good... Except for in length. I feel like they are a smidge too short. For reference I am 5'5ish, 148ish pounds, 34D, 27" waist, and an hourglass figure. Here's what I know about these pants: •Excellent quality garment, free of defects •Thick, stretchy denim material that doesn't lose its shape throughout the day •The dark blue color is a little more acid washed than they would lead you to believe •Nice button fly •Figure flattering cut with not too high of a waist and not too large of flares •Versatile! Can be styled a variety of different ways •Wash up nicely •Only shrink incrementally in the dryer At the time of ordering these jeans were a little less than $36. Had they have been a little less acid washed and a tiny bit longer, I would have given them five stars. I'll be wearing them with chucks and t-shirts this fall, though... So, overall, it is a win. High Waisted Stretchy Bell Bottoms Wide Leg Side Seam Denim Pants

These are made of a fairly stretchy, reasonably soft denim. They're comfortable to wear. Sizing is a little tricky though. I have a 33 inch waist and a 46 inch hip. The company's size chart says a size 14 for me if you go by the waist size, and hips are bigger than the size 18. I was hesitant to order, but I really liked the pants, so opted for the 16. It was a good choice. The waist fits except for a small gap in the center back, and the hips fit too, though snugly, but the pants stretched enough to wear them and it wasn't to the point of feeling constricted. Hope that helps for those of you wondering what size to order. Also, in reference to length, the pants are about 3-4 inches too long for me. In my case that's to be expected because I'm only 4'10" tall, so in other words they're a decent length for the average height person. Abd they look great! The bell is really wide because of the added inset piece (see picture). The top point of the inset starts about 2 inches lower on my leg than I would prefer (another shortness issue), but it still doesn't look bad even so. The pants appear to be well made, no strings hanging or anything. I also took a picture of the fiber content tag in case that's important to anyone.

Immediately in love with these jeans! I am 5'2" and typically a size 10. These are true to size and have plenty of stretch without feeling like I constantly have to pull them up. They're also incredibly flattering, flattening my belly pooch. The details with the seams and buttons are really interesting and make these jeans stand out from others. The flare is pretty dramatic, which I expected based on the product images. I typically have to purchase short inseams or hem regular ones, so I was surprised to find that these are only about 1" long on me. I can see how a taller person would be turned off by the length of these, but I love the fact that I can easily use hemming tape on these instead of breaking out the needle and thread. For $35 I think these jeans are a steal.

This Sidefeel dark blue, high waisted, bell bottoms jeans look exactly as advertised. It arrived in great condition. It's made up of 72% cotton, 26% polyester and 2% elastane, so it’s stretchy and durable. Everything is sewn well. The buttons are of high quality and they’re attached securely to the pants, they also work as intended. At 5”3 and 158 pounds the pants fit my mother comfortably. However the pants’ legs are a bit too long, so they pool at her feet. There are 5 pockets; 4 of them are standard sized while one is small. The standard sized pockets are roomy; they can hold quite a lot. Overall my mother and I love this pants; the quality is great and it looks good.

Love the fit! First i bought and returned the 12 regular, then bought and kept the 10 short. I will describe each in detail below. For reference, I'm 5'2", currently weigh between 178-180lbs. Normally wear size 12. I recommend sizing down. If you happen to get a pair with a funky smell, I'd say just return and buy a new pair, because the second pair was perfect! Accurate, comfy, flattering, decent price. * First pair, size 12 (first 4 photos): a bit loose in the thighs for my taste. Fit at the waist was okay, but i like a bit of compression at the tummy for shaping. The length was a bit long and awkward for my height and taste. I carry more weight between my shoulders and knees, but my calves are a bit thinner compared to the rest of me. Maybe these would look ok on someone with bigger calves. This pair also came with a strange factory type smell that reminded me of Vic's vapor rub, and the bottoms were very frayed, much more than the picture. Returned. * Second pair, size 10 short (last 4 photos). These were just the fit i was looking for- fitted in the hips, booty, thighs, comfortably loose at the bottom but not baggy or flared. The length was just right for me as well. The waist is a bit snug but not uncomfortable. The fabric is stretchy. After wearing a while, the waist felt fine, also not too loose. These didn't smell bad at all, just a typical new smell, and the ends were not frayed on this pair. It looked exactly like the images.

Levi's Women's 501 Original Shorts (Also Available in Plus) (Wife reviewing obv) Giving 4 stars out of 5 because trying to figure out my size was hella frustrating.. took me days to decide and ended up ordering a size large after reading all of the reviews. I can put both my hands in the waist.. I’ll prolly keep them and hopefully it will shrink a little after I wash it. Length wise, it is pretty short, maybe an inch or inch and a half away from the cheeks.. another thing is mine came with a strong scent, almost smells like febreeze lol Bottom line, if you were to ask me, I’d say order your size if you want a fitted look and go a size up if you want a baggy fit..

Levi's Women's 501 Original Shorts (Also Available in Plus) I'm glad I read the reviews to size up. I'm 5'9 165# and usually wear a 28 (size 6) in Levi's, but I ordered these in a 30 and I'm glad I did. They're a bit roomy around my actual waist, but hug tighter around my hips. Love the look of the style, but these are standing shorts, not sitting shorts lol. The "high rise" rides higher than you'd like when you sit down.

I have these jeans in black. For reference: I’m 5’6”, size XL on top (40DD), and L–XL on the bottom. TRUE TO SIZE & FIT I ordered a size 16 — not a 14. Let’s have an honest moment: wide-leg, pull-on jeans without buttons or a zipper, if too small, will make their presence known in ways nobody asked for. I chose peace. Size 16 fits just right — comfortable through the waist and hips, flattering, and no “why did I do this to myself” regrets. They have a relaxed wide-leg fit but still shape nicely. The waistband sits comfortably and doesn’t dig in. If you’re between sizes, think about how you like your jeans to feel — structured or forgiving. I went with forgiving and I’m happy. LENGTH & STYLING OPTIONS These jeans are LONG. In a good way. Perfect with heels or platform shoes if you want that elongated-leg look. If you’re wearing flats, you can fold them — and it actually looks intentional because the cuff/hem is about 2 inches wide. Not a desperate last-minute roll. I secure mine with small safety pins and it works beautifully. Check my photos to see the full length on someone who is 5’6”. Height matters with wide-leg styles. **FABRIC & QUALITY** The fabric is substantial — not thin, not flimsy, not “fast fashion surprise.” When I first opened them, I genuinely thought they might be 100% cotton. They feel durable with just enough structure to hold shape. Inside seams are finished. No loose threads auditioning for attention. Stitching is straight, margins are even, construction feels solid. They did not skimp on fabric (and we appreciate that). Is it luxury designer denim? No. But for the price point, this is strong quality. I’d confidently rate it 4.6 out of 5. TRENDY Ladies, I’m 54. I prefer trendy over “timeless but slightly sleepy.” These jeans are modern and stylish. Higher-set pockets, visible drawstring (yes, it’s noticeable — that’s the point), front seam detail down the leg, wide-leg silhouette, and that bold cuff option. They don’t whisper. They make a statement — in a good way. I styled them with a cardigan for casual everyday wear and with a cropped jacket for a more structured look. Same jeans. Different mood. That’s versatility. **AMAZON VINE DISCLOSURE** I received these through Amazon Vine. And before anyone thinks we are floating through life in free wardrobes — we pay taxes (around 40%) on the full retail value. Often we end up paying close to what you do, plus writing the review. I don’t give high ratings just because something was sent to me. Fit, comfort, fabric quality, and construction matter. If you found this review , please mark it as such — and may your jeans always fit on the first try.

I have several pairs of jeans from this brand and with their consistency in sizing, comfort, quality, and price I would suggest them as a whole. They make jeans that have the coziness of jeggings/leggings without the susceptibility of getting rips and holes, with the look of denim. I'm typically a size 14/16 pant, 5'5 1/2" tall and ordered the XL. It fits perfectly- hugging my legs in a flattering way that makes them look slender with a "tucked in" apron belly. After washing there was no shrinkage or color fading and the distressed bits didn't go postal and just completely unwind. I adore the length on me- they're short enough to show details of my shoes- I am a HUGE shoe girly- but long enough to keep my ankles from photo bombing life. The stitching details on these are not only decent quality- they add to the overall look. It creates a cuffed hem at the bottom, lines the pockets in a bold manner that reminds me the of 90s/2000s when they would use different colored thread in jeans to give a little va va voom pop. Same vibe, but more subtle. Although the pockets aren't terribly deep- they're functional which is a bonus for women's clothing, always, and they're CUTE. The interesting shape adds a little flare and makes the torso look interesting, especially if you're a tucked in or crop top person. All the hardware is fully functional- no stuck zipper, loose buttons, random surprise visits from your undies because your fly decided to skip town when you sat down. The double buttons also add to the overall aesthetic- so they're functional, sturdy, AND cute. Love the distressed bits at the bottom- they're not obnoxious and they don't rip more while you're wearing them- AND- because they're not actual "holes" I can safely wear these to work. Stick to the provided size chart when ordering and you won't be disappointed. These are like jeans you buy at the mall, but for a fraction of the price! I'd like to see more from this brand. They need to produce MORE- they have comfy, flattering, and stylish bottoms- I KNOW I would like everything they release!

These jeans are great. This brand has yet to disappoint and these are no different. These jeans are both flattering and comfortable. The material is soft and stretchy. They are true to size. With this brand I am always able to order my original size, medium, and it fits perfect. These are the same. They have good set we pockets that you can actually use because things fit inside them. It's a nice change to be able to put keys, your phone, or whatever in your pocket. The stitching is great as is the quality. These are not cheap jeans. They are nice, comfortable, good quality, higher end material. They stay put when you wear them and you don't even "need" a belt. They don't stretch out either. The length was perfect for me, I'm 5'4 and I like them a little longer. The 2 buttons is a nice touch, but the ripped design on the bottom cuffs are my favorite. They add so much to the jeans. I really like it and feel it makes it so you can dress them up more. There's no holes/rips on the leg part of the jeans. They still have style by adding them on the cuffs. I would order these again and more than likely any jeans from this company as I have loved everything I have tried from them (overalls, skinny, and boot cut). I highly recommend trying them if you are looking or wanting an affordable ($35.14 at time of review), comfortable, higher quality, flattering, super cute pair of jeans that hug your lower half and highlight all your best parts.

I'm 55 years old, 5'8" tall, 180 pounds and the size 12 fit good, but there were a few things that bothered me. They are slightly long, coming to the very bottom of the heel on my snow boots. I think these would look great on a gal who wears western boots. I feel like at times I have to pull them up in the back, but I've always had trouble finding jeans that fit me perfect. They just won't be my go-to pair.

Not gonna lie I always feel a little self conscious in these types of jeans.. I feel I look a little heavy in them because of my body type. I do try to wear curve love jeans as they fit me better. That being said I got a small in these because it fits a 4/6 pant. I wear a 4 in curve love. My waist is 26 in but my butt and hips are a 38.5. The length is great lots of space in the waist for me. Very cute though. Def have to wash they smelled like chemicals.

At 60+ years old, I was not sure that these jeans would be for me. I'm happy to say that taking the chance was worth it and I love them! They are well made of 75% cotton, 23% polyester, and 2% elastane. The material is soft and about a medium thickness. The sewing is neat and tidy, with firm, strong seams. The elastane is just the right amount to make them very comfortable to wear, even if doing something that requires a lot of active movement. As for sitting, they do not pinch or bind anywhere. The look/style of these jeans is much nicer than I thought after I saw them in person. I ordered these jeans in a size XL, my regular size. They fit me quite well. The waist, hips, and length are all just right. I thought at my age I might look silly wearing studded jeans, but after getting the jeans, I found them to be fun to wear! For reference, I'm 5' 4" tall, have a 33-ish waist, and 44" hips, and 182 lbs. I'm very glad I decided to try this pair of studded jeans. I can highly recommend them! luvamia jeans are some of my favorites that I have because they are good quality, and fit me comfortably. The coupon price currently (9-11-2025) of $33.43 is a great value, in my opinion. I would buy these jeans again, without hesitating. :)
reviewer_name_raw: Nice fit, super comfy, fun jeans.

Levi's Women's New Boyfriend Jeans Nice quality medium weight denim with a stretch. I’m 5’3” 130lbs , shaped like a rectangle according to my scale app (flattering, I know 🫤)l got a sz 27 & fits me perfect. Not too tight, a little loose in the leg like a boyfriend cut should be.

Levi's Women's New Boyfriend Jeans I ordered a 29 (size 8) as I am 5’5 and 152 lbs. they fit well in hips but the waist is a bit loose, I will be wearing a belt so they don’t go down when I squat or bend. I bet I could have sized down but shipping will take 1-2 months (?) so I’ll just keep these. Nice stretch, which would probably also allow for sizing down.

Levi's Women's New Boyfriend Jeans These are the perfect casual pair of jeans! I’m only 5’2 so finding the right ankle length is difficult, but they hit just right. Soft with just the right amount of stretch. The first pair I ordered were too big, definitely run big so size down. The picture doesn’t do them justice.

I like these jeans overall. They are comfortable aroynd the waist, true to size, and look exactly like the pictures in the description. There is not a lot of stretch, so I suggest you order according to the size guide. The design is unique and the quality feels good. The length is a bit long for me—I’m 5'2"—but that’s manageable with heels. For the price, they are okay, though I do think they should have been softened more before selling. The denim is quite stiff, and I had to soak them overnight in a vinegar solution to soften them, which is why I’m taking one star off. Other than that, they are well made and have a nice, stylish design.

I’m a little conflicted here! There are some really great things about these jeans and some not so great things that made rating a bit tough. Overall, I like them a lot! But there are some things I would have wanted pointed out - so the description does say “raw hem” but the pictures only show this a little whereas in person - it’s a lot more noticeable that I thought they had come on done at first. I also saw a few loose stitches in the waist area - not bad that I think it would be falling apart anytime soon, but something definitely noticeable. Good things though - these are true to size and very comfortable. They are a little bigger on me as I was leaning on the side of caution and got a 40 hip (size 10) when I’m a 39 hip (size 8 on their chart). I’m about 5 ft 4 and although they do touch the ground which is the style for these, they are not comically long as I’ve experienced with other brands. I think they are great for my shape - I’m heavier on top than bottom. I think I’m going to hem the bottom to shorten them a little more but would go for an 8 if I got a different color. Overall I think it’s a great budget jean and one that I’m glad I tried.

I ordered these jeans because I liked the baggy style and raw hem. The jeans are a nice medium weight material with a bit of stretch. Unfortunately, they were too long on me, and the fit is not very flattering on me. I'm 5'2", 119 lbs, and I Jesse's a size 6. The fit around the waist and hips was good. But the length and width around the legs didn't favor me. I think this style would look better on taller people. They're cute jeans, and wish they had worked for me.
reviewer_name_raw: Nice baggy jeans for taller people.

user_comment: These wide, flare leg jeans from Dokotoo are well made. I did not find any type of faults with the making of them. The fabric is soft and has a goodly amount of stretch in it for added comfort. The thickness of the material leans toward being thin, but certainly not see through at all. I really like the style and look of them. They remind me a lot of the smaller bell bottom styles of the '70's with what looks to be a triangular piece added on the outside of the leg to flare out the bottoms. The button and zipper fasteners work smoothly without any catches or stiffness. They have 2 front pockets and 2 on the back. All 4 pockets are roomy and functional. The bottom hem is an unfinished raw edge, which I also like. The Dark Blue color is nice and not overly dark, which I prefer. I ordered this pair of jeans in a size 16, which is my regular number size for jeans. They fit quite comfortably and do not pinch or feel tight around my waist or hips. There is enough stretchiness in the fabric, I may have been able to wear a size 14 comfortably. The length is a little too long for my 5' 4" height. I'm thinking with the raw bottom edge and they have the markings of a once upon a time hem, I will maybe cut them off one line up on those markings. For reference: along with the 5' 4" height, I have a 33-ish waist, 44" hips and weigh 182 lbs. Overall, these jeans are good quality, both in materials and sewing, so I will recommend them. A caution for shorter persons as they are longish. The stretchy fabric and nice looking style make them a good addition to my collection of jeans. The price when I place my order (1-5-2026) of $36.99 is a good value for a pair of jeans, in my opinion.

Democracy Women's Petite Ab Solution Jegging I liked that these came in petite size. Jeans are always way too long on me as I am 5’1. I weigh 160 and size 10 fit perfect. The length was perfect. For the price, I’m not impressed and won’t keep. The color is way darker than pictured also. For almost $80 I had higher expectations for these jeans. They’re just ehh.

Democracy Women's Petite Ab Solution Jegging I love these jeans! Very flattering and soooooo comfortable! They are worth the money and I will be ordering different colors. I'm 5"3, 120 pounds and wearing a size 2 petite.

Democracy Women's Petite Ab Solution Jegging I love these jeans! Very flattering and soooooo comfortable! They are worth the money and I will be ordering different colors. I'm 5"3, 120 pounds and wearing a size 2 petite.

Democracy Women's Petite Ab Solution Jegging I am 5' 3" and curvy with thicker thighs. These are 14 petites and fit perfectly. Very soft, comfortable and don't slip. I love the Democracy jeans. These are my 3rd pair and I plan to replace all of my jeans with the Democracy brand.

While these are flattering and stretchy and super cute, get a size or 2 down. I got a L and could have easily fit into a S or M. They stretch and form to your body, which is always a plus. The quality of the material is great. Wash them in Cold. I have not had any fading issues at all. Though I have to wear a belt with them, the flare at the bottom is fun and stylish. Pair these with some platforms for a fun look.The length is great for my height at 5'5" and the small details on the overall design brings out a unique look.They're extremely comfortable and can be worn for hours. They do loosen up over time however, so I have had to wash them each time I've worn them, as the size is far too big on me.Which isn't a bad thing, but hopefully this review helps others size down and get the actual fit and look these pants are meant to show off.

I've had really good luck with the Roswell brand in the past and this time is no different. I'm really loving these jeans! They have a great retro vibe but with a nice stretch that often wasn't present in the early days of this style! Based on the size chart, I ordered a large in keeping with my typical size 12. The fit is perfect with the right amount of stretch in places where I need it without being frumpy and baggy everywhere else. The fabric is medium weight...perhaps a bit on the lighter side and is extremely comfortable. Aside from the comfort and the look, one of my favorite things is that these jeans can be worn on my 5'4" frame without requiring heels. This might not be a great feature for taller women but if you're like me and often feel stuck between petite and regular length, these are perfect! I would definitely recommend!

These GRAPENT barrel jeans are such a great everyday staple. I typically wear a size 10 in pants, but I followed the size guidance and ordered a Large and it fit perfectly. For reference, I am 5’2” and around 155 lbs, and the Large gave me a relaxed and comfortable fit without feeling tight anywhere. Even though these are technically cropped with a 26 inch inseam, on me they hit right at the ankle, which I absolutely love. As a short girly, I usually struggle with jeans being too long, but these worked out beautifully with no hemming needed. The barrel silhouette gives a trendy shape while still feeling easy and wearable. I wore these all day and they were incredibly comfortable from morning to night. There is lots of room through the legs, the high waist feels secure, and the stretch makes them easy to move in. They are roomy without looking sloppy, and the patch pockets add both function and style. If you are petite and usually around a size 10, the Large was spot on for me. Super comfortable, flattering, and perfect ankle length. Summary Highlight True to size when following size chart Size Large fits perfect for size 10 Great for petite girls 5’2” Hits right at the ankle All day comfort with stretch Roomy barrel silhouette Functional patch pockets Trendy but very wearable

Loving these barrel jeans! They have great structure to them so they keep their barrel shape- even after washing! Just make sure you don’t put them in the dryer. I ordered the black size M and they are a great true black. I’m usually between size S and M but I sized up for these and at first, I was glad I did but after wearing them for a whole day, they’ve loosened up. I wish I would have ordered size S. They are a great length too. I’m 5’5” and they hit right at or a little above the ankle. I love the front pocket details and the waist cinches in just slightly so it gives you a great shape. The only issue I’ve had is the black dye rubs off on your skin a little bit if you don’t wash them first. I haven’t had that issue since I washed them. Great price too! Highly recommend.

I’m 5’2, and usually a size 6-8. I got these in a medium and they are very comfortable. The style is very “in” right now, but I did dock a star because they aren’t the most flattering jeans in the world. They gave me a serious mom butt, and they smell weird/bad. That is obviously something to be remedied with washing but it is a very strong smell. So I’m giving 4 stars because overall I do like them. I’d say $35 is a reasonable price for these.

These barrel jeans are super stretchy and baggy. I probably could have sized down but still like the look of the current size. They have 4 pockets. I like that the front pockets and the back pockets are the same. It makes it easier to slide your phone in your front pockets and also safer! The material of the jeans seem on the lighter side which is probably due to the stretchy factor but they feel soft. This isn't a big deal for me BUT it's important to note that they are not a heavy pair of jeans if you're packing them to travel. It's also important to note that if you are short, I'm 5'3, then these pants hit at a weird length so you'll have to roll them up or wear heels to make the length look proper. All and all, I think these are a solid pair of jeans that will be good for traveling to help with weight limits.
reviewer_name_raw: VERY STRETCHY BUT A BAGGY FIT

user_comment: SHOCKED how nice these soft jeans feel straight out of the package. I usually hate jeans, and find it hard to get a decent fit, but these are wide leg, but not TOO wide, long enough to style with short booties, and stretchy without looking too sloppy. So, I consider these a win for my closet and I will wear them very casually. I bought the dark wash in a size Medium, I am 5'5" and weigh ~ 148 and they fit nicely. They have pockets that are useable and back pockets as well. I don't usually wear belts, but they do have real belt loops so you certainly can wear a belt. I don't find them too long, but just right. Nice quality for the price. I do not consider them pajama or sweatpant feel, no fleece or inner french terry and thankfully, NO weird smells or odors. I can recommend if you want a casual pair of pull up jeans to head out the door.

Got as a gift. My sister is 5'2" and they were way too long. She doesn't mind hemming them since they have a nice fit otherwise-- stretchy, comfortable and with usable pockets. She also is willing to line dry them but others might find that a drawback. Care instructions say "do not tumble dry".

Just buy these jeans! Seriously, buy them and thank me later. I've always preferred my jeans on a thinner and stretchier side. I love pull-on jeans, not just for their comfort and convenience but I don't like the zipper/button bulging under a tight shirt. These jeans are SOFT! The fabric is on a thinner side and very stretchy. They feel absolutely wonderful and are the ultimate comfort jeans. It's as if your favorite jeans and sweatpants had a baby. I am 5'7", 130 lbs and size small fits me well. They are on a loose side both around the hips and legs. They are true high waisted, wide leg jeans. I highly recommend them and will buy more colors.

These jeans have plenty of stretch but they are also stylish and create a nice silhouette. I am 5'2" with short legs so I would need to hem these if I didn't wear them with heels. Love the dark denim color and find it really elevates a "jeans look". I do think I could have sized down one and been fine so if you're in between sizes, I'd go with the lower size. They are quickly becoming one of my favorite wardrobe pieces.

These jeans are super cute and very comfortable overall. I love the wide-leg fit and the high waist — they’re flattering and easy to dress up or down. The elastic waist with the drawstring is a big plus and makes them easy to pull on, especially for casual days. The denim is lightweight and perfect for warmer weather. My only downsides: the back pockets are placed very far apart and sit a bit too much toward the sides, which looks a little off from behind. Also, aside from the waistband, the denim itself doesn’t have any stretch, so keep that in mind if you’re between sizes. Overall, still a great pair of trendy, comfy jeans, just wish the pocket placement and stretch were a little better. Note - I’m 5’2 135lbs and I went with a small. Pants fall right about my ankles. Waist fits ok. I think a small or medium would’ve been fine

I am extremely pleased with this pair of casual summer pants. I ordered the XXL in cream white, and the color is exactly as described—a soft, flattering white that feels fresh and versatile without being overly bright or stark. It pairs beautifully with both neutral and bold tops, making it a great staple piece for warm-weather styling. Add a pair of sneakers or sandals or a sweatshirt on a cool night and you have a great outfit! For reference, I typically wear a size 16/18, and the XXL provides a perfect fit. The silhouette is intentionally relaxed and slightly baggy, creating a comfortable, effortlessly casual look. The wide-leg design enhances the laid-back summer aesthetic while still looking polished and put-together. I am 5’6”, and the length is ideal—the hem falls just above my ankle, which is a very flattering cut. It offers a breezy feel without appearing cropped too short. The proportions feel thoughtfully designed, especially for someone in my height range. The overall construction of these pants is impressive. The fabric is strong and sturdy, giving the garment structure and durability without feeling heavy. The stitching and finishing details appear well-executed, indicating quality craftsmanship. Despite the lightweight, summery style, the material does not feel thin or flimsy. Overall, these pants strike an excellent balance between comfort, fit, and quality. They are stylish, well-made, and perfect for casual summer wear. I would highly recommend them to anyone looking for a comfortable, wide-leg option that delivers both durability and a flattering fit.

I wish they'd reduce the pleats around the waist. The overall length, pockets, and other details are fine.

user_comment: The fabric is nice and soft, perfect for the warmer days. I feel like they are true to size width-wise, but see the photo… I’m 5’9” and these pants end 2-3” above my ankles. My dimensions are 130 lbs, 5'3", waist 28-29", long torso, short legs and I ordered the medium in classic blue. I wear these pants when I feel like lounging around the house, but also want to be dressed for running errands. The inseam, of 26", has the hem landing above my ankles. The material is comprised of 90% Cotton and 10% Polyester and is lightweight, soft and thin denim. A composition that makes these pants ideal for Summer weather, or curling up on a couch with a blanket. Given that they contain Polyester which helps with de-wrinkling, I throw the pants in the dryer on cool air for 15 minutes to soften the wrinkles; the results are shown in my pictures. Usually denim can be tumbled dry, but the seller recommends only cold washes and air dry. The medium feels true to size, and are a loose, baggy fit. The pockets are deep and sewn into the material to lie flat against the garment which prevents them from adding weight to your waist or be visually distracting. The tie closure stays in place and adds texture and interest to the pants. The color of the classic blue is very similar to what is represented in the advertisement. The indigo dyeing techniques sometimes generate more than 30 shades of a blue color; this means that two pairs of jeans within in the same dying process can come out "similar" but not exactly the same. I am pleased with the pants and find the seller's description to be accurate. I like wearing these pants with tennis shoes, or flip flops. I do not like the way they look with boots.

I love these very comfortable and cute trousers. I love these pants, they are so cute! I am 5’2 about 150 lbs with a mom pooch and I ordered the Medium. They are the perfect length for me and not too tight. I feel like the pattern makes them very forgiving in concealing your lower belly

How fun! These trousers...er....printed jeans....are ADORABLE! They are high waisted, wide leg (but not "flowy" - just a bit bigger than straight leg trousers), And for me at 5"4" skim the top of my kitten heels so they are visible and become a definite part of the ensemble. Fabric is a stretch jean, nice weight, with all sorts of animal prints to choose from: 1. Brown cheetah - nice warm brown background (almost golden bronze) with black / brown cheetah spots 2. a variety of leopard prints a) Large leopard print in dark brown on a soft brown background b) Mini leopard print in dark brown on the same soft background as above c) Mini leopard print in dark grey on a soft light grey backgound d) Large Leopard print in medium warm brown on a light sand tone background Construction is excellent: 1) Typical jeans construction with metal zipper and at the waist a metal stud closure. There are no stress point rivets so as to present as a more "dressy" finish. 2) Outside side seams are top stitched from top of the waistband to mid hip 3) Inseams are top stitched all the way down, (length for the jeans I received was 29.5 inches) 4) Back seam through the crotch and up to the front sipper is the typical flat feld seam one expects on jeans. 5) Hem is generous - if necessary, before washing one could let it out and gain that amount to the length (another 1.5 inches) 6) Front pockets are on a slight slant off the waistband - essentially top-down opening, nice and deep so as to be functional, with the top slide-in opening the best from a design aspect as the pockets do not gap open. Pocket hems are double sewn - wonderful! 7) Back pockets are unremarkable - they are a rectangular patch pocket and seem to disappear into the busy pattern of the jeans 8) Fabric stretches around the legs and hip - with no stretch the length of the jeans - very comfortable fit. It also has a nice heft to it - great for cooler weather, but would work even for the evening is spring and summer as a bit upscale I accidentally miss-order the size and received trouser that are too large; However, based on what I received I can share that the documented measurements in the size chart appear to be true to size> Order your regular size and you'll be great as to what the design is meant to be. My only criticism is, for me, the crotch definitely seems to be a bit long. I prefer higher waist trousers, but this is more than that. For me, I will have these altered down to my correct size plus have the crotch raised as its quite worth it - these are great trousers, and the pattern definitely puts them in the next level up - even from your best jeans. Surprisingly inexpensive for what you receive. Purchase these and check them out!

The material is lighter weight and softer than typical jeans material – almost like dress slacks, so the pants are somewhat more flowy and not as stiff as jeans would be. The slight bit of stretch to the material also makes them much more comfortable than jeans, nearly as comfortable as loungewear, except the waistband does occasionally constrict a bit when slouching. It’s not enough to be uncomfortable; it just reminds you that they’re high-waisted with a jeans-style waistband, not one made of elastic. I typically wear a size 10-12 (5’5” & 165 lbs) and ordered the size large because my measurements put me in between sizes with these pants. That may not have been the best choice because I feel like the pants are a bit big on me, so keep that in mind if you are between sizes like me. I’ve styled these with a khaki shirt, cream blazer, and tan loafers for casual office wear, and also worn the pants with a black satin blouse and a pair of strappy wedges for a date night out with dinner.
