I own several pieces from luvamia at this point, and they’ve been consistently reliable for quality and fit. This is the first time I’ve had any sizing issues with the brand. For reference, I’m 5’3” and 154 lbs, and I ordered my usual size medium. Technically, they fit, but just barely. They’re a little tighter than I’m used to from this brand, especially through the hips and seat. I have a generous backside, and they definitely put the elastic waistband in the back to work. It dips slightly lower than I’d prefer because of the stretch, but thankfully the elastic stretches instead of gapping the way a regular waistband would. That flexibility saves them from being uncomfortable. (And for what it’s worth, my partner is very much a fan of the snug fit across the back.) I tried them on both before and after washing and drying on normal settings. They did not shrink and there was no noticeable fading. The “Darkness Blue” color is exactly what I hoped for, a true dark denim that is easy to dress up or down. The fabric is a nice medium weight with excellent stretch. The construction is solid with clean seams, even stitching, no loose threads. The front patch pockets are generously sized and actually usable, and the back pockets are functional as well. I really like the small side notch at the hem. It gives the cuffs a more finished look. The length is actually perfect for me at mid-calf. As a short person, sometimes capris can be closer to ankle length on me. At $30.99 at the time of my review, the price lands on the higher end of what I personally consider acceptable for capri denim. The quality supports it, but given the slightly snug fit compared to other luvamia pants I own, I do wish sizing were a bit more consistent. If you’re between sizes or carry more weight in the hips and seat like I do, I would seriously consider sizing up. If you prefer a more fitted look, your usual size will likely work just fine.

These Capri jeans are well made of 75% cotton, 23%polyester, and 2% elastane. The fabric has a good amount of stretch in it, not just in the elastic waistband. They are soft and comfortable. The thickness of the material leans closer to medium, than thin. The have an fully elastic waistband that is wide enough so that it does not want to roll down, but not extra wide. There are no buttons or zippers, they are pull-up jeans. I like the small slit on the outer bottom hems of the legs. There are 4 pockets total; 2 in front that are similar to larger sized patch pockets, and 2 regular sized pockets on the back. I really like the Timeless Blue color of these jeans! This pair of Capri jeans are so easy to toss on and out the door for running errands, carpooling the kids, etc. I ordered them in a size XL, which is my regular jeans size. They fit a bit snug on my hips, but with the amount of stretch in the fabric, they are quite comfy without pinching or binding anywhere. For reference: I'm 5' 4" tall, 33-ish waist, 44" hips, and 182 lbs. I'm very happy with this pair of Capri jeans and certainly will recommend them. The material is so easy and comfortable to wear. They're nice, quality made jeans. The current price (2-9-2026) of $30.99 is a good value, but also at this time, there is a coupon available to reduce the price and make them an even better value. They offer other shades of blue plus a cream white and a washed black for color choices.

I wore them hard too, b/c of my figure (way smaller than now), I always had issues fitting my hips and waist. I had to give them up after giving birth to my son due to medical issues. That was 16 years ago. I have been watching tons of reels of ladies working out and their new wardrobes, and it inspired me to try a pair of jeans again. I am shocked I found the perfect pair on my first purchase! Holy Cow! I am in love. Of course, they aren't as comfortable as leggings, however, this pair has stretch in all the right places. I am still working on losing weight and went with flare b/c my thighs are WAY bigger than my calves. I am just stunned that I have no back gap. I always had an issue there b/c I am swayed back. Seriously, check out this brand on Amazon. Read the reviews and measurements. FYI, Elastic, high-waisted pants are best for those with a sway & not a gap. I hate the idea of high-waisted pants, but these are so comfy. I wear large in leggings, which is what was suggested for this purchase. My waist is larger than the sizing chart, 40, and my hips are 43. I fit perfectly in the large for 30.5 - 32 waist and 40.5 - 42 hips. Keep this in mind. Color is on point. Value for money is great considering I have issues fitting in most years. The Fabric is of great quality and itchy. The seam isn't tight in the crotch/butt region.
Just got mine. I love them. I’m 5’5 and about 145. I ordered medium/regular. They feel great and the material is on point. I will be ordering other colors. I’m very happy with these.

I had ordered these in bay blue and loved them so much I ordered in the light brown color. These are even softer than the blue ones and are like a guilty pleasure! I wore mid heel boots with them and a fall sweater and got so many compliments on them while feeling like I was wearing sweat pants! I’m 5’4”.

I have these EVALESS high waisted stretchy jeans with paneling in a size 12, in the “dirty blue” shade. I think they’re great quality, comfortable, and flattering. The color in this wash seems much darker in person than it appears online but I do like it; I’m hardly seeing any of the faded white streaks in the middle of the thighs. The panel seams on the inner thighs are trendy. The rise is comfortable, the material is stretchy but thick and hugs you, and I find these flattering through the leg. Size 12 fits me true to size. For reference I am 5’5 and curvy. They came cuffed but I will consider uncuffing the bottom as the model shows depending on my shoe choice; as others have stated they are on the shorter side as they hit above my ankle bone after washing one time. I think these look slightly elevated and more expensive than they are; they would work with a variety of outfits and I will pair them with spring tops.

I own several tops from the Evaless brand that I love, so I was excited to try these jeans. I chose my normal size 12 and they fit true to size and are pretty comfortable with a little stretch. I am 5'4" so I usually have issues with pants being too long on me. The hem on these fit me perfectly, but if you are taller, they may be too short for you. I chose the Dirty Blue, and they're just a bit darker than the listing photos. The jeans are very well made, but the style just isn't right for me. The paneling on the inner legs stands out and looks a little odd to me. I think this style would probably be great for a taller, thinner person.

I really love these jeans and am now considering looking for more items from this particular manufacturer because the quality is excellent. This is a first for me for an item not made in the US. These pants are a good weighted material with just a bit of stretch. Stitching looks well done. Double buttons work, are not too tight to close and not too loosely sewn on. The pockets are very deep! My whole hand, all the way to my wrist, easily fits in them. Back pockets are also good sized. I am pretty short, so these are a bit long, but are fine with shoes or boots. Nice fit in the legs and I can comfortably bend in them. If I had anything negative to complain about, it might be no side pocket on the leg, but I like these so much that I won't complain. But, if they come out with a pair that has the pocket on the leg, I'll definitely buy them! I'm currently debating on whether to get another pair or two in black and blue before winter ends or wait till next winter to see if they go on sale. I definitely recommend these if your are of average height and weight or a little on the heavy side. They fit well. I'm currently between sizes so went a size down thinking they'd be a bit tight, but they are perfect. For reference, I'm only 5' and 170 lbs. and ordered a size 14.

The good things I can say about these jeans is that they run true to size and they are pretty comfortable for jeans that don't have a ton of stretch. I got the washed black color and I do like the shade of it. What I don't like quite as much as I hope was the styling and the fit of them. The way they are designed, there's like a weird seam in the front and the pockets are a little weird and I just don't love the design of them in and of itself, but I also don't feel like they're the most flattering jeans. They make my butt look much flatter than it actually is, and at 50 years old I need all the help I can get lol. That's sad I'm sure I'll get some use out of them, but I still wish they looked a little bit more flattering / stylish.

These jeans are so fun! I love the cut of the jeans and the buckle deail in the back is so cute. They are true to size. Went with a large and im 5'2 and 178 lbs. They are all the way to the floor. I am very happy with these jeans. As a mom with a tummy, long gone are the days when I can wear the low waisted or hip level jeans. These jeans are nice and stretchy and are definitely high-waisted so no rolls popping over the top!- For reference, I am 5'5", muffin top tummy and zero waist (not zero as in zero inches, zero as in I am straight as a rectangle). I am usually a size 8/10 in pants, but I went with the 12 because I often find that I need a little extra room in the waist in denim. I think this worked great, and I would say that if you have a larger belly/waist go with the size up. Even with the really stretchy material (and there is a nice amount of 4-way stretch and give to this denim), the waist is a little smaller (the waist fit me more like a 10 where the rest of the pant was larger like I would expect a 12 to fit).- These are full length on me (even with the fold -- and that fold is tacked on the side so it isn't just an ironed in hem).- These are definitely "mom" jeans in terms of the height of the waist and there are no issues with my tummy rolling over. However, as I said the waistband is a little tighter than the rest of the pants for the size, so I did feel that is pushed all of my tummy into the front of the pants.I think these are definitely cute. I very much appreciate the stretchy denim and the high waist. I do think the waistband has a little less give and runs a little smaller than the sizing of the rest of the pants (especially if you have a muffin-top tummy/wider waist) -- so consider sizing up if that is your case, too.

As a 69 - going on 70 year old woman, who still lives in jeans and t-shirts, my wardrobe was in crisis mode. After a long and challenging illness and treatment, I lost so much weight that all my pld jeans became baggy masses that I couldn't keep up without the risk of public embarrassment. And some have turned into... let's just say 'ventilated' versions of themselves - rips under the butt that weren't there when I bought them! I can just hear people thinking 'Look at that grandma trying to look trendy!'.So, when these ETTELLO jeans arrived courtsey of Amazon Vine, I was overjoyed. The moment I opened the package, I knew I was in love. The jeans are well-made, with strong stitching, and the fabric is soft yet sturdy, with this magical 4-way stretch that's actually real denim. The design? Perfectly high-waisted, straight leg with trendy ankle fold hem that even this grandma can rock. The 5 pockets are a dream, and the light blue color is so fresh. They arrived quickly, well-packaged, absolutely no strange smell, and I couldn't wait to try them on.Now, here's where I almost cried. I used to be a solid size 6 at 115-120lbs, but I ordered a size 4 because, well, I dropped to 100-105lbs (yes, I'm working hard on gaining it back!), and they looked TINY. I thought, 'No way are these going to fit,' but lo and behold - they fit perfectly! Even better, they'll still fit when I finally gain my weight back, thanks to the stretchy fabric. And for reference, I'm 5'4" (I haven't shrunk too much, yet) with a 31-32" insem, and the length is right at my ankles - perfection. If I ever want them longer, I can just undo the ankle fold, and adjust to any length I want, but for now, I'm loving them as is.These jeans really hold everything in place. My skinny backside, which had been hanging somewhere around mid-thigh in my old jeans, is now right where it belongs. My daughter and nieces immediately fell in love with them too (sorry, ladies, Amazon Vine only offers you one!). But don't worry - I'm already planning on buying more in black and dark blue, and probably gifting a few pairs to my girls.Long story short, these jeans are a win! Reasonably priced for quality, functionality, and versatility they offer, and they would look great on any woman, regardless of age, size, or shape. Highly recommended 👌 👍

These are true to size. I sized up because I typically go with my size and many times Amazon brands tend to be on the smaller size. Definitely regret doing this as I think my regular size would have fit just fine. These look pretty baggy, but they'll be fine for wearing around the house. The length is great, I am 5'5" and they come down to my ankles, so I can use them with flats or heeled shoes. They aren't quite as dressy as I had hoped the denim would look in person, but I can still dress it up with a cute top or sweater. I know the baggy/relaxed and wide leg look is back in, and it is taking this Xennial a bit to adjust to this again. I have been in the "mom jean" skinny jeans mode for so many years, that I am trying to rediscover more wide-legged pants again, and these are a great way to explore this look again. I'm not sure if this is more related to getting a bigger size than I probably should have, but the backside is not very defining, so if you are looking for a bit of lift or accentuation back there, these probably wonn't provide that. Again, not an issue if I wear a longer top or sweater, but definitely something to note. Will update again later with how these hold up after a few wears and washes.

I have seen some gorgeous jeans in ads only to order them and they're not jeans at all: they're a jean-type pant, kind of a legging, dyed a phony blue, attempting a jean look and failing. Not these! Finally, a literal pair of jeans with real jean material that is stretchy, yet holds everything in in such a flattering way! I am 5'4" and weigh 177 lbs. I am mentally preparing myself to start eating healthier and add some fitness in my life, but I'm just not there yet. So I bought these pants in size 10, when I normally would wear a 12 to 14, hoping they would give me the motivation to start my slim down journey. I pulled the jeans out of the packaging and almost cried tears of joy when I felt the material and quality. They were perfect! I held them up to me and they looked pretty small for my size, but I decided to try them on to see how much I would need to lose. Welp, the stretch allowed me to actually fully squeeze in them and I can wear them NOW! In addition, if I lose 30-40 lbs like I hope to, these jeans will fit even better. As you can see in my photos, the jeans hold me in, instead of showing every real-life bulge I actually have. I wore them all day running errands and they were still comfortable, just a little tighter in the waist than I would have wanted but I'm lucky I can wear them at all. Do I recommend these? Absolutely. I actually recommend buying more than one pair.

Levi's Women's Premium 501 Original Shorts ​​​​​(Also Available in Plus) I’m 5’7, 135lbs, on a scale of Kim to Kendall butt, I have a Kourtney. And I got a size 28 (: *I did originally order a 27 and it was wearable but not at all breathable, 28 was perfect. Pros: The quality is great. They’re a true vintage high rise fit. They are loose enough to breathe and be comfortable which is perfect since there’s no stretchiness in them at all. They are great coverage, so even if you bend over your butt cheeks won’t show. Cons: So while they fit perfect around the waist and hips, the cut off at your legs is horrendous lol. In the last picture, the piece I’m holding was attached with strings that went over my thigh but it was so bad and even tighter than the actual shorts so I had to cut them out. The third and forth picture show what I mean, sitting down you will have biscuit can bulging thighs, period. Lol and they roll up when sitting down so it can get really uncomfortable quick…and as you can see the space on my waist and belly area even when I’m sitting so it’s not that the shorts are overall too small. When standing they aren’t tight around my thighs at all though. Also, in the third pic when I’m sitting, that poof that looks like my belly is actually space just more space in the shorts. Lol. The last two cons I have aren’t really cons that just are weird little things about the shorts that bug me but not enough to actually call them cons. So first one is the crinkle on the butt in the center area. I dont have a huge booty but I have a booty, if that makes sense, so it’s just a weird thing they do I guess. If you have a bigger butt you may not have that issue idk. The second thing is there’s 5 buttons. Which isn’t an issue expect the lowest one is LOW lol and it can be a booger to get them in the loops as some of the loops are not that big. So to sum it up, they’re great shorts for anything that doesn’t involve a lot of sitting.

Levi's Women's Premium 501 Original Shorts ​​​​​(Also Available in Plus) I feel like "cute/flattering but uncomfortable" is the tagline I could use for pretty much every pair of Levi's I've tried, minus the stretchier styles. To start, I got these in both a SIZE 32 and 33 in the color LUXOR HEAT. I'm anywhere between a size 8-12 with a 31" waist and 43" hips, give or take, but I always feel like I have to size up in Levi's. True to form, I would not have liked my true size 30 or 31 in these, so I'm glad I went up. I ended up keeping the size 32 (pictured), as the 33s were way too baggy. I could probably have done a 31 but I like having a little extra room for my thick thighs and big butt. Keep in mind these do have the button fly, like all the 501 styles do. I personally hate button flys, but I wanted to give them a shot anyway. These shorts are adorable on - I feel really cute when I wear them and my butt looks great. However, they are pretty uncomfortable. This jean material is what I'd consider a classic, sturdy fabric with almost zero stretch, though I will say the Levi's Premium fabric is softer than the Original. These do not conform to your curves much, though I'm sure wear will eventually soften the denim (or you can search for tricks to soften them, like I eventually did). If I'm walking around or standing, I don't really have complaints, but I would probably not wear these in a situation where I'll be sitting very much. On that note, if you have thicker thighs, these shorts feel like they're going to cut them off when you're sitting. I was a soccer player for a long time so have always had larger thighs, and this is one of the reasons I've never loved wearing short-shorts, because they cut into my thighs. The edge of the fabric just digs in while you're sitting, even with these cut off styles. I'd recommend these to someone who likes a stiffer denim and/or has skinny thighs, or someone who just doesn't care, but I find myself super self-conscious sitting in these, so I don't reach for them much. I kept them because there are situations I'd still wear them, plus I just needed shorts. I much prefer my Levi's Premium 501 Mid Thigh Shorts in the same size.

I just received these jeans, & I’m already wearing them as I write. I'm thrilled with them. -nice thick denim/ elastine blend material: the garment feels extra sturdy in the hand, & like it will be long lasting & hold its shape well; (unlike other jeans I own, that are so much thinner & have damage warping by the zipper area) -awesome faded brown color: looks a notch above wearing just basic jeans, in my viewpoint; a bit more ‘dressy’ -perfect comfortable form fitting: I was so happy to put these jeans on & find them just perfect form fitting, so soft, so comfortable in design & fit. They give a nice stretch when sitting, conforming to the body, softly, rather than the 'pinch' of more rigid jeans. (Note: I'm 5'4", & not way overweight but right now with a bit of an older woman mid-section gut-tire, approx 150ish lbs, especially after a lot of winter time indoors with less exercise + some cortisol stress middle weight gain this year. The L - large size fit me perfectly, in every dimension, including the back bottom edge just touching the ground with no shoes on. You could certainly be a few inches up or down from my height, & find this size to fit). -front design ridge (down the front of each pant leg): adds a nice touch of style/ quality, to take the jeans up a notch, toward being more of ‘dress’ jeans, rather than backyard gardening jeans -wider leg bottoms: give a nice design for slimming your appearance, since they widen as they go down, assisting in the appearance of the waist area appearing thinner, for us aging women, who can use that beneficial little bit of gain. -no label by manufacturer sewn on outside: I love this. It's a plus to me. I appreciate the logo on the cardboard tag: “Style is eternal, be your own label.” Wow! I’m impressed even with the mind behind these jeans. I’ve always disliked clothing labels. This is magnificent. I could hug these designer people. -garment care: per tag: machine wash gentle or dry clean acceptable. NO tumble dry; which is fine with me, because jeans with 2% “elastine” or spandex, blended into 98% cotton have warped in the past in the dryer, giving ripple wave damage wrinkles all around the zipper. Air dry is the best care to avoid this damage; so the label gives perfect advice. (I’ve found with such garments, you can get away with initial, brief use of low heat, for the 1st 10 minutes only, as long as they are still damp; & then switch to no heat, & throwing a towel in the dryer with them to absorb water & assist drying; hang dry for a final stage. But don't forget them in the dryer. And again, the label says 'no tumble dry' so for your warranty & safest care, listen to the manufacturer, not me).

Really cute in person. The Mariela is slightly thicker than a jeggging but less than regular denim. Stretch is all the way up so there’s even some give in the waistband. Nice and long so I can wear my boots or platforms as I hoped. Don’t stretch out too much with extended wear. Based on the measurements I should’ve ordered a medium but I went with small to compensate for the amount of stretch. If I went up it definitely wouldn’t started being baggy in the waist after a while. For reference, I’m 5’3”/32c/135-137lbs depending on the time of month/small pooch in the front since I never say no to late night street tacos/slight bit of birthing hips that never bounced back. For Gap/Old Navy and Hollister/AF denim and jeggings I get away with 6/28 for high rise and ultra high rise denim if that helps. Mid rise and low rise I’m a 4/27. Sweats and leggings of any brand I’m a womens small. I like all styles of bottoms but the FIT I prefer to wear is tighter in the hips and upper thighs then slightly relaxed the rest of the leg. Obviously, flares I give more grace to width from the knee down.

I ordered a Medium based on the size chart. I am 5'8", 140 lbs, I typically wear a small or medium, generally size 6 fits but depending of the brand I can sometimes fit a 4 or 8. My measurements placed me directly in between a small and a medium on this size chart (which happens to me ALL THE TIME), so I ordered medium with comfort in mind. I feel like the medium fit good and the small might have been too tight. They are stretchy (but not too stretchy) and might shrink some after being washed, so I think they'll be perfect after that. For pants like this I do prefer them a little bit more snug around the booty 🍑, but I still think they provided a decent fit. I'll back after the first wash.I like that the blue color isn't a super dark navy, it's more of a medium blue. They have REAL front and back pockets (🌟 win-win) which is always very important! They are very soft but still a thicker denim material. Another review said that they weren't high waisted, but I disagree!! These came up above my belly button. The 27" inseam is meant to be cropped, and for me they are at the perfect length. I can see how they would be full length on the shorter ladies. Depending on your style and the occasion, these could be worn out to dinner (but not a fancy dinner) or out on a semi-casual outing. They're perfect for looking nice and put together but still feeling comfortable!

I’m an admitted Luvamia junkie, so when I saw these striped jeans from them I knew I had to have them. My experience with Luvamia is that their clothes often are a little tight to begin with but then their denim stretches while wearing it so you can normally order your usual size. However, on “specialty items” liked these striped jeans, I always size up because their things that aren’t plain denim tend to run a little smaller. In my photos I am wearing a large on my 5’5” 169# body. You can see that the waist is still a little tight in these photos but once I wore them for a little bit it loosened up and wasn’t showing my rolls as much. When I wash Luvamia, I just wash it in my regular laundry. It usually tightens back up a little bit they are never as tight as they were when they were brand new. These are so adorable I’m in love with them. Though they are striped, they aren’t clown-like and feel very beach and fun. Once again, Luvamia didn’t disappoint.

My first review but I feel like I used these reviews a ton to get my right size. So I want to share mine stats to help others! Overall I’m glad I stuck with my normal size 4. If you have bigger thighs may want to size up otherwise stick to normal size I hope this helps!

I really love Luvamia so I was excited to see these pink pants come out just in time for Valentine’s Day! Like most Luvamia bottoms, these fit true to size - I am wearing a medium on my 5’5” 161# body. The material on these isn’t the typical Luvamia denim - they are somewhere between a denim and a chino. Like maybe a soft, lightweight denim. My only complaint - and this is true with a lot of Luvamia - is that there’s a little extra material at the flap at the top of the zipper. I tried to show this in my photos where I am wearing the heart sweater. It’s just below the button at the waist and it kind of makes it look like the pants are too tight when really it’s just extra material. I haven’t tried to iron it more flat. That might help temporarily but I think when you keep sitting/standing/moving it will just pop out again. Outside of that minor flaw, I do really love these pants. The pink is Cherry and just what I expected based on the photo in the ad. The length is really nice - I felt comfortable wearing these out even though it’s winter here and very chilly outside. The pockets are flattering on the front and are functional as well. The legs have just a bit of a flare on them, making the thighs look a little slimmer. Like all Luvamia, these pants do relax a bit after wearing, so if you are really between sizes, you can size down knowing that they may be a little tight when you put them on but they will loosen up. It’s been my experience that even after washing and drying, Luvamia never gets as tight as it was when you first get them. These are definitely great for the price and if you can get them on sale, even better.

This pair of wide leg, cropped jeans are well made. The fabric content is 75% cotton, 23% polyester, and 2% elastane. They are between medium and thin for thickness, so they should be comfortable going into summer. The material is soft and has a good amount of stretch in it for extra comfort. They have 5 pockets; 2 front pockets, a small watch-pocket in the upper part of one of the front ones, and 2 back pockets. The front pockets are a little small, but still functional for a chap-stick, tissue, or keys. The waist has belt loops for those who like to or need to wear a belt. The button/zipper fastenings work smoothly without stiffness or snags. I ordered them in a size XL, which is my regular size for jeans. They fit very comfortably around my waist and there is no pinching thanks to the stretch in the fabric. I'm on the short side, so they are closer to being a normal length for me, than looking cropped. I'm fine with the length as often the problem for me is that jeans are too long instead! For reference: I'm 5' 4" tall, 33-ish waist, 44" hips, and 182 lbs. with an apple shaped midsection. These are good quality jeans and I definitely will recommend them. They're so comfy that it makes it easy to wear them all day. There are 4 different shades of blue available, along with black, white, and hot pink. I really like the Cobalt Blue shade that I chose. The current price (2-7-2026) of $35.99 is a fair value for them, in my opinion. Currently, there is a coupon available to reduce the price and make them a good value.

My tween daughter is a fashionista. Skinny jeans are coming back in style for the kids... (Heck, I've never stopped wearing them!) So when I saw this pair of LOVER BRAND FASHION Women's Jeans, I knew she would want to try them. I ordered them in the (Medium, Black) version and they fit her great. For reference she is 5'4ish, 135ish pounds and wears a juniors size medium from a big box store. Here's what we know about these jeans: •Excellent quality garment, free of defects •Made of medium thick, very stretchy denim-like material •Wrinkle resistant material that is easy wear right out of the dryer •High waisted cut is figure flattering •Buttons and zipper work correctly •Nice true black color •Versatile-- can be styled a variety of different ways •A fantastic value for the money Overall, this pair of high waisted jeans is fabulous! They are definitely worth a spot in your wardrobe, especially at a little less than $23! They wash up great, are comfortable, and easy to throw on. MOM & TWEEN APPROVED! HIGHLY RECOMMEND!!!

These LOVER BRAND FASHION high waisted skinny jeans are really well made with sturdy, thick denim that feels high quality and durable. The dark wash looks sleek and versatile for casual wear. They look fantastic in the product photos (and probably on most people!), but unfortunately, they didn't fit me quite right, I gained a bit more weight this year than I realized, so they were a tad too snug. No fault of the jeans though; I just need to go up a size next time or lose the weight which is my plan. Thankfully, my home gym is getting set up again, and these will be my motivation pair, the first jeans I slip into once I'm back in shape! One thing I can on is I've always had a hard time finding jeans that were long enough, I'm 5'7" with most my height in the legs so pants are usually to short, these are perfect in length. Highly recommend for anyone looking for sturdy, flattering skinny jeans. Just double-check the sizing chart! 5 stars, because the product itself is excellent; the fit issue is on me!

These didn't work for my figure. I just didn't find the fit to be flattering. For a data point, I'm 5'7", 175 pounds, and normally wear a size large or 12/14. I carry my weight in my hips and thighs. These are not jeans material, or cut. First off, they are pull pn style with a very elastic waist on the back. Secondly, im too tall for these. I normally wear low on my hips to allow for some extra length in pants, but the cut of these makes them awkward looking when I do. And you can see in the pics, even when worn low they are short on me. If you are petite, I think these are perfect. Anything over 5'7" will probably struggle. Sizing up isnt really the answer, as the elastic back just creates a saggy look from behind, and there seems to be a slight flare shape cut into the pants thay makes them look off when not worn at the waist.

I think they look cheap but I do like that they are not see through. I'm 5'4 and have a 24.5 inch waist. They fit well height wise but a bit big everywhere else. My teenager liked them so I gave them to her. They are comfortable but I think a bit overpriced.

Take me back to my mid-90's flares, but with style! I absolutely love these jeans! There are no zippers or buttons, just elastic in the rear and a flat front. Side pockets are perfect, deep enough, and back pockets exist. I love the wide hemmed pant legs - so cute. For me, at 5'4'' they are slightly long, so far I've had to wear them only with heeled boots (low profile sneakers have them dragging on the ground). I find them super comfortable as well as stylish. I normally wear a size 12/14 jeans and these XL fit me perfectly! They are medium weight fabric, no stretch except in the waistband. Seem to be good quality and worth the price. I want to wear them every day!

Hybrid & Company Women's Butt Lift Stretch Denim Jeans After some recent health issues, I lost weight suddenly. And with that, I adopted a pancake butt. It's been hard finding pants that fit in the rear, leg AND waist. I'm 5'6 about 115, and the 1 Long were about 4-5 inches too long. So I'll have to cut and hem them. But, it's nice to wear something that doesn't sag. The material is a decent thickness, zipper seems legit, pockets are fair size for jeans. Unsure about shrinking.

Hybrid & Company Women's Butt Lift Stretch Denim Jeans I ordered a pair of 5S red pants and they fit absolutely perfectly, and they are so comfortable— for that review I give 5 stars… so I really wanted a white pair, but they only make the short lengths for a few colors. Since most brands don’t vary the other measurements in their sizes—only the length—I ordered a regular length size 5 in white. I was so disappointed I am apple shaped and everything I gain goes straight to my upper body and none of it goes to my hips or legs. Currently I fit in the healthy weight range for my BMI, but I know I look better when I was 10 pounds thinner. After a few years of fighting it I’m trying to just accept it and buy new clothes. here are my horrifying measurements— bust 38, waist 31 (ok probably 32 at night) hips 34– no joke— my hips are currently barely 3 inches wider than my fat waist. So when I was 10 pounds thinner my measurements were 35, 28, 34– so I’m the worst apple ever where nothing ever goes to my hips or butt. If jeans are low rise I can wear size 2, but high rise jeans at this weight I have to wear 6-8 or I can’t breath. Problem is that when I get those sizes they billow with extra fabric on my hips and butt and make me look awful. That’s why I was so excited the red 5S fit so well. But the white regular length size pinched my middle completely even though they were still midrise. So I returned them and ordered size 7 and they still pinched but not as bad as the 5, only they were ridiculously long and more baggy in the hip than I like. Maybe my red pair of size 5S is a fluke. I don’t know cuz I don’t need or want the other colors they sell in that size. If the company reads these, please make more colors (tons of women love white jeans!) of the short size…assuming they’re the same measurements as my perfect stretchy red pair shown in pics. My rating of 3 stars is due to not being able to get an awesome fit in a different color. Sorry I didn’t do all the measurements for the white pairs before I sent back.

Problem is that when I get those sizes they billow with extra fabric on my hips and butt and make me look awful. That’s why I was so excited the red 5S fit so well. But the white regular length size pinched my middle completely even though they were still midrise. So I returned them and ordered size 7 and they still pinched but not as bad as the 5, only they were ridiculously long and more baggy in the hip than I like. Maybe my red pair of size 5S is a fluke. I don’t know cuz I don’t need or want the other colors they sell in that size. If the company reads these, please make more colors (tons of women love white jeans!) of the short size…assuming they’re the same measurements as my perfect stretchy red pair shown in pics. My rating of 3 stars is due to not being able to get an awesome fit in a different color. Sorry I didn’t do all the measurements for the white pairs before I sent back.

Hybrid & Company Women's Butt Lift Stretch Denim Jeans Fit great and look awesome. I weigh 110 and I'm 5'4" tall. I have no butt. These pants give my flat butt a nice lift. They are very soft. The purple is a beautiful color. I have washed them multiple times in cold water and dried them on normal dry. They are still like new.

Hybrid & Company Women's Butt Lift Stretch Denim Jeans I’m so excited to have found somewhere that sells mid-rise skinny jeans. It’s been so hard to find jeans that fit in the era of high-rise (I’m 5’2 with a short torso, and all the jeans in stores go up to my chest or armpits). I love the fit of these jeans, and the color is so vivid! I ordered true to size, and while the waist sits where it should and I can slip them on with no problem, the general style is more skintight than I expected. I could probably stick with this size and see if the jeans stretch out the longer you wear them, but I’m going to order a size up as well to see if it fits the same without being skintight. Update: I ordered the mint-green ones in a size up, and I also ordered the black ones one size up (both 9, while my original order was 7). The different colors definitely fit differently! The black size 9s fit perfectly, so I’m keeping them for sure. The green size 9s actually ended up being too big. The legs fit almost as tight as the 7s, but the crotch was noticeably loose. I’m returning the 9 and keeping the original green size 7 I ordered. So if you’re thinking of ordering different colors, I’d suggest ordering two different sizes and returning whichever doesn’t fit. New pictures are attached with size 9’s labeled. I’m very happy with these jeans now that I’ve found the correct size of each!

Sidefeel Women's Wide Leg Jeans High Waisted Stretchy Capri Pants My daughter is normally a size 4 at 117 lbs and 5'5, and these size 4 fit her tts. They fit great in the waist, and they have a stretchy fit. We both loved the tapered look on her. The material is nice and durable, and we loved the darker color. Highly recommend!

Sidefeel Women's Wide Leg Jeans High Waisted Stretchy Capri Pants I was pleasantly surprised by these pants. The inseam says 20 inches. Being that I'm only 4'11 I was hoping that they would fit more like a wide leg crop instead of a capris. They fit just as I was hoping!! I'm about 140lbs and usually wear size 10 and the sizing was spot on. The material seems to be nice quality with a little stretch! I would recommend and plan to order another color.

Amazon Essentials Women's Ultra Soft Mid-Rise Wide Leg Jeans Ok the important thing first; I'm 5'7", 138lbs and usually wear a comfortable "29", or 6-8, in jeans. I'm a pretty solid medium, sometimes a large, never a small. I got a size 8 in these jeans and they're definitely too big. The size chart has the 8 at a 29.5-30.5 and the 6 is 28.5-29.5, so i think me getting an 8 is my bad lol. You'll see in the pics that I added a belt & it definitely helps? But they're still super big. I do think if you're in between sizes, it's safe to size down in these!Moving on, they're DEFINITELY baggie and super soft. If you get the right size I think they'd be so cute for a baggie, comfy, casual look. The only thing I don't like is how LONG they are though, and I'm decently tall. But again, sizing down and wearing them a bit above the midrise length they're advertised as may fix that too.The price is also a plus. A nice, comfy pair of stylish baggie jeans for under 30 bucks is a great deal i think! I'll probably come back & buy the size 6 soon because they are so comfortable and affordable.ps- I didnt realize my mirror was so dirty please don't judge me lol. Have a great rest of your day!

Aeropostale Women's Low Rise Baggy Wide Leg Jean Why did you pick this product vs others?: Amazing jeans, amazing fit, 100% cotton. I am SO HAPPY WITH THESE!!! (I am 5’2” & 130lb, size 4S fit like a glove) Fit: Perfect Material quality: Fantastic

Lee Womens Ultra Lux Comfort Any Wear Wide Leg Pant Okay, listen up fellow Amazonians: I am 6'2" and 210 lbs, and these pants are so close to being long enough it’s almost disrespectful. Like… they ALMOST understood the assignment. Stupid close. Close enough that I’m willing to forgive them because everything else about them is fantastic. They’re thick but breathable (in a good, quality-fabric way). They’re wrinkle resistant, which is great because ironing is a chore I absolutely refuse to participate in. They have the perfect wide leg that makes it look like I’m purposefully elegant instead of floundering to both have pants long enough that are also stylish. Thy are pretty sturdy and not very stretchy so buy your actual size. And the navy color? Chef’s kiss. Honestly, if Lee ever decides to add two more inches of length, I will personally write them a thank-you letter and bake them cookies. Until then, these are the best “not flood pants” I’ve ever found. Would buy again. Already did. In black. Same review but you know, I'd say black instead of navy. Duh.

No Nonsense Women's Knit Denim Straight Leg Legging Pull-on Pants These knit denim straight leg leggings are very comfortable and fit true to size. The material feels soft, stretchy, and great for everyday wear. I’m 5'3", and they are a bit long for me, but that’s just a personal fit preference. The overall sizing is accurate, and the pants are flattering and comfortable. Overall, very happy with this purchase.

Lee Womens Ultra Lux Comfort Any Wear Wide Leg Pant I got these to wear to work, I work at the post office and so I got the navy blue color. Based off of the reviews I read about shrinking after washing, I went on ahead and bought the long and I am so glad I did. I am 5 foot 4 and I normally wear a regular length or a short. I washed and dried these pants and they shrunk about 4 inches. Other than that they are fantastic they have held up really well and I love how thick the fabric is but they are so soft and comfortable it feels like I'm wearing pajamas almost. I am including pictures of the pair that I washed and dried next to the ones that haven't been washed it so you can see the length difference. I don't know how they would do if they weren't put in a dryer if they would shrink or not, but I did put mine in the dryer and they are the perfect length for me now

I love love love these. I want more. I’m 5’8 156lbs order size medium. I absolutely love these ANRABESS wide leg jeans! The high-waisted design is very flattering and gives a nice silhouette without feeling tight or restrictive. The drawstring waist is a great feature because it lets you adjust the fit easily, which makes them super comfortable for all-day wear. The denim is soft and lightweight compared to traditional jeans, so they feel more like casual lounge pants while still looking stylish. The wide leg cut is trendy and relaxed, making them perfect to pair with sneakers, sandals, or even ankle boots. I’ve worn them running errands, traveling, and even to casual outings, and they always look put together. Another big plus is the pockets—they’re actually functional and deep enough to hold my phone and keys. If you’re looking for jeans that combine comfort with a modern, relaxed style, these are a great choice. I’ll definitely be buying another pair in a different wash! 👖✨

user_comment: Aeropostale Women's Low Rise Flare Jean Flattering and true to size. 5’1,121lb, 24waist. Color like picture. Also I like that the waist fits snug and that there is a bit of stretch in the thigh area. They don’t feel tight or restricting.

Aeropostale Women's Low Rise Flare Jean Flattering and true to size. 5’1,121lb, 24waist. Color like picture. Also I like that the waist fits snug and that there is a bit of stretch in the thigh area. They don’t feel tight or restricting.

Lee Womens Ultra Lux Comfort Any Wear Wide Leg Pant I originally ordered this Lee Lux Comforte in the Navy in size 6. Amazon insisted 6 was the size for me. According to the size chart, it was close. I’m 5‘6“ tall. I weigh 135 to 140, waist is 29. hips are 38. I am also a little bit long-waisted. The waist of the 6 was OK. It was firm with not much give. However, the 6 was quite snug through the hips and thighs, and it made the pockets gap. I opted to return the 6 because these will primarily be for work and they were just slightly too small everywhere. I ordered the same slacks in size 8. The waist is still OK in the 8 a little bigger and a little looser, but I’ll still be able to wear them after Thanksgiving dinner. There is more room in the hips, but they still hug the top of the hip nicely before they fall to the wide leg. The leg in the 8 is loose at on my thighs, but the 6 was tight on my thighs. They are a bit longer. Unfortunately, they still make a funny wrinkle at the groin. This happens to me sometimes because I am slightly long-waisted. Because these are on sale for $16 and I really need navy slacks, I’m going to keep the 8. For anyone with a waist over 29, or hips more than 36, or generous thighs, or a long-waist, I think the 8 is a better choice. The fabric is not thin. It is a nice weight and it will be good for work. It might be warm on hot days.

Aeropostale Women's Low Rise Flare Jean These are cute! A little short on me but I’m on the taller side (5’8). They’re super comfy and the size 2 fits true to size

Amazon Essentials Women's Ultra Soft Mid-Rise Wide Leg Jeans Update: I dropped a star because these collect lint like nobody’s business and I have to spend almost 10 minutes de-linting whenever I wear them. The second thing is that the back pocket is too skinny. I put a Mac mini in my back pocket at work and these are the first jeans it hasn’t fit in. Original review: I wouldn’t necessarily say these jeans are “soft”; it’s more that they are thin (and a little stretchy). They aren’t the thick, rough work pants some jeans feel like. I’m 5’6 and between a size 16 and 18. These size 16 jeans fit very comfortably in the waist and tummy, snug around the thighs, and then long enough to need hemmed at the bottom. They are very wide at the bottom, almost giving bell bottoms. Other than that, the fit is pretty typical to size 16 jeans I own from other brands. Everything is double stitched and finished off properly with backstitching. I don’t expect these jeans to fall apart at all.

Aeropostale Women's Low Rise Baggy Wide Leg Jean Took a chance and I love these. Fits on the hip and loose everywhere else as it should be! This is pretty relaxed, so if you like a snugger fit in the waist, then size down. I’m 5’2” and 125lbs and got a 27 short (US 4 Short), but could probably do a 2 short to be honest (historically I do recall Aeropostale running a little larger than other brands). I love the Sassafras color wash, it’s not too dark, but a classic deeper indigo, exactly what I hoped for. The tag does say 100% cotton, but there is stretch to the fabric. It’s not a stiff denim, but not a very thin one either, so it’s just right in my opinion.

Aeropostale Women's Low Rise Baggy Wide Leg Jean I tried both the 0 and 00 sizes but neither one worked for me. I’m 5’4” and 112lbs. My waist is 24 inches. The 0 was way too long and too loose in the waist. I tried the 00, which was perfect in length but was still too loose in the waist, especially since the butt was more form-fitting in the smaller size. When I sat in the 00, the waistband rode up and gapped a lot in the back. I loved the color, style, and material of these jeans so I was very bummed I could not find a size that fits.

Amazon Essentials Women's Ultra Soft Mid-Rise Wide Leg Jeans These jeans are definitely on the simple side — not super trendy, but if you’re looking for a basic wide-leg jean at a low price, they do the job. The legs are very wide, and the width stays the same from top to bottom, which gives them a boxy look. I’m 5'5", and they’re about 2–3 inches too long on me, so I’ll need to hem them or they’ll drag on the ground. The denim is a thicker, non-stretch material that feels decent quality for the price — not stiff, but not soft either. They’re not going to be my everyday go-to jeans, but they’ll work fine for casual wear or when I want something comfy and roomy. Overall, solid for the cost, just don’t expect a high-fashion fit.

Levi's Womens 314 Shaping Straight Jeans (Also Available in Plus) I'm 5'5 and about 210. 45-42-51 are my measurements. I got a size 37 plus and I'm normally I'm an 18/20 plus. These come up just above my belly button and are a bit loose honestly, but I expected that since the measurements for for these says they're 43". The band/seams dont have a lot of stretch but the denim does so for my bigger girlies I'd focus on your waist measurements and inseams more than anything else, I tried to show that in one of the pictures. As far as measurements go, these jeans seem to be true to size. I could have gone a size smaller for a tighter fit, but these are comfortable how they are now so I'll just wear them with a belt. The fabric is soft but seems durable enough, it definitely don't feel like rough denim. It's comfortable and I absolutely amazing not going to kind wearing them all day. The color is also accurate, but as you can see in the pictures, it really does depend on lighting. The pictures with my legs showing were taken in sunlight. These are my second pair of Levi's and I expect them to last a long time so I'd say they're worth the price as well.

EVALESS Women's High Waisted Wide Leg Jeans These jeans are cute and quite comfortable because of the slight stretch to the denim. And they're not too stretchy to the point that they feel stretched out by the end of the day. The style is cute, I love the pockets on the front and the seams above the back pockets, this adds a nice stylish touch. My one qualm with these jeans is that they are not full length as pictured in the listing. I am just under 5'6" and my legs are proportionate to my torso. I do not normally need to wear 'long' or 'tall' sized jeans. These could've definitely been longer. The cuff is sewn, so if I want to uncuff them to make them longer, I'll have to rip open the stitching, which isn't a huge stitch, but the pants could've just been a bit longer. I like the color and the fit overall. Again, they are comfortable too.

Riders Womens Pull on Waist Smoother Boot Cut Jean This is my first time ordering jeans online because I can never seem to find ones that fit me even at the store. Riders Womens Pull on Waist Smoother Boot Cut Jean I am in the middle of my weight loss journey and needed something while I am in between sizes. Jessica Simpson Womens Adored Curvy High Rise Skinny Jean Favorite Jeans & affordable! First time ordering jeans online & after I’ve lost 40+ lbs, at that! Loves: I am 5’8” and these go below my ankle, so I can cuff them at my ankle, if I wanted & wear cute bootie shoes. I have SKINNY legs and they are very fitting & flattering! Material, zipper, & button all feel very durable. All 4 pockets are REAL! Also, the price is not bad at all compared to other pairs of jeans on here with much thinner material. Possible inconveniences: Not a lot of stretch in the waist area, but it works. I’m 168-170 lbs and a size 31 was suggested . I got a size 30 because all other stretch jeans I own, usually loosen up throughout the day; idk if these will, honestly. The material is on the thicker side. If my thighs and calves weren’t skinny, these would not fit, so they are SKINNY. I could have felt very comfortable in a 31, but I am expecting to lose additional weight, so these will do, but whatever size is suggested to you, these are true to size in the waist. BUY THEM!

EVALESS Women's High Waisted Baggy Boyfriend Barrel Jeans I haven't worn jeans in ten years. I had these in my cart for months and rly happy I decided to finally get them! They fit true to size. For reference, I’m 5’6”, 175lbs. 32 waist, 45 hips. Usually I’m a size 12/14, but I ordered these in 16 for a looser fit and glad I did! The front pleats are well placed for a flattering fit, especially if you’re curvier. The material is fairly thick, but also stretchy. Surprisingly comfortable! They hold up well in the wash & don’t shrink when dried. The rise hits at just the right spot — although the waist is a little baggy on me, so I have to wear a belt. Very pleased with the quality. I never spend more than $20 on pants, but these are worth the money for sure. 10/10 recommend. If you’re questioning getting them, do itttt:)

EVALESS Women's High Waisted Baggy Boyfriend Barrel Jeans I'm 5'5, curvy fit, 135ish. Not long legged. I normally wear a 27/6 in jeans ... I sized up to an 8 in this. Nothing was tight but I didn't live the look. I've seen barrell jeans (not tried on) and mom jeans--each a baggy look. I do own the Free People knit jumper and a FP denim oversized set of bibs and like both. Each of those are an obvious oversize. These jeans, on the other hand, just were not baggy looking as the model shows. I read many reviews and hoped they'd work but they literally look like my mom wore in the mid 80s and hers fot about like this. Just not a cute look, imo, if they are not baggy enough. Go big, or go home. I still wear skinny jeans. I have wode keg jeans. I have cropped flares. I cannot wear straight leg or boyfriend style bc I'm a curvy fot (the waist always sticks out too badly). Providing this info, as I think it is all relevant. Last, I'm almost 50. This look might seem cuter on a gal my size but in her 20s. Or with no curve and less boots. Material was fine; blue color a tad off, seemed almost frosted-ish.

user_comment: These jeans are well sewn from a heavy weight cotton/polyester mix denim with a nice stretch. I have washed and dried them several times and they get softer each time. user_comment: First impression was that these pants felt low quality upon first touch. Despite that, they were actually comfortable and stretchy. I was able to squat, bend my legs, and do some lunges comfortably without the fabric squeezing my legs or waist. I also had extra room on the waist line in case I over eat at a buffet. The bottom of the legs are truly wide-legged, but the white stitching at the bottom isn’t clean which gives it a low-quality look along with the strong “fake jeans” blue. I haven’t washed it yet, but if I noticed significant shrinkage, then I’ll update my review. For context I’m 5’1 and the pants were a tad bit too long for me. If I could get it tailored, I’d remove like 3 to 4 inches to touch my ankles.

These jeans feel very sturdy, especially for the price. The front pockets are quite deep -- not as deep as pockets in men's jeans, but much deeper than I'm used to in women's jeans. You can absolutely fit a smartphone in these front pockets vertically, with room to spare. I haven't washed these yet, but I'm definitely going to wash these in cold water only with black and blue clothes. This indigo is *intense.* Overall, a great value. Because this listing didn't seem to include measurements, here are the measurements for the size small: * The inseam is 28½" (73.5 cm), which seems short, except these are baggy and kind of hang low. * The front rise is 11½" (29 cm). On my short body, that meant the waistline is almost at my ribcage. * The back rise was a little harder to measure accurately, but I got about 16" (41 cm). * The leg opening is about 26" (66 cm). * The front pockets are 9¾" (25 cm) deep on the inside fold and almost 7¼" (18 cm) wide on the bottom hem. I'm about 5'1" (154 cm). In the photos, I'm wearing boots with 3" heels, and the jeans cover most of my feet. I couldn't wear regular sneakers without these dragging on the ground.

The arm openings aren't too baggy either which is such a issue with these types of shirts but not this one!! I buttoned it up all the way and tucked it in the front and I LOVE it with jeans!!! It's bright and happy and fun and I'm going to wear it all year and just add a jacket! You can dress it up if you wanted but I think it's definitely more of a casual shirt.

user_comment: LuminBlaze Strech Low Rise Jeans for Woman Elastic Vintage Flare Denim Jeans i’m 5'1", 138 lbs, athletic build with narrow hips, thicker thighs, and an average booty. i ordered my usual size 4 in the dark blue. these fit true to size and are super cute. they have a decent amount of stretch, which had me debating between sizing down to a 2 or sticking with a 4. i’m glad i stayed with the 4. they’re comfortable, not tight, and still really flattering. i’m short with a shorter torso, so these don’t feel super low rise on me. that’s more my body shape than the jeans themselves. length-wise, they’re not overly long, which i love, but if you’re tall, they may hit a little short. overall, i love the fit and the look and am really pleased with these. great everyday flares with stretch in the right places.

I wear size 29/medium pants and the medium fit as expected. The waist is high, reaching my navel. They are not tight in the waist. They are skinny/tight and stretchy. I am 5’7” and they are plenty long. They do bunch a little in the legs, but not too bad. I love the color.

Quality control needed. The pair of jeans I received, are unwearable. The button was hanging off and as soon as I put it through the hole to close the jeans it was completely off. Okay, these are stretchy jeans, think more jeggings than jeans. They do fit true to size. I am 5'6", 132 lbs, and I ordered a small. user_comment: Flamingals are my new favorite jeans. I have ordered 3 pairs so far, & have loved all of them! The first 2 I ordered a large & really like the relaxed fit, but they do stretch out after wearing them for awhile. For this pair I decided to try a medium & although the waist is a bit tight, I’m hoping they will stretch out like the others. Other than that, I love the way they fit & the look of the undone hem at the bottom. They are a durable denim & feel like good quality. In addition, the price is right! In other brands of the same quality, you can end up paying $100 or more. For reference I am 5’3”, 140lbs.

My stats: 5' tall, 114 pounds, 29 1/2 inch waist, 36 inch hips, 22 inch inseam; 60 years old, carry my weight in my abdomen and thighs. I purchased size small/short. I have to fold up the bottoms a couple of inches, which I usually have to do, so not a problem there. While they are comfortable and I wear them a lot, I am not totally satisfied with them and would try another brand/style rather than purchasing them again. On the plus side, they are very stretchy yet are heavy enough to look like jeans. The back pockets are nice. I washed them in cold water and dried on medium heat and they did not shrink on me. What I don't like: Mainly the front pockets, which are deep and they allow too much fabric in front of them, such that the abdomen area looks poofy and the thighs look wrinkly. I was hoping for something a little more flattering. While I do not wear nor want to wear them with a shirt tucked in, I am attaching photos of the pants with my shirt tucked in so you can see the waistline, which also feels a little too thick for my tastes. An additional negative: It gives me a slight camel toe, of which I am constantly aware, though it's not so bad that I won't wear them. I am also attaching a photo of me in a pair of pocketless exercise pants so you can see what I look like without pockets poofing out.

I am 5'7" and 164lbs with a 31" waist. I normally wear between a size M and L. Based on the size chart I was between a size L and XL. I got this in a L and it fit as expected, I also got a pair in XL and they were too big. I might have been able to size down to a M because these are very stretchy. I also got both a 31" and 29" pair and the 31" inseam was better for my height. The light pair pictures are the 31", L and the dark paid are the 29", XL.These are super comfy and fit well. They do have a big of an odor to them and that lingered after washing. I imagine after a couple more washes it will be gone. They did not shrink after washing.These have nice big hand pockets and back pockets. They are super comfy. I am not a huge fan of the crossover waistband because I feel like it doesn't suck me in as well and I have issues with it rolling over in the middle. However, I know a lot of people really like that. I have similar pair of jeans to these from Blue Rose that have a straight across waistband and I like those better.The color of these was a bit lighter than expected and I didn't find it that flattering, but again that is personal preference. The XL I got was in the dark wash and I liked that a lot better.These are great quality and super comfy and I would recommend.

Okay, lots to say about these pants. I wasn’t sure if I should give them 3 stars or 4. If it’s based on my personal opinion of them for MY body, I’d say 2 or 3 stars. But in terms of overall quality and other factors I’ll mention below, 4 stars is much more accurate. First, the actual quality of the material is nice, thick, and stretchy. They are extremely comfy, even so in the crotch area which usually is bothersome for me with rigid denim. These are not rigid denim at all. They are more of spandex / jegging material but decent thickness. I like that they’re actually high rise, they sit nicely right at my waist line. Other pants in similar style usually run about an inch too short on the rise, causing discomfort and Unflattering appearance at the waistline. These are a true high rise, which I love. The belt is removable and matches the jeans perfectly. Can be used to functional purposes of tightening the waistline or just as a cosmetic additive. These pants have real pockets on the front and back which is wonderful. In terms of the “jogger” style at the ankle: aka the elastic cuff at the ankle part, I’m not a big fan. The leg opening is still quite wide, the ankle part just hangs off my leg which Is odd. Additionally, the inseam is far too short for me. I’m 5’9 with longer legs and the inseam is even beyond ankle length for me, these are actually capri length which is not flattering on me especially with the wide elastic cuff at the bottom. I will say, they did look better with a high top chuck than with just socks on, but I probably won’t ever wear these. They are just WAY too short. If you are super short these may be a good fit for you. But I’d still be mindful of the wide elastic cuff and how it hangs off your ankle / calf. By the way, I am 5’9 and 150 pounds and typically wear a size 8. I ordered a size 8 and other than the length they are very true to size and fit my hips/butt/ waist perfectly. They are fitted at the hip / butt / waist, hugging the hips, then relax down the leg to a straight leg all the way to the elastic cuff. As you can see in these photos, they aren’t super flattering on me. Even if I sized up to a 10 I don’t think the length would work for me. Overall, the actual quality of the material, craftsmanship, and stitching is definitely 4 stars; good quality. If these were on the right person… they could be very cute! Definitely for short girls! Hope this helps!

I'm 5'4" 134lbs, 27" waist and got a size small. My body type is curvy and overall they're very cute but the rear is a little gappy when I sit so I prefer them with a belt. Still, I only wear these doing something where walking is the maximum activity required.

I love the stretch and comfort of these jeans. They’re form fitting but the material is soft and extremely flexible. They’re the jeggings with the 70s flare/bell bottom look. The material is thin however, so even though they have a lot of stretch, I can imagine they can easily wear down after so many machine washes. It sits high on the waist which can be firm flattering for curvier figures. I like that it has pockets that have a good amount of depth on both the sides and back. I ordered a size XS as I’m 5 ft 108lbs. It fits more like a small and the hem runs past my feet so I’ll likely have to tailor them or wear higher heels. $41 dollars is a bit steep for the thinness of the material but wearing comfortable jeans with this amount of stretch could be a flex.

NYDJ Women's Marilyn Straight Denim Jeans I'm a high rise convert, these are perfection! I'm small but have the muscle tone of Wonder bread plus I'm a 62 Y O grandmother. But in these jeans? Not blessed in the back of the house and these make the most of what I've got by being nice and snug without flattening what I have, great back pocket placement. Nice and higher but not too high up. I'd just tried on $200 white jeans yesterday at the mall but they were too sheer not to mention too expensive for what they were offering me. I'd pay if I had to but... I can't believe the price of these and am ordering another because coffee guzzling me and white anything means I always order two if I love it lol.

For reference, I am 5'9", 133 lbs, normally size 4-6 in pants or size 27-28 when sized that way. This review is for the white jeans in size medium.I ordered the size medium, which is what I typically order in these types of pants when these size ranges are offered (and it's usually a bad sign when the jeans come in this size range rather than by waist size or the numerical sizing). The model in the advertised photos has a slim body, as opposed to a more curvy body, and the pants fit her well, seemingly. My body type is more like the model's, but these do not fit that way at all. They are too loose in the thighs. I do not like the way the look at all. I'd suggest, if you did decide to take a risk and order these, that you only do so if you have a thiccer body type. Or perhaps size down, but that is a risk that I cannot recommend, as I have not tried the smaller size. I just know that these are a NO for me.The material is also super thin. Which isn't a big deal for $25 pants, if only the fit were better (for me). They lack a size chart, which is one of my biggest issues with this, as you really are taking a risk when ordering these, especially with the leggings-type sizing.

user_comment: I absolutely love these jeans!! They're super cute, comfortable & have plenty of stretch. Which I need for my thick thighs lol. Very good jean quality & the distressed look is very nice. I ordered an XL pair & they fit perfectly. I'm 5'4 & weigh 210

5'7", 115 lobs, 28 w 30" inseam; bought small/long and they fit me perfectly. Love the color and slightly "worn" look - excellent fabric weight that is not at all transparent. Great stretch and very soft. Fabric is thicker like regular jeans but not heavy, and not thin like some jeggings. Opaque fabric for those of us who are modest, but cotton/poly/elastane blend is breathable and comfortable. NO SMELL which is super important to me. Labels says made in India and I've often gotten clothing made there that smells skunky. These only have typical new clothing smell and I always wash new clothes before wearing. Not sure what the front "tummy control" panel on the inside is all about. Seems rather pointless to me. Why only 4 stars rather than 5: The so-called tummy control is a stretchy nylon piece barely stitched into the inside of the jeans which actually shows through a little if you look closely. You can see by the photos that the hip area bells out a little bit. I remember back in the late 70s/early 80s slacks often had that feature in women's sizes and I called them saddle bags. I don't have much of a figure so this part "bags out" a tiny bit on me. There's also a bit of slack just below my rear end. Again, I don't have much of a butt so if you're a normal sized girl this may not be an issue. I'm hoping when I wash them this will smooth out a bit. Pockets in front and zipper placket are non-functional. Don't really see the point of that either. The back pockets are not very deep. Personally, I wouldn't feel comfortable putting a wallet or phone in them as they are too shallow and risk of falling out might be an issue. The dreaded toilet phone bath... Overall, they are very nice jeans, I'm happy with the purchase and would buy them again. In fact, I think I did buy a pair similar to this years ago on Amazon. Price for the quality is very good. Cleo the Grey is a very curious girl. And likes a selfie. Update: after washing, the jeans did not shrink at all. Still fit like a glove. Unfortunately the "saddlebag puckers" and under-bottom wrinkles did not go away. Leaving at 4 stars. Ladies with hips and butts will probably be better suited for this style.

These wide-leg cargo jeans are really comfortable and have a nice soft, stretchy denim that makes them easy to move in. I normally wear a size 2, but the smallest available was a 4, and I’m glad I went with that—it fits well around the waist, so I’d say they run a bit small. The length is a little short even on me (5’4”), and I don’t find the pockets or back especially flattering. That said, they’re still a cute, trendy pant for the price and great for casual wear.
reviewer_name_raw: Comfortable and Trendy, but Run Small

user_comment: Value Rating: 5/5 stars – At around $40, these are priced fairly for the quality. Cheaper versions of this Y2K cargo style often feel stiff or paper-thin, and these land in the sweet spot: thick, soft, and well-stitched without being heavy. Specifications: • Fabric: 68% cotton, 29% polyester, 2% viscose, 1% elastane • Closure: Button/zip fly • Rise: Mid-to-high waist • Fit: Wide-leg, baggy cargo style with utility pockets • Color: Black with fade wash Key Points: These fit exactly the way they should for a cargo jean—baggy, relaxed, and casual, while still flattering at the waist. I’m between 120–125 lbs, usually a 26 in jeans, and the size small fit perfectly at the waist. They are meant to be roomy through the legs, and they are, but not in a sloppy way. I wouldn’t mind if they shrink just a little after a wash, but I’m still happy with them as-is. The fabric is what surprised me most—it’s thick enough to feel durable, but soft enough that you don’t get that stiff, board-like denim feel. The fade is definitely noticeable in person, so expect that vintage wash look. To me, it reads intentional and trendy, not cheap or garish. Final Thoughts: If you’re looking for a baggy cargo style with comfort and structure, these are a win. No complaints on fit, fabric, or style.
reviewer_name_raw: Thick, Soft, and Surprisingly Good Quality

These jeggings are comfortable and well-fitting. They are true to size having ordered an L on my 5’5” 178# 32” inseam body. I ordered them in the darker blue color and am happy with the depth of the color. The waist is high which is nice to help hide a muffin top. The waistband is generous and doesn’t bind as I’m showing in one of my photos. I also demonstrate the stretch in the fabric where I pull away the fabric at my thigh. There are two functional pockets in the back and none in the front. The length comes just to my ankles and the legs are reasonably snug all the way down which I like. Moving, bending, and just living feels good in these. They don’t pull down in the back when you squat or bend over. All in all, I’m very happy with these and know they will be a continue to be my “go to” item all fall and winter.
reviewer_name_raw: Well-fitting jeans, good stretch, nice color

I ordered these Xineicy Women's Jeans Y2K Wide Leg Baggy Jeans High Waist Jeans for Women Loose Vintage 90s Boyfriend Denim Pants in a Size Small for my 10 year old daughter and knew by looking at the size chart that they would be on the large side. They remind me of the old JNCO jeans from my childhood... and my experimental fashionista kiddo has been wanting to try them for ages! These jeans are high quality denim. They are a little on the long side and are a smidge big in the waist, but she will grow into them in no time! Plus, we really like the dusty/rustic color of them. When paired with a cute long sleeve graphic tee, a belt and a pair of sneakers-- this outfit gets her tons of compliments! If you are looking for the big old early 2000's jeans-- these are the ones to buy!!!

I first purchased the brown version of this tank top, and have worn it a lot over the summer. It has a slight razorback cut (see photo) and is unforgiving in terms of the slightest bulge. For reference, I'm 5'4" tall and weigh 106 pounds and you can see the unflattering fit of the grey blue, which was narrower. Both were a Size XS, but the brown version both fit much better and complemented my warm coloring. Returned the grey blue. Like most clothing, you almost have to try it on before deciding...and even then there can be a lot of variation between sizes/colors. Overall, the top is okay, it washes up well (no shrinkage, but definitely dry flat), and the price point is great. The trick is finding a color that both fits and flatters.

Very nice heavy wide leg white pant. I would say they are a little on the extra wide side through the leg and that would be ok. The only problem with these pants is that they run really long. I am 5’7” and without a heal the cover my feet and then some. With a heal they are still long, but definitely do able. I would recommend these pants for someone over 5’8”.

I love denim jackets, this is my 4th and finally got black! My review includes the following: -usually take an XL (am busty) but noted that the jacket has a slim, tapered fit, took a chance and ordered XXL. -jacket arrived, tried it on and the fit is perfect. The taper and slim fit hugged my body, flattering and accentuates my figure. There is a bit of extra room to add additional layers for more warmth. -as with my other denim jackets, the “look” is vintage. Can be paired with skirts, dresses and pants. -fabric is 79% cotton, 20% recycled cotton and 1% elastane. Also fabric is mid-weight, with low stretchability. -can be washed, however, recommend cold and hang to dry. -the black color is intensely saturated with slightly dark, brass shank buttons, including the chest pocket flaps. -garment length: 22.5” for XXL What I really like: - medium weight denim, saturated black, stunning -jacket construction inside and out is impeccable. -perfect fit, right out of the Amazon package -brass shank buttons add a striking contrast, with an elegant look -great value for a high quality jacket Consideration’s: -your body type will tell you if you need to adjust the size ordered. I am 5’9” wt. 168 and busty. -this is a tapered, slim cut jacket that can highlight your figure. -lastly, you may want to add layers, to wear in cold weather. Negatives: None Would I recommend: Absolutely!

First glance - this looks like a standard denim jacket. First touch - feels like thick durable denim, does not feel like spandex or thin stretching material that looks like denim. Despite original concerns with sizing - I am usually a size L, but depending on brand I may take an XL. I have 5' 5.5", 165 pounds, 34DDD bust. This jacket not only was a perfect fit across the shoulders but also fully closed/buttoned at the bust line. Material is 99% cotton, 1% elastane. Simple silver buttons inscribed "essential basics". Two functional pockets on the chest, two functional pockets and the waist and two pockets in the jacket at each side. Bottom waist area has buttons to adjust to create a smaller waist; was not personally necessary in my situation but is an option. Fit surprisingly and exceptionally well! Very comfortable.

I really wanted to love this Dokotoo babydoll denim dress because the design itself is very cute and flattering in theory. I ordered a small and I’m 5’3”, about 120 lbs, and the fit is quite roomy; very oversized even for a babydoll style. Length hits a few inches above the knee. The fabric is airy and breathable, but noticeably stiff, which affects how the dress drapes. What adds to the bulk is the midway folded seam on the skirt (the bottom part of the dress) and on the sleeves; that structured fold combined with the stiff fabric makes the dress puff out instead of flowing. The waist seam is gathered as expected for a babydoll dress, but with a softer, more fluid fabric, that gathering paired with the hem detail would have looked much more flattering and drapey. Fabric labeling is also confusing: one tag mentions cotton/linen/denim, while the sewn-in label says 85% cotton and 15% polyester. There’s no stretch. Sewing quality is solid, with clean seams, a flattering V-neck, subtle puff sleeves, and a back tie to help keep the shoulders in place. Overall, it’s a pretty dress with good construction, but the stiffness of the fabric and the oversized cut make it look bulkier than expected. With a softer, flowier fabric, this would easily be a 5-star piece. My head says this is a 3-star dress, but my heart says it’s a 5-star dress.

These arrived today. I tried them on and left them on the rest of the day! They are so very comfortable and good looking as well. I highly recommend that you use the sizing chart and measure because I would normally buy size medium, but the chart recommended size large and was right on. I’m glad I didn’t buy them smaller. The fabric is soft and stretchy, but also smooth and crisp enough to look good. I can’t say how well they wash - yet. They are a great length for me - I’m 5 foot 5 and they come nicely over my shoes but don’t drag. One minor thing is that the dye is not consistent throughout the pants, there are stripes of white on the legs. I don’t know if that’s on purpose or not. The pockets are nice and large and easy to get hands in and out of because of the stretch, but my phone won’t be falling out like some other pants. I am overall very pleased with these pants.

Sweater came in very quickly and first thing I notice was how soft it was, and the red was a great color. I got this for my wife who is 5' 9" and ~160lbs so we got the large size per the chart. When she tried it on, it was good on sleeve length but very snug around the chest and belly. The instructions do say if you want a loose fit to size up, listen to them! My wife is still on the fence if she will wear it but for others it may be very nice. I will say that it's a quality but very thin knit sweater. Probably good in warmer weather, but please know that if stretched, it gets see through. I've added some photos to show what I mean.
