I'm not sure what compelled me to order these jeans but I'm so glad I did! I kind of love these! They're so weird and out of my comfort zone, but sometimes you just need to try something new. They are such a cute, different style. They hit at the ankle so they actually look cute with both flats and with heels. They are jean material but very stretchy and comfortable. The zipper on the side seems pretty sturdy. The white is a little bit see through, but the pockets in the back make that not a huge issue. They fit pretty well and are flattering on the butt. I'm 5'3", 125lbs and ordered a 4. They're great, but I honestly could have sized down to a size 2 and the waist would probably fit better (not quite sure that size chart is accurate because I certainly do NOT have a 25 inch waist). But I still like the way that the 4 looks. Can't wait to wear these and try to style them in different ways for the spring/summer! .....Plot twist, I wrote this whole review and then realized I was wearing them backwards the whole time. Tried them on the correct way (with the pockets in the front) and they're still cute. But I honestly prefer them the other way. So good news, these pants are apparently reversible

These were $49 when I ordered them and it looks like now they are almost half that with a coupon. For the cheaper price, I would maybe recommend these. For the $49 price, I definitely would not. For $27 you might be getting what you pay for. I am 5‘4“ and 165 pounds and ordered the size 14 and they’re a little loose, I probably could’ve gone for the 12 but didn’t want to be squeezed too much. They’re comfortable and fairly cute, but I don’t know if I just got a bad pair, but one of the legs is shorter than the other. So I don’t know if this is a quality control issue, but I would hope would just be a one off because I’ve gotten stuff from this brand before and liked it, and would return them if I could, to try a different pair. If it was the same length on each leg, then I would really like these pants. The little ruffle at the bottom is cute and the top with the little front pockets is comfy and looks nice. The zipper doesn’t quite zip all the way to the top, which is a little bit annoying. So again, if these have a coupon and you’re able to return them if you get a wonky pair like mine, I would probably recommend it. If these are full price, or you can return them, I would say pass it and find something else of better quality. This is not a good value for the full price. I only took off two stars since I would hope mine is just a one off mistake. :/

My dimensions are 5'3", 130 lbs, 28 - 29" waist and I ordered the size 6 in the black. I recommend reviewing the size chart and then also taking into account the materials composition: 72%Cotton, 26%Polyester, 2%Elastane. Usually, this blend tends to get loose during wear, so I find that I have to add waist cinching pins; I took a picture of what I currently use. The Elastane provides the stretch for all types of movement: sitting, walking, stretching or relaxing. The pants are not restrictive or form fitting except for the high waist which offers a flattering and slimming silhouette. The material is a medium weight and breathable due to the 72% cotton. The stitching and cut wash well as I have washed them twice which caused them to shrink a little bit. The color is as shown in the advertisement and pairs nicely with other blacks and greys. The zipper is hidden within the side-seam and does not add any weight or visual distraction to your waist. The ruffled hem pairs nicely with heels, tennis shoes or flip flops. I have shorter legs hence they are closer to my ankles then up towards my calf. I have been very pleased with the quality and durability of these jeans.

I got these jeans because I have been losing pregnancy weight. I have been in a size 6 for a few years, and as my waistbands have been getting a bit small I figured I would be back in a 4 soon, so I wanted to be prepared. I was surprised when these fit me as is, so I'm not sure if I have hit 4 sooner than I thought or if they are a little on the larger side. They might be a tiny bit tight, but not uncomfortably so, and less tight than my size 6 jeans are loose. The jeans do go just past my feet in some places. I'm 5'4" but I'm pretty sure my legs are a little shorter than many people my height. I usually buy jeans that are labeled as having a shorter inseam. The raw edge hem on mine looks just like battlements. I doubt that's the case with every pair and it's probably just coincidence for mine, but to me it looks odd. I don't mind a jagged edge, but it looks like it was done in a pattern on purpose, so it's kind of weird. I will either cut off some of the longer ends to keep the ragged look or I will hem it straight. I haven't decided which yet, but it needs to be a little shorter on me anyways. I'm not taking stars off for it because I knew it didn't have a shorter inseam to begin with. The fabric is on the comfortable side of average for jeans. It's not too stiff, but not thin and very stretchy either. The fabric is thick enough that it seems durable, and as long as the jagged edge doesn't get caught on anything, I expect them to last for a good decade. (I don't wear jeans terribly often though.) I wouldn't buy these if you ride a bicycle often unless you are rolling the leg up. A bike would eat these up. These are high waisted as listed, on the shorter side of high waisted if that makes sense. They aren't super high, but they are still high. I like them like that. These are currently listed at approximately $40, which seems about average for a decent pair of jeans, which these are. If you are looking for a pair of jeans and don't mind the rough edge, I think the price is reasonable.

These are the second pair of wide leg jeans from Trendy Queen that I have gotten. I absolutely LOVE the fit on my frame. I am 5'9", 150#, 29" waist and 38" hips. I have about a 33" inseam. I usually wear a size 8 in most clothes, but in Trendy Queen jeans I wear a size 6. These jeans are very flattering on an hourglass shape like mine. Their denim has just the right amount of stretch. It does not get loose after wearing for several hours and holds its shape while remaining comfortable. Now - the things that are a bit off about the jeans. Both this pair and my other pair of jeans from Trendy Queen had a VERY strong chemical/plastic odor, which took several washings to get rid of. But once it was gone, they were fine. My other pair are a regular wide leg raw hem jean (now discontinued?), but this pair has an irregular hem. I didn't think it would be quite so square-cut irregular, but it looks really weird, at least to me. I think I will be further cutting these jeans to a wide cropped length raw hem and wearing them with chunky boots instead. Because I do love them, I just don't love the weird hem.

I love the Trendy Queen brand - they consistently deliver quality, style, and great value - and these jeans are no exception. The fit is perfect and true to size according to their size chart. For reference, I’m 5’6”, 120 pounds, and the size 4 fits me exactly right. The high-rise waist is flattering without feeling restrictive, and the wide-leg straight style looks like something you’d find in a high-end boutique. The denim has just the right amount of stretch - soft and comfortable, but structured enough to hold its shape all day. No sagging, no baggy knees by the afternoon. They move with you while still looking polished. They’re easy to dress up or down and pair well with everything from fitted tanks to cozy sweaters. Stylish, comfortable, and well-made - another win from Trendy Queen!

These wide legged Plus-Size Jeans are a medium to heavyweight denim and have a lovely light color to them. They fit as expected with a bit of extra length-but most jeans and pants are long on me as I’m only 5’4. They have a high waistline, double button & zipper front with an elastic band in the back-helps with gapping. These are great for cooler weather-not a lightweight jean. Very nice-very in style-the wide flowing leg straight from the knee onward.

Levi Strauss Signature Gold Women's Mid-Rise Bermuda Shorts UPDATE 2: third year in a row buying these shorts for summer they are my favorite! I wanted to give an update on sizing because I’ve been working out and lost weight. I am now 5’4” 121lbs and size 2 fits perfectly! Although my shorts from the last 2 years are still in great shape they don’t fit anymore. These shorts last for a long time. :) I am buying 8 new pairs for this summer in size 2 because they are literally all I wear lol! I used to hate shorts but with these they cover my cellulite and give me confidence! No more long pants in the summer. Yay! I also started to order my step daughter these because her school dress code requires girls to wear longer shorts and she likes them as well. She’s 5’3” 135-140 with a straight figure and she likes the way size 4 fits. UPDATE 1: I ordered these last summer I think? I still am loving these shorts! I am back to order more for upcoming warm weather here in Texas. Black is finally back in stock- yay! I ordered a black pair and love them. They fit the same as the dark wash jean ones I have. I actually think I like the black ones even more than the dark denim, I didn’t think that was possible! Needless to say I definitely recommend these. I’m ordering now because when warm weather comes these sell out like hot cakes! Get yours now ladies! They have become mostly the only shorts I wear besides Nike athletic shorts for working out. I need to order multiples because I wear them constantly. Not having to pull your shorts down from riding up between your legs all day is amazing. Curvy ladies will know what I mean! I’ve tried so many other shorts and none of them compare to these. The only Bermuda shorts I’ve ever found that are flattering on me and don’t make my short legs look ridiculously frumpy! I wish they had even more washes for me to order because white and light wash aren’t my thing, not flattering on my big thighs. I only wear medium, dark or black wash denim. Although I’m tempted to go outside my comfort zone and possibly try the light wash.... ****Original review: I’m 5’4” 135ish and I ordered size 6. I range between a size 4-6 or 27-28. I’m normally a 4 in jeans. Size 6 is comfortable, ever so slightly loose. The size 4 fits as well (I tried both) but I just preferred the size 6 because it had a little more wiggle room. The pictures of me posted in the shorts are of the size 6. A lot of the reviews said to go up a size and I can see why because the size 4 was pretty fitted on me. I don’t like my shorts very tight fitting. Unless you are not curvy or very slim, then you may not need to go with your larger size. Or maybe you want a super tight fitting look, in that case you could probably stick with your normal size. They are extremely comfortable and flattering especially in the dark wash. I am a 30 year old toddler mom and these are very cute and practical for me! I don’t like short shorts so these are a win win. I don’t have to worry about them riding up and pulling them down during the day. Ladies who have thick thighs that rub, a big bottom and/or mommy tummy these are for you! I’m so glad to have found some comfy and cute longer length shorts that look flattering. They have a nice stretch and they don’t give me muffin top or cut into my stomach. I think curvy and/or plus size girls will love these too! I ordered like 5 pairs because I love them so much lol! The only slight issue I had was they stretched out a little bit when I wore them, but it wasn’t a big issue. They weren’t totally bagging out or anything. The only other thing would be I wish the back pockets were blank with no wavy line, not a big deal, just personal preference. Overall they just fit my daily casual mom style. I like how the hem is finished with a little roll, makes them look more pulled together in my opinion. They look cute with tanks, tees or even long sleeves! I like how they have a skinny leg, otherwise Bermuda shorts tend to look frumpy on me if they aren’t fitted to the leg. Levi’s always comes through for me. These definitely look cute and classy and more expensive then the price tag! If you hate short shorts you need these! They are a total confidence booster for those of us who want to hide cellulite, big thighs or just prefer a little more coverage. No inner thighs sticking together in the heat with the longer length lol! Lastly, you can’t beat the price!!

EMMIOL Women Denim Shirts Oversized Long Sleeve Button Down Blouse Distresse Jean Jacket Fall Outfits 2026 Lightweight denim with inconsistent quality stitching on buttonholes, though most seams were ok. This is pretty lightweight fabric for denim, and not heavy enough to stand up to any kind of work (like gardening) but comfortable for casual wear. The edges of the pockets have minimal backing so might tear easily if subjected to pulling or stress. This shirt is a very generous cut (as expected for oversized) and even at that, it runs large. I ordered a small (5'2" / 118 lbs) but the sleeves are WAY too long (see photo) but ok with the cuff folded back one time. Also, the sleeves have a sewn-in button tab to help keep the sleeve rolled up to the elbow. I washed the shirt as directed (warm water, tumble dry) and didn't notice much appreciable shrinkage. Overall, this is a lightweight but comfortable generously-cut shirt appropriate for casual wear, but just a little too big to be comfortable for me. YMMV

ANRABESS Women Short Sleeve Tops Scoop Neck Ribbed Knit Slim Fitted Basic Tee Shirt Casual Summer Clothes 2026 Spring Outfits This is a great, basic, ribbed scoop neck top. ANRABESS Women Short Sleeve Tops Scoop Neck Ribbed Knit Slim Fitted Basic Tee Shirt Casual Summer Clothes 2026 Spring Outfits I have this shirt in Navy, Pink & the darker purple! They are my go to!! I needed something breastfeeding friendly but didn’t want it to look obvious and this shirt was perfect. I wear them on constant rotation. When I originally ordered them I was about 4-5 months PP and now I’m 9 months PP. I ordered a medium because I did not want something too tight fitting so I like that the medium has more give to it. The small did fit but I returned it because the medium felt better for me. Size reference I am 122lbs and the girls (used to be) a size 34DD - I also ordered a medium when I was closer to 130-135 and the girls were still perkier. So if you’re in between and not sure what size, hopefully my range helps you! They are super soft and comfortable, the pink one definitely has a different feel to it, maybe because of the different dye to make the shirt? So the blue and purple are softer but I really like all of them! My navy one is beading up a bit, but truthfully, I wear them constantly. Definitely a must have for the stretchiness, design and price!!

Joe's Womens Charlie Ankle Exposed Button Fly Is it possible that a pair of jeans can move a woman to tears? It seems ridiculous, but yes, almost. As soon as I pulled these Joe's out of the bag I immediately felt how luxurious the denim was. It's soft, yet sturdy, and they feel positively plush on the skin. Yep, never had a pair of Joe's before but I'm familiar with the brand and had been planning on trying at pair at Nordie's and purchasing if I was pleased. Well, these make me more than pleased. I'm ecstatic. First, the color of 'Stephaney' is somewhat darker than the product picture shows, but the Icon styling and wash pattern are the same. The stitching is very well done and meets the brand's claim of meticulous attention to detail. There are leather logo details on the inside back waist and on one rear pocket, and logoed button, zipper and rivets. The cotton has a soft brushed feel and the 2% spandex gives flex where needing and hugging where it flatters. It's woven and cut in a way that feels it will hold its shape better than other jeans that sag during the course of the day. The fabric has some substantial weight but isn't too thick or heavy. These don't feel like they'll thin out and lose their shape at all. I'm relatively curvy with a 31" waist and 41" hips. But hey, these size 29 jeans unexpectedly lay perfectly on my waist without creating a muffin top, and fit around my heavyish thighs. The waistband is sewn without topstitching which reduces bulk and creates a smoother profile. And I really love how my bum looks in these too! The pockets are well placed and the stretch gives a bit of a lift and a cradle. Best of all, they're incredibly comfortable. The inseam measures in at 28-3/4" and perhaps a scant long for my 5'2" frame for an ankle jean, but they work without needing to hem them — still perfect to wear inside a pair of boots or in warmer weather with a pair of flat sandals. So Joe, you and me, same time next year for another pair in the new season. Or maybe sooner for a different style for spring/summer ;D

I ordered the washed black/gray color. I really liked the color. I also really like that they're true to size as well as the fact that they're super high-rise. They have a 12-in rise, which I discovered I really like for wearing with crop tops. For me personally, I like that I expose less skin that way. Anyhow, I decided to return these because I wasn't completely in love with how they look on me. As others have mentioned, the material is a tiny bit on the stiffer side which means they don't sit in as flattering of a way as it does in the pictures. I wish they draped more loosely down the legs rather than stiff edges. I'm going to try a pair in a different color with distressing to see if it fits any more to my liking. It may also be that I don't wear jeans like this and I'm just not used to how it looks on me. I do wish they were more fitted in the butt so it was more flattering on the booty. These look good with platform sneakers, but I didn't care for how they looked with ankle boots. They're very comfortable, but I kinda wish they were in inch or two longer. I'm 5'7", 123lbs, and 27" waist. The size small fit perfectly without being too tight. The waist was 13.5" across on the size small. UPDATE: I ordered a pair in the sky blue color with distressing. The material is much softer and sits better. More like the stock picture. I like the feel and look of this pair better than the washed gray jeans, but I ultimately decided to return them because I like the gray color better and would be more inclined to wear that color with my wardrobe. I also think I'm just not used to wearing this style of jeans and feel a little bit self-conscious about how they look on me. Which is a bummer because they're very comfortable and I still like how they look with crop tops. My only concern with the sky blue pair is since the material is softer and more flexible, I'm not sure the durability/longevity of it. But since they won't stretch out like skinny jeans do, I think it would be just fine. Also, I found the sizing between the two colors to be fairly consistent. 12" rise, 14" waist, 31" inseam, and 10.5" ankle opening.

I deliberated quite a bit before ordering these jeans, because based on the other styles that I saw pictured by customers, I wasn't sure if they had seams down the front and patches in the back with no pockets. I finally crossed my fingers and hoped they would look just like the product pictures -- and hallelujah, they do! The style I am wearing is Vintage-blue05 Straight, which is vastly different from some of the other styles. Those other styles aren't for me -- I was looking for something reminiscent of the boyfriend jean trend in the late 90s, and that's exactly what I got! But with cute leopard star patches on the back pockets! These jeans are pleasantly faded, have a nice thick quality, and definitely outrank my 90s jeans because they have great stretch to them, too. They are SO COMFORTABLE, it's ridiculous. I could sit cross-legged in these with no problem. They are going to be so comfortable to wear all day. They can be dressed up with a pair of booties or kitten heels, or dressed down with sneakers or Birkenstocks (or other slides). I can wear a t-shirt and oversized blazer with them, or a cropped sweater or sweatshirt, or a tank top on the weekends -- they are very versatile, especially with that cute leopard print touch on the back pockets. And the back pockets are real pockets, with the patches sewn over them (I know in another style a reviewer noted their patches were sown directly over the seat of the pants with no fabric underneath -- that is NOT the case with this style). The sizing is perfect. I'm 5'6" and 130 lbs and the small is perfect. They don't arrive with a ton of wrinkles like most of the clothes you get online, and they have no weird smells. They're well sewn and seem durable and high-quality. Overall, these rival any jeans I could have picked up at a department store or a higher-end boutique for a lot more, so I rate them an OUTSTANDING value at this price point, and highly recommend them if you're looking for a super-comfy, Y2K throwback pair of slouchy boyfriend jeans that you can wear with anything. I would give these 10 stars if I could!

These are really comparable to a softer and warmer version of Pajama Jeans. I wear these to work and I feel like I’m wearing soft cozy fleece sweats. Highly recommend!

True Religion Women's Stella Low Rise Skinny Jean Ok, first of all, these are authentic. All pockets are real pockets, and they are extremely well made. I'm 5'0", 108 lbs, and I bought the 27s. The fit is PERFECT, but I will definitely need to have them hemmed. For now I've been folding the excess pant leg up and under, and it works just fine, because the fit is slim enough that it stays in place all day without budging.I can't gush enough about how thrilled I am to have found these jeans. The high rise pant trend needs to die. It's not really flattering on anyone, but it is murder on us short girls. At only 5'0", even "low rise" jeans come up so high on me that the waist band always covers my belly button. Some "low rise" jeans come up so high on my torso I can practically tuck my tits into them. Not only is it crazy unflattering, but it's incredibly uncomfortable. The jeans always dug into my stomach when I sat down. For years I have had to rearrange the waistband of my jeans every time I sat. I so missed the 90's style, "sit so low on your hips you flash your crack every time you sit" jeans. They were so much more comfortable and flattering on me! I've been searching for years, but couldn't find anyone that still makes a true "sits on the hips" jean. And then I found these! I couldn't believe it when I put them on. After trying on hundreds of pairs of "low rise" jeans that came up to above my belly button, these actually sit right at my hip bone, right where they should. And unlike the 90's low rise jeans, these fit so well they don't have that weird gap in the back of the waistband when you sit, and they don't flash your undies. They stay put. They are AMAZING. So flattering and comfortable.The only slight drawback is that the back pockets do sit a pretty low, so they're not the most flattering butt jeans ever. And if you're someone who is constantly shoving your phone in your back pocket like I am, the pocket flap is not the most convenient thing ever. But those are minor issues for me, considering how overall thrilled I am with these. After the first pair arrived and I saw how well they fit I immediately ordered two more pairs, and they are equally amazing. Love 'em.

The rhinestone jeans in dark blue are a nice alternative to my usual black skinny jeans. The fabric is very soft and not stiff like some other jeans fabrics and is stretchy. The color of the jeans is not exactly dark blue, is more of a lighter blue with some distressed marks that look pretty. The rhinestones are a nice feminine detail and make the jeans stand out from the traditional jeans. I am 5'2" tall, 135lbs, 29" waist, 41" hips and size 14 fits well from the hips, which is the most difficult part for me to find a good fit. The jeans are medium rise and not completely high rise, with medium rise the jeans fit well from the waist but if I wear them as high rise then they are big from the waist. The jeans form some creases below the zipper and to the sides but wearing them medium rise helps. The jeans are a little long on me and if I wear them with booties, they are ok but if I wear them with flat shoes then they feel long. The fit is straight and they are not wide leg. I wash the jeans inside out, using a cold, delicate cycle and allow them to air dry to protect the rhinestones. They take long time to dry but I want to preserve them as much as possible. I enjoy wearing them with cute tops.

I wasn’t sure what to expect with these jeans since I haven’t worn elastic-waist denim since I was a toddler, but I was pleasantly surprised! The cut is modern with a wide leg, and the elastic waistband doesn’t have a ton of stretch, which actually works in its favor—it lays flat without bunching and looks more structured than I expected. The denim feels sturdy, the seams are clean and tight, and the pockets are nicely sized. For reference: I’m 5'3", 126 lbs, with a 30" waist, 40" hips, and a 27" inseam. I ordered my usual size M. I could get them over my hips and button them, but they’re definitely snug. A size L would likely fit better in the hips and waist, but would also be too long on me. Even though these are advertised as low rise, they fit more like a high rise on my frame—the waistband sits about an inch above my navel. Key takeaways: If you’re curvy or carry more in the hips, consider sizing up. If you prefer the jeans to sit lower on your hips (as “low rise” suggests), you’ll likely want to size up as well. Overall, these are well-made, stylish jeans—I just wish the rise matched the description a bit better.

I decided to order the Lesore Womens High Waist Wash Denim Wide Leg Straight Leg Jeans Pants with Cuffed Hem, as am in my major jean rehaul era. For reference, I'm 5 ft tall, 120 lbs and normally wear a small, 4-6 in most clothing, and can range anywhere between a 26-28 in jeans. Unlike some black rinse jeans, this doesn't have an unpleasant smell. Interestingly enough, the fabric is a blend of 61% Cotton, 31% Polyester, 7% lyocell and 1% Spandex. Here, I decided to order a size up to a 6, as I wasn't sure how the elasticity would be in general. Thankfully, this is a light to medium weight denim with more stretch than I expected and the size 6 fit just right with plenty of room to breathe. I honestly would've been just fine with a 4, as it sits a little bit low, but doesn't fall off my body. Construction all around is mostly solid, but I'm not too crazy about the plastic feeling buttons, which makes me wonder how long those will actually last. Since I'm a bit shorter, it falls just at my ankles which I expected and am totally fine with. Overall, the color is on point, they're nice enough for their price point and are super comfy in general.

Meh, I am on the fence with these. I don’t think they are worth the current price of $36. I am 5’4” and generally wear a 14 or 16 depending on the cut. I usually err on the side of caution when ordering online and size up. The color is true to the website photos. Material is your standard Jean material with a little stretch. So for the overall fit they are okay. Waist fits fine, no CT, thighs and calves fit as expected, however where I run into an issue is the inseam length. These are just a tad too long to wear with anything other than something with at least a two inch heel. They are sewn cuffed so it would be a pain to alter. 5’7 165 Lbs 33” waist 32/34D 29” inseamI ordered the size 10, and while they fit, I felt they needed to be two sizes smaller to allow for the amount of stretch. I look a little bit better and pulled together in the tighter stretch jeans. I love the feel of the pants, they’re lightweight and as I said, they offer quite a bit of stretch. I don’t think they’re particularly flattering, but that could also be that I just didn’t feel they were tight enough and let’s face it…My body isn’t all that flattering. I feel I have a pretty average inseam maybe a little on the shorter side but I like the length as anything too short looks like a Capri, which I do not like. The wash is stylish in color but if your expectation is the cool Boho style of the model, they aren’t quite all that. Certainly not on me. Solid average pant.

I’m 5’4”, around 215 lbs, and postpartum, so I’ve been in the process of updating my fall wardrobe to fit my new body and hopefully find pieces that make me feel good again. I ordered these jeans in a size 16, and I was pleasantly surprised with how comfortable they are. They have a nice amount of stretch without feeling too thin or flimsy, and the high waist gives me some support in the midsection, which is something I really appreciate right now. The overall fit is flattering, and I like that the wide-leg cut is on trend — it makes them feel current without being too “young” for me to wear confidently. What threw me off at first was the cuffed hem. I wasn’t sure if it was supposed to be a trend I hadn’t heard about, so I even asked my teenage daughters. They said it’s not really a thing, at least not around here, and I’d agree. I ended up unrolling the cuffs, and once I did, I loved how the jeans draped — they have a nice wide-leg/flare look that is very stylish right now. That said, my biggest complaint is the hem itself. The jeans come with a raw edge at the bottom, which makes them look unfinished and honestly a little cheap. Even if they were designed to be cuffed, I think a proper sewn hem would make them look much more polished and higher quality. For the price, I’d still say they’re decent, but I’ll probably have someone sew a real hem onto them so I can get more wear out of them without worrying about fraying. Overall, these jeans are comfortable, flattering, and affordable, but just be prepared that the raw hem might not be to your liking unless you’re into that look or willing to get them hemmed properly.

This style of jeans is something new for me! The thighs are a bit more loose than skinny jeans and the high waist is a new aspect. Having a high waist is definitely flattering on my post-pardum body, especially my belly area. I am 5’5” and weigh 145. I ordered the size 8 and it fits pretty great.The material has some stretch to it but it is not on the extremely stretchy side. You can comfortably sit. The pockets are real and have depth to them. I enjoy the big cuffs at the bottom and the length is just right for my height to wear with flats. The instructions say that you can use a warm iron, so I will do some pressing to the cuff area. The fraying on the cuff is cute and obviously intentional.The sewing is good and it seems to be made with quality. These feel good to put on and I’m excited to try out this newer style!

This dress is great in person. I gave it 4 stars due to the length. I ordered both a medium and large for me to try on. I’m petite (4’11”) and a little busty (C cup) but am a bit heavier than normal right now (160lbs.). The medium fits a little snug on my chest - I could wear it and make it work and I love the length which hit right above the knee. The large was more roomy and comfortable on the chest but a little longer than I’d like (falls below the knee). I decided to go with the large mainly bc we hope to get pregnant again soon and I wanted some room for a growing belly and bust lol. The fabric is a soft, lightweight denim and very comfortable! Not thick which is great for south Alabama. The buttons are functional too which is great for breastfeeding later. I would say it fits true to size.

Sidefeel Women's Skinny High Waisted Jeans They are stretchy but thick! Not see through or cheaply made. I have a big natural butt, hips, and a small waist. Based on the reviews, I sized down to a 10 instead of a 12. So glad I did! These are a must buy if you want quality white jeans!!! I’m 5’1”, curvy at 170 pounds. I got these in the light blue color with the elastic waistband and drawstring because I already have several dark blue pairs of regular jeans and have been wanting a pair of baggy jeans for awhile. For reference I am 6’ tall, currently weigh 165lbs, and have a 32” waist. I usually wear a size M-L but I’ve learned from previous experience to size up when buying clothing off here. Since I wanted a baggy fit I ordered the XXL and it worked perfectly! It fits more like an XL like I knew it would and gives me that perfect 80s baggyness all us Gen X loved so much. As far as the length goes, these go past my ankles in the XXL size so if you are a tall woman then you are definitely safe with the XL and XXL sizes. Possibly even Large but I can’t be sure since there isn’t a size chart. These jeans are honestly made way better than I expected. They are also really comfortable as well. They are stitched and serged well meaning they should hold up to washing and wear for quite awhile. They are what I would call a medium thickness and have four pockets that are all pretty deep. So far they’ve come in pretty handy when my hands have been full. I’m really happy I got these and may end up repeatedly washing and living in them at this point.

The quality of these cargo jeans is great—the material feels durable and the construction is solid. I like the classic look of the DarkBlue color. However, the sizing is a major issue. I ordered a large based on the recommendation (size chart not offered) and while the fit was decent through the hips and legs, the waist was a huge disappointment. It has zero stretch and is disproportionately small, making it uncomfortably tight on my 32 inch waist. The only "baggy" part of these jeans was the saggy backside, which was not the look I was going for. These jeans could be a fantastic buy if the sizing was more consistent or if you have a specific body type with a much smaller waist-to-hip ratio. For most people, you'll likely need to size up for the waist and be prepared to deal with a baggy fit elsewhere.

HDE Women's Plus Size Skinny Jeans with Pockets These are basic looking pull up jeans. The material is relaxed and not stiff. They’re really stretchy and comfortable. They have working front and back pockets. The front pockets are pretty big. They don’t look like fake jeans like other pull up jeans like this. I’ve ordered a few pairs of other pull up jeans and returned them because they looked like fake jeans. You can’t tell that these are not regular jeans. Where the zipper would be is sewn as if there is a zipper. The only thing that gives them away is the solid band around the waist and there is no button. As long as your shirt covers the waist, you can’t tell that these aren’t regular ol jeans. The legs are pretty long. They’re too long for me and they bunch up around my ankles and throughout my legs. I’m 5’2” but my legs are kind of short. I ordered the 2X and they are too big. They are too baggy every where and I definitely need a belt with them. I’ve gained a lot of weight and my waist and stomach are where most of my weight is so I was surprised that they were loose even at the waist. I might buy a pair in the next size down & hope they fit better because I really like them & they’re priced good. I’m just not sure about the long length of the legs. Overall I really like these jeans. If you’re looking for some pull up, stretchy, comfy jeans & you don’t have short legs then I would recommend them! 4 stars- because they are way too long for me.

HDE Women's Plus Size Skinny Jeans with Pockets Okay, the first thing any woman wants to know about a pair of jeans is: how are the pockets? These jeans have real pockets! Not just real pockets, but FIVE real pockets (the standard for jeans)! They're not as deep as men's jeans' pockets, but I can fit my entire hand in the front pocket, something I can't say about most of the women's jeans I've tried on. My large phone (iPhone 12 Pro Max) fits comfortably in the front pocket too. That said, these are technically jeggings and not true jeans, as they have no fly or waist opening (not a major issue for women, but don't you dare take away a man's fly), but beyond that, they feel like regular jeans, and will probably wear like regular stretch jeans. They're not for the most rugged work, but then, stretch jeans generally aren't. The fit is good, but I would recommend sizing down if you want a tight fit. I sized by the measurements, and mine are a little baggy. I'm happy with the inseam length—usually I find pants to be on the long side, with my 27" inseam, but these are just about right, with only a small amount of bunching at the ankle.
reviewer_name_raw: Real pockets! But maybe size down

I recently purchased the Kensie Ella High-Rise Straight Leg jeans in a size 14, which is the largest size offered. For reference, I am 5'3" and weigh 195 lbs. I usually wear a size 16, but these jeans have a lot of stretch, so the size 14 fit me quite well. The fabric is soft and stretchy, which made them comfortable to wear. While I'm getting used to the straight-leg style, I do find the jeans to be flattering overall. However, I noticed some minor issues with the front, where the fit isn't as smooth as I would like. Additionally, the price is a bit on the higher side for what you get, which is something to keep in mind. All in all, these jeans are a nice addition to my wardrobe, especially if you're looking for a comfortable and stretchy option. Just be aware of the sizing and the fit in the front before purchasing.

I am impressed with the fit and quality of these jeans. I really like the higher rise (I have a longer torso so these come up to just under my belly button.) The light wash and shorter inseam works well for Summer, although I will wear these year round. The denim is thicker and do stretch out some with wear during the day, so it may be best to size down if in between sizes. I am 5.5 1/2", 120 lbs and ordered a size 4 in the Pace colorway.

Fit a little weird on me, maybe because I'm short, at 5'0". The legs are very baggy, almost jenko like, and that's great if you like the style, and I'm willing to see if this style grows on me, but for the most part as of right now they feel weird. They're a great idea for the summer because they're so floppy they feel like you're not wearing any pants at all, almost like palazzo style.

Honestly these jeans feel high quality and fit comfortably. They are actually relaxing to wear and are flattering to my curves. I’m 5’5 and 250lbs. I got an XXL. The only downfall is that one of the buttons popped off almost immediately as I tried to unbutton them to try it on. It looks like an easy fix though.

I ordered these leggings for myself in a size large, which is my regular size. For reference, I am 5 foot 3 and 165 pounds and they fit perfectly. I got them in the hot pink color, and yes, they are very pink. Think darker bubblegum, Pepto Bismol pink, which happens to be one of my favorite shades. These are made to look like denim skinny jeans, and while they obviously are not actual denim, they are cute and you can tell that is the look they were going for. (also, they have no functional pockets, but they are warm). That part honestly adds to the charm. They are high rise in the front, but the back dips down into a V shape, which is a little unusual. It did not bother me, but it is something to be aware of if you prefer a straight waistband all the way around. They are very soft and on the thicker side, which I really like. They feel well made and not flimsy at all. They also have the scrunch butt detail, which some people love for accentuating the backside. For me, they are mostly just comfy leggings for around the house. I plan to wear these with an oversized t shirt, or layered under sweats when it is cold, the same way I wear most of my leggings. They are buttery soft, comfortable, and cute enough to feel put together even when lounging. Easy 5 out of 5 stars. Thick, soft, well made, and a great pink.

Fabulous classic denim shorts with a relaxed look. They have a high rise that fits perfectly around my waist. The button closure and zip fly makes for easy putting on and taking off is a breeze and the zipper never gets stuck. At 5’4” and 175 lb. the length is perfect with a 7-inch inseam that falls a little lower than mid-thigh for me. The length and fit around my thighs make them the best fitting shorts I’ve ever owned. The hem is cuffed making them stylish and contemporary. The classic five pockets are perfect to carry all your small essentials easily. They are loose and stretchy making them cool and breathable in warmer weather. They let you move freely and are super comfortable against your skin. I can wear them all day without them losing their shape. The fabric is 58% Cotton, 24% Polyester, 16% Viscose, 2% Elastane. I chose the classic blue color which is true to the shorts pictured. They have a distressed look but not to be confused with the torn denim look. They are very versatile and can be worn with a simple T-shirt, sweatshirt, sweater, denim jacket or an elegant top. You can pair them with flip-flops, sandals, strappy heels or my favorite western boots. The care is also very easy: machine wash and dry on low and they come out perfect, ready to wear. Don’t be alarmed by the mom jeans description. They are truly flattering and I definitely recommend.

I ordered these to have an additional option for warm-weather wear, and I can already tell these cute, classic, 5-pocket jeans shorts will become one of my go-to items this summer. They’re high rise, which I prefer, and a good length. They extend mid-thigh on me (I’m 5’5”). Their fabric is 52% cotton and 42% synthetics, which makes them quite stretchy and giving and therefore comfortable to wear. I’m impressed with how well-constructed these are. No loose seams or threads but also attention to little details such as the stability of the cuffs. The cuffs are stitched in 4 places so they won’t unravel during wear or in the wash. Lovely! Finally, they’re sized as expected. I ordered my typical size, and they fit me fine.

I think we can all agree that blue jean shorts will never go away, so I was excited to try on these shorts and discover how comfortable they are. They are made of a cotton/polyester/viscose mix fabric that looks like blue jean and has a nice stretch in all directions. I am 5’ 115lbs and ordered a medium because my weight is in my middle. They fit but were long, like bermuda shorts. I expect that because of my height, but they easily rolled up. They are classic blue jean styling including the watch pocket. I expect these to be my get up and go shorts when the weather turns a little warmer.

The fit is perfect. The material is great quality, the denim is soft and does not feel heavy. The color is soft and will be great for Spring and Summer. They fit loose but are snug around the waist and loose from the thigh down. I ordered a small and have a little room to gain some summer weight and still have a comfortable fit. I am 5' and they do not pool much at the foot. The material does not have a huge stretch to them but you will be able to fit into them if a little stretch is needed. I love the way they fit. These will be great if you are looking for a loose jean that has style as well.

These jeans are stylish, and I’m glad I ordered them. I went with a size small, my usual here (5'7", 135 lbs, 34-27-37), and while they’re a little snug in the waist, I’d still call them true to size based on the chart. Comfort-wise, they’re great. The wide, open leg style gives them a nice, modern look. Problem with them is they really look like "Mom jeans", where the front puffs out just a bit and the pockets don't lie flat, also puffing out so I look a little wider than I am, especially since the waste sinches so much. Another drawback but not a dealbreaker is breathability. Some jeans feel lighter and more airy, while these are on the heavier side, trapping heat more than I expected. Case in point: I tried them on just before walking my dog in 80-degree weather at dusk, thinking the wide leg might help keep me cool. Spoiler—it did not. That said, aside from my legs cooking a bit, the jeans themselves were genuinely comfortable and easy to move in. Overall, stylish and comfy for everyday wear—just not my first pick for warm weather or sexy.
reviewer_name_raw: Stylish, Comfortable Jeans—But Don’t Expect Breathability

user_comment: These women’s jeans with bling are really cute and fun for a night out! I’m 5’7” and about 175lbs. and the size large fit me just right. The denim fabric is light weight, soft and has just the right amount of stretch, making them comfortable and easy to wear all day/night. The zipper is a bit rough to zip and the button seems weak which is why I deducted a star. The bling detail is what really makes these jeans stand out; it adds just enough sparkle and style without being too flashy. You can easily dress these jeans up with heels and a blouse or keep it casual with a hoodie and sneakers. Quality is nice and if you want jeans that are eye-catching, comfortable and fun these are a great pick!

These jeans look good on me and fit me well (aside from the length), but I can’t rate them highly because the sizing and fit seem very off from what’s shown in the photos, plus they have the dreaded mothball smell that some denim has, which I have never successfully gotten to come out of any denim, ever. The fabric does have some stretch that makes these very comfortable ... if they fit you. The button at the waist has that cheap metallic rattle that makes me think of off-brand clothes I’ve seen at stores with odd names that also sell skintight neon club dresses — although it does have a single rhinestone on it, which is funny and kinda cute. Also, the tag says you can hand wash them even though the listing says dry clean only, so that’s okay. About that sizing: Normally, I wear an XS or S (with a 26 inch waist and 33 inch hips). I got these in small and they’re semi-fitted in the thighs and baggier on my lower legs, nowhere near the wide leg style that’s currently trendy. They definitely don’t look the way they look on the models. There’s a video review from Yvonne M. in the listing and these look just a little looser on me than on that reviewer. They are high-waisted, as described, but they’re also several inches too long and I’d have to cuff them seriously or have them hemmed to wear them (I’m 5’1”). I’m not sure these would fit like the product photos even if you size up, as the waist might be too big then, but if you do want the wide leg look, you don’t mind returning things, you’re not sensitive to smells, you’re tall or don’t mind hemming, and you’re really inspired to take a chance on these, then yes, I’d say get at least one size up. Otherwise, maybe take a pass.

user_comment: For reference, i'm 5'1 and 150 pounds.I ordered a small, xsmall would of been better. Over all pants were great quality, denim with plenty of stretch. When ordering size down ,im short and these drag . The drawstring works and can help if you have a smaller waist.

These are so gorgeous. Straight out of package the rhinestones are GLEAMING. I expected to see little rhinestones loose in the bag when I pulled them out but no, all gems were still secure on the pants. I squated a few times in them and no gems came off then either. I would be cautious though if your thighs rub together, there are rhinestones between the thighs too. The jeans have minimal stretch, straight legs and a tapered waste. I typically wear a size 10 or 12 US and the large just barely fit me. Granted I am bloated this week, so hopefully they will fit more comfortably once the water weight goes down. I'm also 5' 9" and typically need long length or tall jeans. These hit me right at the ankle. I wish they were just a bit longer but for average length jeans they're not the worst. Also just fyi, they smell like burnt popcorn. Hopefully that will go away in a day or two after I air them out. Even so, I'll definitely be wearing these this holiday season.
Love these pants.Wanted something different and I got it. I’m 5’5 and they wasn’t to long. Want more pictures? Follow my Instagram at Fabulouskiana_

I'm a longtime wearer of skinny jeans and have never found a pair of wide leg jeans that I like. I think my shape is a major factor here though. They arrived as pictured and described. Here's what I ordered, Fit Type: 26" Inseam | Size: Large Petite | Color: Ice Blue For reference, I am pear shaped, normally a size 6 in pants, 40" hips, 28" waist. I'm 5'3" tall, 135 lb. I ordered based on the size chart in the lower pics. Happy to say that they fit well. If you're like me, with a small waist and larger hips and behind, you'll love the fit of these, no gap. High waist is comfortable. The pockets are deeper than most. Uploading some photos so you can zoom in and get a closer look to see what I mean. The control top is comfortable, I wouldn't say that they have much slimming going on but not having buttons is definitely smoother and sleeker looking in general. Details & stitching all look good, stretchy. They are not really cropped at my height and I'm tempted to cut them a bit shorter. I feel like the wide legs just make me frumpy and are unflattering, especially when this wide. I washed them on cold and hung dried. I don't feel like they shrunk but I'll update after more wear and washes if that changes. Super comfortable and a decent value at the time of my order. Nice weight, they're not thin. Recommended for other body types. I wouldn't order them again with my shape though, just personal preference.

These are great, trendy jeans. The stretch-denim fabric ensures they're super comfortable, and the overall quality is solid. The seam down the center front adds an interesting detail, but a bit of an odd feel while you're wearing them. The pockets are perfect. The fit of the size M is spot on. The length is good for me- ~5'6" wearing tennis shoes. If you're taller the length might be an issue. As far as the overall look on me- hmm. On the one hand, the style seems slimming in the hips. But I think the wide leg, while stylish at the moment, tends to make my legs look shorter. Anyway, you can judge by the photos. Obviously I'm not going to look like the model ;o)

I really like these jeans a lot. The denim is very thick but has a lot of stretch to it, so they're comfortable to wear all day. The legs are very wide but not distractingly so. They are a bit shorter than I expected. I am not super tall (5'8") but I feel like these would look best styled with platforms and they are a tiny bit short for that. Overall, a good deal for a trendy look. They run true to size and stayed snug, never feeling stretched out of restrictive. They come in S-M-L-XL, which is a bit unconventional as opposed to the traditional numbered sizes, but I ordered L and they fit perfectly. I can see them pairing with a loose t-shirt, fitted tank, or going out top equally well.

I am 5'8" and 165lbs with a 31" waist. I normally wear a size 12 in jeans, sometimes a size 10 and occasionally a size 14 depending on the brand. There was not size chart for these so I got them in a size L (I own clothes from size S to 2XL, so who knows about sizing anymore); typically I am between a M and a L. The L was bigger than expected, I could have ordered a size M. However, they are very stretchy and comfy and I would prefer them to be a bit bigger rather than too tight, I can always just wear a belt with them. The length is perfect for me.As I said these are incredibly stretchy, you don't have to undo them to pull them up and it feels like wearing sweatpants. They are 80% cotton, 18% polyester, and 2% spandex. They aren't super soft but are still comfortable. The material is fairly lightweight but still had a durable feel to it.I love the design of the front and back pockets. The pockets look cute and are very functional and well sized (the front pockets are huge). These have a wider leg on them which I love as well.I love jeans that look cute like jeans but feel like you are wearing comfy lounge pants, so these are a huge win for me.The denim isn't the best quality but stitching looked clean on these and they washed up really well without any shrinkage. I do think they are just priced slightly too high for what they are, I would expect to spend $40-$45 for jeans like these but I guess tariffs and all that.Recommended if you are looking for a lightweight super comfy and stretchy wide leg jean.

Glad I did, as these fit me nicely but still somewhat tight around my legs, so note that these run SMALL and are not true to size. I would suggest ordering a size up. Because the of stretchiness of the elastic waist, these are very comfortable when sitting down with just enough stretch in the waist and hips to allow a more roomy fit. It's a medium weight material - not thin and just about as thick as some skinny jeans. The pockets are nice size, as I can fit my phone in the front pocket easily.Overall, I like these but not sure I would order another pair at the price advertised as it's quite expensive for shorts whereas I can get name brand much cheaper.

PRETTYGARDEN Women 2026 Fall Clothes Long Sleeve Shirts Ribbed Knit Fitted Basic Tee Going Out Tops Underscrub Casual Outfits I ordered this in light blue, black, beige, & brown in a size large. For reference, I’m 5’8”, 155 lbs, 38DD, and currently postpartum. I specifically did not want anything overly tight that would accentuate my midsection or love handles, and the large fits perfectly. The material feels substantial, not thin or see-through, and the light blue color is exactly as pictured. The length is great too. It’s long enough to tuck in or wear with high-waisted jeans without constantly adjusting it. This is one of those basics that looks simple but elevated. I’ll definitely be ordering more colors.

Wow, what a find! I recently discovered this Wiholl brand, and I have ordered several tops from them with great results. This particular sweater is extremely well constructed from a silky-soft knit, and the color is stunning — sort of a warm cinnamon-y terracotta. I’m typically a size 13/14 and a bit of a shorty, so I generally order an XL in tops to keep them from being too clingy around my middle. For this style I took a gamble and sized up to an XXL, hoping that with its fine weight it would drape beautifully and float just a bit away from my torso … more like a tunic. And it does! For me (5’ 3”) the hem hits at low hip, giving me the versatility to wear it with jeans OR leggings, and my lower hemisphere is nicely covered. The icing on the cake is that this sweater is easy care, and it can be laundered in the machine. I plan to turn it inside out and wash on the delicate cycle with cool water, and then place it in the dryer for 5 or 10 minutes before smoothing it into a towel to finish air drying. The garment tag states that washer and dryer are fine, this is just me being cautious because I love this sweater so much! *** Because I rely so often on the reviews of others when I purchase on Amazon, I give my honest opinion about items such as this in the hope that you’ll find it , too.

These are great if you want the look of jeans and the comfort of leggings. I wore them while decluttering my closet, crouching, squatting, climbing and crawling around, something I would normally do in yoga pants. The tummy control is a nice feature. I also like the thickness of the material. It's thick, but not stiff or too hefty. They seem like they should last quite a while. I am 5'2" and got the 28-inch inseam. The length was perfect on me. I did take away one star for the very strong bleachy chemical smell that took 4 washes to get out. I've noticed this a lot lately with clothes and shoes I have purchased on Amazon. I am not sure what the chemicals are for, but it is worrisome when it takes 4 washes to get rid of it. I will probably purchase these in black as well.

I would have given these black jeans looking pants 5 stars because the shorter inseam is clearly stated, just don't think they're really tummy control pants (the waistband is tighter than the belly). But I do like them, so would recommend them if the inseam is a fit for you and you don't want super tight tummy control features. These are very soft, comfortable, straight-legged, have plenty of pockets, stitched well, and are not thin, but not extremely thick or hot - good sturdy quality. They have stretch so are easy to move in, but also like that they're mostly natural cotton so are durable, breathable, and easy to clean. I care for our mother who is 5'5" and short waisted so I ordered them for her. She usually needs an inseam of 32-33", and these are basically floods on her at 28" - not short enough to be capris, but I did just fold them up a couple times to make them more like capris, and she likes wearing them. Just not in the winter. They are actually quite slimming, smoothing, and flattering. So just check the measurements, and don't expect super tight tummy control, but at least they won't make you feel like you're being squeezed. Otherwise, they are very comfortable, give a little support, and seem to be made well.

These cotton-blend jeans are great. They're soft and stretchy and wildly comfortable. I'm 5' 3", 140lbs, and ordered a Medium with a 28" inseam. The length is perfect. If you have a bit of a booty they will look fabulous on you. I ordered the dark blue color and though the stitching is visible, it's not super obvious; the dark wash is the more obvious feature. The jeans do not have a zipper since they are "tummy control" and this only adds to the comfort in my opinion. They're also a great option for pregnant or postpartum women for this reason. Just order a size or two up if that's your intention!

Ordered a 6 because I'm in-between sizes and have a sensory issue to my clothes being too fitted to me. For context, I'm 5'4 and 110 lbs, so it fit great on the waist and has stretch. haven’t had jean shorts in years and i’m really glad i bought these. recently lost weight and wanted some pants that weren’t leggings — these are good material and fit really well. (i’m 5’4 and 135 lbs, but have my saggy postpartum lower belly (my son is 4 hahaha)) — definitely recommend these though!

I like these shorts They fit great and are super comfortable. The stretchy material makes them even better, giving just the right amount of flexibility. I was a little worried they might be too short, but the length is actually longer than I expected—in a good way. They also aren't overly high-waisted, which I appreciate. The quality is good, and overall, I'm really happy with them. For reference, I'm 6' tall and weigh 175 lbs. I usually wear a size 10 or 12 depending on the brand, and I went with a 10 for these and they fit perfectly. I got the vintage blue color and now I'm seriously considering grabbing another pair.

I recently ordered the Sidefeel High Waisted Jean Skort in the Bluing color, size 8, and overall, I really like it! The material feels soft yet durable — it’s got a nice stretch to it that makes it comfortable to move around in. The fabric feels high quality, not stiff like some denim can be, and it has that nice bit of flexibility that makes it easy to sit, walk, or bend without feeling restricted. I love the asymmetrical wrap design, which gives it a modern and flattering look. The high-waisted fit also helps accentuate the waistline, which I appreciate — especially postpartum, when I’m still getting back to my pre-pregnancy shape. I’m currently three months postpartum, 147 pounds, and 5'0.5" tall. I usually wear a medium, and while size 8 fits, it’s just a bit tight around my belly area. I honestly think size 10 would have been perfect for now, but once I lose a bit more of my belly, I know this one will fit like a dream. The zipper quality is great — it glides smoothly and doesn’t get stuck, which is always a plus with denim. The color (Bluing) is beautiful, a nice medium blue that goes well with just about anything — from a casual tee to a dressier blouse. Overall, I’m very happy with this skort. It’s cute, comfortable, and stylish — I just recommend sizing up if you’re in between sizes or recently postpartum like me. I can’t wait to wear it more once it fits just right!

Lee Women's Legendary 26" Utility Crop Pant These pants are really nice and fit perfectly. I’m petite (5’0”, 142 lbs) and wear a size 6. They’re supposed to hit at or above the ankle, but on me they land right at the ankle—which is actually perfect. I didn’t have to alter them at all. The fit is flattering and comfortable, and the length works great if you’re petite. I’m very happy with them.

For reference, I am 5’7, 170 pounds, athletic build, and usually wear a size Large. The material is soft, stretchy, and really flattering. Great on its own and perfect for layering. I originally bought the crewneck version of this top and liked it, but the scoop neck is definitely more flattering on me. Just such an easy, versatile staple and absolutely worth grabbing in multiple colors.
reviewer_name_raw: Soft, flattering, and perfect for layering

user_comment: Love these tops. Purchased in black and in white. They are slim fit so I sized up to have a more comfortable fit. I’m 5’2” 134 lbs size DD bust and I ordered a large and it fit perfect. Very flattering. Great for layering.

OK, so I am either a 10 or a 12 and I decided to get the large based off of the size chart and the reviews, they are definitely flattering and they are definitely comfortable. I’m not sure about the size so I am actually washing them right now and the reason I say that is, they are a little tight in my waist naturally I do have fluff so I’m going to see with the amazing stretch and softness if they will get a little bit more stretch stretching so I could stick with the large rather than going up a size and having them too loose. They do seem like good quality and they look really nice. Am actually going to pop off the hymn to give an extra inch. I am 42 years old. I am 175 pounds and I am 5 foot six and I am washing them as we speak so fingers crossed that the extra inch and the softness flatter even further.

These shorts just arrived & I quickly tried them on. I am 230 lb. and 5’5”. I purchased a 2xl. They are stretchy & seem to fit comfortably. I wish the back of the waist came up a little higher & the thigh was a tiny bit looser.

LifeShe Women's Casual Detachable Hoodie Denim Jacket Overall, I like this hooded denim jacket and I love the ability to remove the hood. Color is great and I love the length. It’s cropped, but not too short. The only thing I don’t like is that it gets baggy at the wrists? I wish it tapered down more to the wrist. It’s just an odd placement for extra fabric. I’d say it runs true to size to maybe a little big. I’m 5’3” and 120 pounds and could have gone with a small but wanted the ability to wear things under it in chillier weather. I am going to keep it and I can see myself reaching for this to run errands in the spring or fall.

LifeShe Women's Casual Detachable Hoodie Denim Jacket very cute jean jacket with removable grey hood. the only issue is one button to hook hood in was missing with only strings hanging there. so i need to sew a new on one. it’s nice weight denim - good quality. I am 5’8” 157 lbs and i do have bigger hips. so the bottom button will close but it’s tight. the rest of the buttons have a ton of room to close. not a biggie as i will likely never button it up anyway.

I’m 5”8 and currently about 170 pounds. I purchased these jeans in a large, the length and fit is perfect. I bought these jeans when I was 14 weeks pregnant. I wanted something that didn’t have the waist band going over the belly because my bump was not big enough yet. These jeans were life savers because they were stretchy and didn’t bother my belly like my regular jeans did. The material feels nice. However, for some reason it gets itchy around the button after a while. I usually tuck my tank top in if it bothers me enough. The price was good for the quality. I am now 21 weeks pregnant and have not needed the extender that came with the purchase, but I will probably need it in the upcoming weeks.
Fit well and are comfortable. Good length for shorter people (I’m 5ft2in). If in between sizes, go with the smaller size!

These jeans are so perfect! I am 5'0" currently weighing 160 at 15 weeks pregnant. Bought size medium. The material is so stretchy and comfy, plenty of room to grow in them. Comes with an extender which I may have to use in my 3 trimester. They are a little long on me but I usually wear boots with these kinds of jeans anyway! So perfect, I will be buying more colors!!!!

I am 17 weeks pregnant and was looking for more stylish maternity jeans. These are perfect, comfy and stretchy. I think I’ll be able to use them throughout my whole pregnancy. I ordered the vintage color in size large. I was size 10-12 before this pregnancy and I’m 5’3. The length is a little long but with a short heel, it works great. Will be buying a second pair in a different color!

ZMTUO Skinny Jeans for Women High Waisted Stretch Vintage Denim Pants It wasn't until I took pictures that I realized just how SLENDER these pants make my legs look. They make me look like I've lost about 20 pounds and the heavens above blessed me by redistributing them straight to my tooshie. I'm 5'5 1/2, typically wear 14/16 in women's pants and ordered the XL based off the size chart. These are true to size, color- and COMFY. They don't just look good, they FEEL good. I haven't, but if I NEEDED TO- I absolutely have a full range of motion to round house kick my enemies like Jackie Chan. These hit me at the ideal length and don't make me look like I'm preparing for flood waters- even with heels they still look pretty dang cute and not awkwardly "not quite there" short. Perfectly long enough to cover my ankles so the old men won't go crazy. Even after washing they haven't shrank, pilled up, or bled dye all over my other clothes. Zipper is solid- doesn't snag or pop open after the bloating hits when you're done with a nice spaghetti dinner. The pockets are REAL pockets. Not that "to the first knuckle" fraudulent "pockets." The back ones are also real and they add a little something to look at on your bum. The material on these are the four way stretch sort of jeans, sort of jeggings- but not so at risk of holes and ripping. These shape your body nice so if you've got a little tum or cellulite- these are the girls girl of pants- they got you covered. I have several pairs of pants from this brand and their sizing across the board is consistent and true. They provide quality at a lower price- these are the pants you get at the mall, without the mall prices. I don't just recommend these pants, I also recommend the brand. They're solid, sexy, inexpensive, and worth it.

ZMTUO Skinny Jeans for Women High Waisted Stretch Vintage Denim Pants Fit & Style: The high-waisted design is spot-on. It sits comfortably at my natural waist without feeling restrictive, and the double-button closure adds a nice secure feel. They hug my legs perfectly from the hip down to the ankle, creating a flattering skinny silhouette without being uncomfortably tight. The dark wash denim is classic and versatile, and the contrasting stitching along the seams gives them a subtle vintage vibe that I really appreciate. They look polished enough to dress up with a nice top or keep casual with a sweater. Comfort & Stretch: The stretch denim claim is absolutely true! These jeans have just the right amount of give, allowing for easy movement throughout the day. I can sit, walk, and bend without feeling restricted at all. They have the look of rigid denim but the comfort of a much softer fabric. Quality: The denim feels like good quality, substantial material, and the construction appears durable. The stitching is neat and seems well-done, and the back pockets are placed nicely. Size M 130 lbs 5'7"

ZMTUO Skinny Jeans for Women High Waisted Stretch Vintage Denim Pants I am 5’3”, 170 lbs, and bought a size XL knowing I will have to hem them—but these fit not just well, but comfortably all over the parts of me they are on. They curve where I curve, and make allowances for my belly. The stretch is super-comfortable, not compressing, and with enough rise and give that they don’t slide down my backside when I sit or bend my knees up. Quite possibly the best-fitting pair of skinny jeans I have ever owned.

Gloria Vanderbilt Womens Amanda Classic High Rise Tapered Jean Petite I am 4' 10" and approx. 190 lbs. I own this pair & 3 other washes in a size 16 Short Plus. I'm not sure I've ever had a pair of pants that fits me well and doesn't need to be hemmed, even when I'm ordering "short." There is slight gaping at the waist, but far less than on others brands with my body type. Makes them good, comfortable sitting jeans, as opposed to some "standing" jeans - iykyk. I wear these to work in a nice casual dress setting. I wash ~ 4-5 wears. They don't stretch out very much over multiple wears and reset well after a wash. The color has been holding extremely well over multiple washes as well.

Lee Women's Regular Fit Chino Short The Goldilocks of shorts - not too long, not to short, they're just right. They fit above the hip bone and below the belly button. They have two front and two back pockets (you have to cut the threads to open them), the fabric is soft, there is no hem to iron out on the legs. Washed well. I used to wear Banana Republic shorts but after they changed their style, I needed to find a new pair I liked. I'm 5'8", 152 lbs, size 10 (I did size up as recommended and they fit perfectly).

Lee Women's Regular Fit Chino Short The quality is great, and so is the color. I’m 5’0 and weigh 207lb, a cute ball. Shorts and pants are hard to find, but I definitely recommend this to my plus size folks, it holds my belly well! It’s easy to move around in and it isn’t tight. I originally found a navy blue pair at Goodwill, same style and with the hot summer, I needed a new pair that matched more of my clothes. Very happy with this purchase :) !! And very glad Amazon had the same style in stock.

I got this top in khaki and I was not expecting to love it this much!! For the price, I honestly thought the fabric would feel cheap, but I was shocked when I tried it on. The material is thick, soft, and feels really high quality. For reference, I’m 5’4”, 124 lbs, usually a size S, and this fits true to size. It’s definitely more of a winter tee since the fabric runs on the heavier side — perfect for cozy days at home but also cute enough for dinners, restaurants, or a casual date. The sleeves are my favorite part — they give such a trendy, elevated look and make it feel way more styled than a basic t-shirt. Super easy to dress up or down and looks more expensive than it is. Such a good find!

(5ft 4in - 115lbs - Ordered size SMALL) Super cute top!! Fits perfectly (Although i personally wish i sized up to Medium because i like things more oversized). I got this on sale for a good price, and i definitely think it was worth the money. Super soft and comfortable, not too warm but also perfect for layering. Fabric is good quality and I like that the neckline is full coverage but not all the way up my neck. Fairly stretchy too! The only reason i’m giving 3 stars is because there is a tiny stain that i can’t get out. I noticed it when i first tried the top on but i was so excited i wanted to wear it so I just wore it backwards and my hair covered the tiny stain. I would return and buy another but I impulsively cut all the tags off !! Either way it’s still cute!!

I was on the fence about ordering this, but I'm so glad I did. It's boxy in the perfect way. Cropped without being too short - it's very easy to avoid baring anything with high-waisted pants (or maybe even mid-rise). I usually wear a small (5'8", 155lbs) in tops and rarely a medium. I wanted to boxy, oversized look so I got a medium, but I definitely could have gone with the small and still achieved that. The material is thick and good quality. It's actually knit, not printed (you never know!). I get compliments when I wear it.

First let me say, I do love the leggings and I have bought them multiple times. They are comfortable and stretchy. They look good when they are on. If you are looking for something that is comfortable, cheap, and easy to throw on, this is it. But be aware they do NOT last long and are not “fleeced lined”. There is NO WAY these should be considered “fleece lined”. When they are stretched, you can see through the material, and I have bought them enough to know that it won’t take long for the seam to pull apart in the crotch area. I just bought these ones and have worn them for a few hours. I am 5’2 and wearing the L-XL. Currently I weigh 180lbs. You can literally see the wall through the material. There is nothing in there that I would consider fleece lining. And if you look at the seam, you can see already see where it will eventually rip away. So in short, a great bottom for cheap, that won’t last very long, but is comfortable. It is not fleece lined as advertised though.

I can not state in words HOW MUCH I LOVE these leggings! I'm about 20 pounds overweight right now and these are high waisted which holds in my fat so well! I've included photos of both the blue and black leggings to show the leggings holding in my belly. lol. They're stretchy so they're also SUPER, super comfortable. I'm 5' 1" 138 lbs. I ordered the Small/Medium, Blue & Black Pack. These fit perfectly. One of the problems I frequently run into being so short is that leggings don't fit well in the knee area. They're usually loose and saggy. Sometimes they're also loose and saggy at the ankle . But these leggings are perfect! You can see in the photos they're tight and fit awesome around my knees, ankles, and everywhere else. And as an added plus they make my booty look very round and perky. In reality, my booty is sort of droopy but for some reason these perk it up. They do have a sort of fleece lining, but for some reason they're not hot. I did not feel overly warm wearing them. Also PLEASE NOTE - they are NOT real jeans! The buttons, belt loops, pockets etc are simply painted on. These do NOT have buttons, belt loops, or pockets and they are NOT jean material!! They’re cloth. Although from far off they do a great job of looking like real jeans. But honestly I love them more than any of my jeans. Can't say enough great things about them.

For reference, I'm 5'4", 194 pounds. Hips 48", waist 45". I ordered the 2XL in blue. (It probably doesn't mean much because I rarely buy anything with a fitted waist, but in American plus size brands, I usually wear between a 16 and a 20.) These leggings are very comfortable. The material is stretchy and super lightweight but has just enough of a fleece coating inside to feel verrrrry soft on your skin. The waist is quite high, and there's a little extra length at the ankle. The color is maybe a bit too bright to be hyper realistic, but otherwise these leggings look very much like jeans... The pockets on the butt were so real looking that I had to make sure they were only printed on. The fit: snug like leggings should be, but a little bigger than I expected. As you see in the pics, they're not in any danger of falling down, but they're not even half as tight at the waist and hips as most of my leggings are. If you're close to my size but with a bigger butt, you're probably still okay. The one thing I didn't like: they arrived with a very strong "dry cleaner" smell. I'm sure it's nothing a good wash won't fix, but it was very noticeable as soon as I opened the package. Beware if you have a really sensitive nose. Overall: excellent. Can't beat it for the price. I'll be wearing these a lot.

I love these leggings. They are super soft and fleece lined (which is so comfortable). I am 5'2" and 117 lbs (29-30 inch waist) so I bought two pairs in large/xl based on my waist (shown in the photo attached) and I own a pair in small (which is what I originally purchased). Honestly, you would not see a difference in a photo of me wearing either the small or the large size. The large is a little longer in the legs and comes up above my bellybutton, but other than that I don't feel much of a difference. It definitely feels more comfortable pulling it up so it aligns with the top of my legs to look like jeans rather than tights (and I'm short!). I will likely purchase it again in the large size since there's not much of a difference to me in tightness. Although I could see this stretching to fit a heavier person, I wouldn't consider it xl sized. I usually wear small or medium in other stores, and I'd consider this more of a medium size.

I bought these in size S/M but I think I could've gone with a Large very easily. I'm normally a size 6/8 and small in women's clothes, medium in anything"trendy" (like forever 21, H&M, etc.). I'm 5'8”, measurements 34-24-38, and most pants are a little short on me, these were no exception. I was expecting to probably have to exchange these for the larger size, especially right after I took them out of the package. They look like clothes for a ten year old, but these leggings stretch an absurd amount. I would not suggest a S/M for someone who has any extra "love-lumps" going on because I feel like these will role down in that situation. They're high waisted enough to feel really comfortable and they're not the warmest leggings but it's not like I can feel a breeze when I'm wearing these (like some other leggings I have, looking at you Target). Would buy again. My biggest suggestion is that, if you are trim and slim, buy the S/M. If you are anywhere else on the spectrum, buy the Large.

WAY small. According to the size chart I should have ordered a M. I ordered a L because I have wide hips and despite the size chart saying it would be ok reviews said it ran a little small. They zipped/buttoned but we're not comfy. Should have ordered up another size. Also, I'm 5'3 and this is the length on me. They feel like good quality jeans, but good luck getting your size right.

The jeans fit true to size and my daughter says that they are very comfortable on. She likes the star detail and they seem to be pretty well-made; however, the shape doesn't quite match the pictures on the ad. My daughter is 5'7'', so I can see why they may be a bit shorter, but overall, they do not seem to be as baggy or flared as we had hoped.

My daughter is 5 ft tall, 130 lbs and I ordered these shorts for her in size large. They are a little big, but still fit great and are comfortable. They are stretchy and are easy to move in. We like the dark blue color and the longer length. The cuff on the hem is sewn in place, so it doesn’t come unfolded or curl up when washing. The shorts had no issues with washing and drying.
