These Yoeasy barrel‑style jeans turned out to be such a great find. They’re incredibly comfortable, have that easy, relaxed fit, and run true to size. I’m 5'6" and 162 lbs, and the medium fits just right without feeling tight or restrictive. Even though they’re cropped, they work surprisingly well in cooler weather — I’ve worn them with boots and they still look intentional and stylish. The wide‑leg shape and drawstring waist make them great for casual days, but they’re polished enough that I can wear them to work too. Soft, comfy, and versatile. A fun addition to my wardrobe that I’ll be reaching for in every season.

I’m kind of in love with these!! I definitely ordered a size too big though. My recommendation is to look at the size chart and order your regular size. I sized up because I’m 5’8” and curvy; I’m pretty tall and usually jeans are too short on me or can’t accommodate for curvy booty and wider hips. These are way too big in size XXl (I usually wear a size XL or 14/16). But if I cinch the front with the drawstring I can still make these work. I’ll definitely order these again in my regular size though. But they’re still so cute and super comfortable!! I love all of the stitching and seams that give these a super cute barrel shape. The color is beautiful dark wash/blue color and so nice. I love the slight fading on the thighs and back. The front pockets are SO BIG and can hold so much! (They can fit a full sized lotion bottle of lubriderm brand as an example!) Seriously, you won’t need a purse with these awesome pants. Back pockets fit your phone as well. There are no belt loops so keep that in mind. The pants have a nice drawstring that you can tie in the front that is a cute detail. The drawstring really does secure the pants and you won’t need a belt because of it. The back also is cinched and stretchy on the waist line! The material is soft, thin and stretchy. It has a good thickness though and doesn’t look overly cheap or thin! They are just soft on the skin and really comfortable. They aren’t stiff or overly thick and flow nicely on your legs. They look like nice jeans, and they feel comfier than sweatpants. They’re so breathable. I would wear these around the house all day and not be bothered one bit. I love these jeans so much. My biggest recommendation is to not size up unless you really want something loose, but they’re so comfy and stretchy as is. They’ll be comfortable in your regular size! Just order them!!

These jeans are super cute! Got several compliments when wearing them. Fit is perfect. I got a small and am 5'4", 120 lbs. Love that you can cinch the bottom or leave them wide. Very comfortable too! I will say the dark blue is not as dark as the picture but I still love them.

roswear Stretchy Distressed Cuffed Skinny Capri Jeans, Washed Blue, Medium: I’m 5’2” 164lbs, 34” Waist, 40” Hip, Inseam 24/25”, Upper Thighs 21”, Calves 14.5”. I typically wear a M/L in tops and M or 8/10 in pants. I’m wider on top and smaller on the bottom. This pair of medium capris fit nicely. There is enough stretch in the waist to give the right amount of wiggle room. My only complaint is with the pockets. The interior of the front pockets slip up and stick out a little, likely because I carry more weight here (see photos). Annoying to me, but maybe not for you. Just a tad more material or sewing this down would be an improvement. The rear pockets also sit a little low on me, which I’m not a fan of, but not a deal breaker. What I like is the overall look, style and feel. These capri jeans are comfortable and unrestrictive because of the stretch. It’s just enough to be able to sit, squat, and kneel with no digging in. The waist is cute with the two buttons. No issues with the zipper. Overall, I like the distressed wash look and the way these fit. I think the quality and value are good. Would recommend. I hope this is to you.
reviewer_name_raw: Comfy Stretch Capris, Pockets are OKish

yoeasy Women's Wide Leg Jeans Mid Rise Stretchy Straight Leg Baggy Drawstring Casual Denim PantsSuper comfortable, soft denim that is indeed stretchy and very comfortable. This is the second pair of this style jeans I own. I initially ordered a pair in XL as I wear a 12 (most of the time 10, 12, 14) and wanted the baggy look of the listing photos so I sized up to XL only to realize you do not need to size up on these.I ordered these in size L, dark blue and love the look and quality - love the wide leg. What I don't love is the elastic band at the waist is very wide, and is so high-waisted that on me (short waisted person) they come up almost to my chest. If I pull the elastic band/pants down to where it rides across my natural tummy waist, the crotch hangs too low. I think they may fit tall people a little better than 5'5", size 12, short waisted people like me.When you first put them on they will fit more snug, but they stretch! Within 5 minutes they will be baggier and as you wear them they will get even looser. They really do have that loose-baggy-fit you see in the photos. The high waist can be 'hidden' by my tops so it's not a big issue and I love the wide ankle opens, the baggy look and the color. They look really cute with flats and leather sandals as well as cute tennis shoes and little short boots - so really, I can wear them with almost anything.

My favorite part of these yoeasy pants are the big roomy pockets honestly. My ID, debit card, and phone fits in front and back pockets. These demon pants are strong like they would hold up from multiple washes. I’m not sure if I have short legs I’m 5’6 and about 150 and I chose the medium because size which fits perfectly and not short length. The pants are soft but not super soft which fine; I don’t want my jeans to feel like pajamas. These are casual pants and I would not wear them for a night out as product description suggests. These denim is not lightweight. I would not wear these on a super hot summer like day.
reviewer_name_raw: Nice weight jeans with roomy pockets

user_comment: These are nice quality stretchy waist wide leg jeans. They have huge pockets front and back. The denim is soft anf have a baggy fit. The denim doesn't have stretch just at the gathered waist. I'm 5'4" and they don't appear super cropped. I could wear super flat shoes and not have them drag at this length. They fit naturally mid-rise but you can pull them higher or lower with the draw string waistband. They do not include belt loops. Overall if you are into the baggy wide-leg fit these are for you. I will like them when pockets are needed and for overall comfort while one up from athleasure but still casual.

Pretty comfy overall except the top piece of elastic in the waistband is thin and it irritated me because it felt like it was cutting in to me. The pants are very flowy, which I liked. They were long enough on me, which I was pleasantly surprised because I’m 5’8” and it’s hard to find pants long enough. I have a bit of a belly so I ordered size XL and they fit great, I’m a size 18 in jeans. Ignore the two spots on the pants in the pic- that was just from accidentally splashing water on them while washing my hands.
So cute. I’m 5’2” and the length is perfect for me. I’ll update review after I wash them. These are large. I’m just under 180lbs and carry my weight in my thighs.

user_comment: I can see why SPANX have become popular. They have anice stretch to them and help to shape your figure nicely. I have gained weight recently due to cancer .medications. I am short and have been uncomfortable in my usual jeans. I decided to take a chance on the SPANX. I do like the shaping and stretch. They weren't to tight either. It does seem like they're geared towards taller ladies though. I folded the bottoms under several inches so I could show what the correct length looks like in the pictures. I often have to get my pants altered since i'm only 5'1. So the length I expect to deal with, but the other part that seems that it's really made for tallo.Ladies is the waistband it came up very, very high on me. Fortunately, I was able to fold the waistband over once so that it was in a comfortable place, and it didn't really get too bulky. The crotch wanted to slip down a bit which I don't prefer. I had to pull them up a few times, and can tell they will have that tendency to slide down a bit on my figure. I really would have liked functioning front pockets, but perhaps they wanted them to lay more flat and shapely, so it's just an illusion of front pockets. Overall, these are excellent jeans. I really like the quality and they had no problem going through the wash. I would appreciate more sizing options for us shorter ladies. Many companies offer jeans in short length, or petite, so that the fit works better for those of us not not graced with long legs and torso. They are on a higher price level, so I recommend making sure they fit and feel fabulous for your figure.

Ok. So, I put off trying these on. They just looked small to me and I thought "these aren't going to fit me at all". I am up ten pounds because Christmas cookies are delicious and I'm weak. But, I mustered up the courage one night. And when I pulled them out I said "Oh my god!" out loud. They are stretchy and pull everything in. But, they aren't uncomfortable. The fabric is soft. Spanx makes such a great product. I still have pairs of theirs that I've had for years and they're still holding strong. The one thing you might want to consider about these is the length. I am 5'7" and these are very long on me. You'll have to throw on a pair of heels unless you're Taylor Swift. (And, if you ARE Taylor Swift...Hi Taylor! Big Fan!!!)

Ok. So, I put off trying these on. They just looked small to me and I thought "these aren't going to fit me at all". I am up ten pounds because Christmas cookies are delicious and I'm weak. But, I mustered up the courage one night. And when I pulled them out I said "Oh my god!" out loud. They are stretchy and pull everything in. But, they aren't uncomfortable. The fabric is soft. Spanx makes such a great product. I still have pairs of theirs that I've had for years and they're still holding strong. The one thing you might want to consider about these is the length. I am 5'7" and these are very long on me. You'll have to throw on a pair of heels unless you're Taylor Swift. (And, if you ARE Taylor Swift...Hi Taylor! Big Fan!!!)

I did not expect to like these so much - my go to jeans are usually from The Loft and have the typical button, zipper situation. These jeans are pull on - no zipper and only a fake button but this is actually REALLY nice because now I don't have to unbutton or zip if they're feeling a bit too tight, you know? I have regular Spanx garments and these are not like those - the compression just feels like skinny jeans up top but the flare on the bottom is super cute. I rolled them up once on the cuff to keep them from getting destroyed but they're the perfect length for a boot. I didn't notice it unti I wore them but there's a very slight mothball odor. I have no doubts it will abate once I wash them and it's not enough to make me take them off. I'm wearing them as I type this on a computer and it's not digging into my stomach nor leaving a big gap in the back. For reference, I'm 5'5", 140 lbs with a medium booty and I got the medium. When you see them, they look small but pulling them on, they fit like a glove.

These were essentially what I was looking for, a lightweight, comfy, denim pull on but not quite a home run. FABRIC and STRETCH The fabric is NOT stretchy, only the waistband has stretch. Of course there will be color variation, but the pics show a more weathered, distressed look than the mine. SIZING and FIT: I tried both XL and 2X and there's not a whole lot of difference between the two as far as I can tell, but there's more room in the hip and thigh in the 2X. The fit isn't super flattering, especially from the back, but that could be because I have a relatively big, round, bum so it isn't as gathered back there as it is in the model pics. I think I'd probably need a 3X (which they don't have) to get that cute extra baggy look. OVERALL: For reference, I usually wear about a size 16 in pants. I'm plus sized (50-44-48) with a large bust and bum but no hips. I carry most of my weight in my belly. So while these will fit a plus sized body, they fit more like a regular relaxed jean than a baggy/slouchy jean. If I keep them, I will probably go with the smaller size (XL) because they're slightly more flattering as a relaxed jean, but if I could, I'd prefer to size up to get the slouchy, oversized look like the model.

Judy Blue Women's Mid Rise Bootcut Jeans 82337 These jeans are super soft and comfortable but unfortunately I will have to return them because they’re incredibly long. My son who is 6’3” joked and said they could probably fit him and sure enough, they fit him perfectly. This is him in the photo.

Pros: I love this brand and style and have two pair—one green and one khaki. The style is cute and fits me great (usual size 6) given my rectangular figure. I love that they are a fitted high waist that doesn’t require me to belt them. There is enough stretch for me to have them snug but not that it affects how they look. I love this particular denim color and the fabric is a nice weight. At 5’5” and average leg length, they fit in a way I can wear flats or low boots. Perfect for me. Cons: Really I don’t have much to say in terms of negatives except I wish we could move on from this ugly distressed stuff. I love the color of these jeans, but I hate the distressing so much! On these jeans the back distressing is my biggest negative. It looks like I sat in something. ☹️ I’ve ordered a different pair of jeans in hopes of finding something else I can love.

I’m 5’6” 130 pounds and 6 fit well. Crotch hangs a tad low but I wanted the loose look/feel. The fit is good in all places, good length for comfy sneaks or lower heeled sandals. Was good fit around the waist without a belt but belts go great with these too. Can dress up or down. Good quality. Would recommend.

I ordered this jacket in ‘Blue’. It’s a faded blue and doesn’t have worn spots as shown in the ‘Washed Blue’ color. It’s neatly stitched with contrasting orange thread and is a traditional full cut characteristic of denim jackets. The pockets are deep and form two large inner pockets that would securely hold a phone. The label states that the fabric is 99% cotton and 1% spandex, which varies from the online information. The spandex results in a mildly stretchy fabric that allows for movement. Machine wash cold and tumble dry low.I’m 5’4” tall, about 122 pounds with a 37 inch bust, 27.5 inch waist and 38 inch hips. I ordered size small and it looks oversized and shapeless. I plan to wash and dry it in hopes that it will shrink a bit. The hem falls at the top of my thighs and it’s very roomy through the body. The sleeve length is perfect and the shoulder seams fall 1 inch past my shoulders. If you’re looking for a full cut then you’ll be happy with this well constructed jacket.

Ok these are fire 🔥 First let me start off by saying I weigh around 225-229, and I’m 5’2. I bought the XL just to be on the safer side and, like other reviews have said, I could have gone with a large and been just fine. The waist band stretches! These are extremely comfortable! I bought the black pair, I plan on wearing them to a $uicideBoy$ concert in September. I was hoping they didn’t have the frayed look with the holes, cause the picture on Amazon shows the model wearing the black pair with no holes, well they have them so unfortunately I can’t wear them to work (pretty strict uniform policy, no frayed, hole jeans allowed). But I absolutely love them! They aren’t super heavy feeling. Actually feel pretty light weight. I love all the pockets! Just be aware the pockets in the front with the buttons, the buttons are just a design, they don’t actually clip closed. The material seems well made, we shall see how they hold up at a concert. I’ll probably wear them out and about just to break them in. Honestly I might buy my sister a pair and maybe even another pair for myself, in a smaller size. They seem like great quality for the price. They are super sexy though honestly, I like that I can adjust the length too with the button straps at the bottom.

Love love loveee the quality of the material. It is a cute cropped long sleeve. It’s thick enough to be comfortable, but thin enough to layer with a jacket. and the brown color is so cute.! For reference: I am 5’2, 115 pounds & my bra size is 32A. I got an extra small because of my small chest and becaude I prefer a tighter fit.

Great shirt. Color is as pictured. Brown/grey= warm taupe. The sleeves are not too tight on me which I usually have a problem with. The length hits exactly at my waist. The material is somewhat thin, but not too thin. It is very stretchy. I am 5'4", 130 lbs, 34DD, and purchased size medium. I would recommend.

I HATE buying pants of any kind because I have thick thighs and I'm short. My main hesitations were that they would be too tight in the thighs and too long. The fit was AMAZING and the fabric is super soft!. I was tempted to buy a size up because I was so concerned it wouldn't fit my thighs, but after reading some reviews, I decided to order my usual size. I did roll them up a few times to get my desired length. I typically alter my pants because the legs are nearly always too long, even in petite! What I like is they come already rolled up, so if you need to make adjustments, it looks completely natural. I bought these in a 12. My height is 5' and weight is 163. Would definitely buy these again!

This mini skirt from EMMMIOL is really nice. I am loving this denim miniskirt! First of all, I love the wash of the denim that I chose (Dark Blue). I love that there are shorts underneath. The shorts aren't bulky and they are really short compared to the skirt, so you don't really see them. I love the two slits in the front of the skirt. They add a sexy touch. I love the fading in front and in back that gives it a used or destroyed look. The back is the perfect length, and it has two functional pockets. This mini skirt has a side zipper closure. The material is made of cotton, polyester and spandex. The perfect mix IMO. It's lightweight for denim and not bulky. I think this mini skirt is both comfortable and flattering.This mini skirt is true to size. I'm between a small and medium in bottoms right now. Because I didn't want this mini skirt to fit loose, I ordered the small. I have to put in a bit of effort to zip up the closure which was expected but other than that it fits fine. For reference, I am 5'7", 28" waist, 36" hip.At the time of this review, you have your choice of three different washes and the price for this mini skirt is $19.99 which I think is more than reasonable.
reviewer_name_raw: Mini skirt has short shorts underneath!

user_comment: Love this skirt. It is super cute and stylish with that old school Y2k look I grew up with! Even though it is a bit short and trendy, I still feel like women of all ages can wear it if they are comfortable with the length. It doesn't feel that "mini" to me but it is on the shorter side. It is a skort which makes all the difference and makes it comfortable to wear! The denim feels high quality, is softer to the touch and has just the right stretch. It zips up from the side and the only thing I think could make it better is if it had a button at the top of the zipper. If it fit tightly, I worry the zipper could slip down. I am 5'4 and typically a size 3-5. I am typically a small but got a medium and am glad I did b/c it fits well and I think the small would have been a bit too tight. I got the dark blue and it is a very nice color. The current listing price of $21.99 seems like a good price!

My wife loves denim shorts but didn't have any denim skirts, so when I saw this I knew she'd appreciate it. And she does - loves this skirt! Having the built in shorts seals the deal considering how short the skirt is (would leave you very exposed with bending slightly). And guess what, it has 2 actual pockets! That's a nice perk. As for material, these feel and look like quality. Despite being denim, my wife says they don't make her feel hot, and they're very comfortable with a bit of stretch. The size zipper makes for easy on/off action. They do run a bit small so keep that in mind. She's 5'6", 135lb, and they're close to being too small on her. And to reiterate, they are quite short. All in all, great skirt/short combo that makes for an eye-catching look.

I recently ordered this woman's mini skirt, and it is very cute. The fabric is really soft, stretchy, and breathable. I ordered a size 4 (I'm 137 lbs and just over 5 feet 7 inches tall), thinking it would be more than enough after having four kids, including twins. However, it turned out to be tight. I usually carry weight in my hips and thighs, so this might just be my experience, but it felt much tighter than I would prefer for a size 4. I believe that if I go up one size, it will be perfect. As an '80s kid, I love the 90's look and feel - it's not obvious and cheesy, just enough with the smooth side zipper (kinda hidden), stylish side slit, and the back pockets. Oh, and I love the built-in shorts for coverage! It is a low-to-mid-low-waisted skirt that pairs well with various tops for both casual and dressy occasions, with or without heels. It's easy to wash, just toss it in the wash. Overall, it's a little more expensive than I'd prefer, but the quality, softness, and fit feel great.

Just sent back med.which is the size recommended for the size of jeans i have. Way to big. So i got small. But there is way to much material in the but and the legs are longer than I am. I'm 5'4" 138 pds. I will be sending these back also.

Nice quality jeans. I am 5ft 5in. 157lbs bought sz med. I am a no rear end gal. I have to use a belt to hold them up and they are about 4inches to long even with ankle boots. Really need hemmed. They are wider legged than I assumed. More of a bell bottom to me than flare. It took weeks to get them.

Okay so, just to be fully upfront, they aren't the most flattering shorts in the world. But they aren't meant to be. They're meant to be modest, practical and comfortable, and by God are they. They're the ideal "mom shorts". I'm 5'7. My waist is 30 inches, my hips are 40, and my thighs are pretty big. So I almost never order clothes, especially bottoms, online. They are nearly guaranteed to fit poorly. So I was skeptical about these shorts. I've never found a pair of shorts that doesn't ride up, have half my butt hanging out, or gap uncomfortably at the waist. But I was desperate for a pair of shorts that fit properly, so I took the risk. I'm so glad I did. They fit PERFECTLY. they're stretchy, long, and comfortable. I've never had a pair of shorts that fully covers me like these do, and I am over the moon for them. They're lightweight and so comfy. As an added bonus, the pockets on these are INSANE. I've never had such big pockets on any piece of clothing. Just to prove my point, I put a full sized 16.9 fl oz water bottle in one pocket, and it fit EASILY. Picture for proof. I can not recommend these shorts enough. The size chart is actually accurate for a change, so just go based off the size chart and you'll be golden! And just as a note, they're not real jean/denim material, they're much more stretchy and lightweight (which I'm not complaining about at all).

They're the ideal "mom shorts". I'm 5'7. My waist is 30 inches, my hips are 40, and my thighs are pretty big. So I almost never order clothes, especially bottoms, online. They are nearly guaranteed to fit poorly. So I was skeptical about these shorts. I've never found a pair of shorts that doesn't ride up, have half my butt hanging out, or gap uncomfortably at the waist. But I was desperate for a pair of shorts that fit properly, so I took the risk. I'm so glad I did. They fit PERFECTLY. they're stretchy, long, and comfortable. I've never had a pair of shorts that fully covers me like these do, and I am over the moon for them. They're lightweight and so comfy. As an added bonus, the pockets on these are INSANE. I've never had such big pockets on any piece of clothing. Just to prove my point, I put a full sized 16.9 fl oz water bottle in one pocket, and it fit EASILY. Picture for proof. I can not recommend these shorts enough. The size chart is actually accurate for a change, so just go based off the size chart and you'll be golden! And just as a note, they're not real jean/denim material, they're much more stretchy and lightweight (which I'm not complaining about at all).

I love the Women's Luvamia Jean Shorts for comfort! The fabric is a soft denim with lots of stretch. For reference, I am 5'3", 135-140 lbs and usually wear size 8. I ordered a medium for a loose comfy fit. The legs have a wide opening and the waist is high. The pockets are big enough that my smart phone fits easily. The zipper is heavy-duty. Overall, they are very well made and I would not hesitate to re-order in different colors.

Levi's, Womens, Wedgie Boot I am 5’3 126lbs… I ordered size 27 long. The fit is spot on! 👌 Nice thick denim with tiny stretch. The rise is not to high, hits right at the belly button on me. Not to tight and not to loose. I was worried about ordering the size long because I am short, but glad I did. They did shrink in length just a tiny bit but still able to wear 2-4 inch shoes with a heel and still look long! I don’t normally do reviews but when buying, I like to read reviews as it is very . So anyone wondering 🤔 if they should order and worried about the fit or if they would fit, I thought I’d be too and leave a review!✌🏻 I have A LOT of jeans but this fit is the best out of all!

I REALLY wanted to love these shorts, but they simply don’t fit me. The button style with high waist is very cute, and the wash is pretty too. The leg openings are huge even on the size small. They have stretch to them but also seem thick enough so I think the quality is good and comfortable too, just won’t work for someone with slim thighs.

This pair of jeans from Sidefeel are well made. The material is soft and of a medium thickness. They have 2% elastane in them, so there is some stretch to get them more comfortable when the sizes are borderline. I ordered them in a size 16, my normal size. The waist is a bit snug, but with the stretch in the material it's not too snug. My waist is 33-ish and hips are 44", so according to the size chart farther down the item page, these should not have been snug on my waist. The rest of the fit is comfy for me. I'm 5' 4" tall and the length is okay for me. I love the raw hem on them! It just looks good with the style of this pair of jeans. The pockets on the front are styled differently and really add a nice touch to the look. They are full sized pockets, as are the back pockets, too. The buttonholes are perfectly sized so that the pants can be buttoned up or unbuttoned quickly. My arthritic fingers had no problem with using the buttons. Over all, I do like this pair of jeans and will recommend them. They are of good quality and have a nice looking style to them. The current price (7-15-2025) of $36.99 is fair, and currently there is also a coupon for a discount to make them a great value. There are other colors to choose from, aside from the Blue that I ordered. I really like the shade of blue on the jeans I received, though.
reviewer_name_raw: Nice, medium weight pair of jeans.

CAUTION on washing these…..I would have given 5 stars but the grommets have started to come loose around the denim and my husband has had to use his carpentry tools to get the correct curve on the grommet so that they will GRAB the fabric properly. I am in Canada and ordered these while in the US at a friends and I ordered Large and XL. The difference between the two is like a small to a Large. It makes NO sense. There is clearly NO quality control when it comes to consistency as I am not the ONLY review to say this. The XL was HUGE and the L was snug but perfect PLUS they do give after about 15 min of wearing them. THAT was fine with me but my BFF bought the M and L and the difference between her L and my L was 2 entire INCHES. Again, QUALITY CONTROL and CONSISTENCY. For a Free PPL look (erhem) in the design these are spot on and the shape is not NEARLY as “barrel” legged as some I have seen which is better as there are some where the barrel leg literally looks as if they are wearing barrels on each leg. NOT attractive. May be comfortable but NOT attractive. So, I am going to do what I find makes me CRAZIER THAN ANYTHING when it comes to reviews and people say the above and that is it. Well great. What if that person is the same weight and height but an XL fits them like a glove and the L fits ME like a glove? Saying ur weight and height does NOTHING to help me chose my size. I was fortunate to be able to order two and send back one but not everyone can do that so when I give a review about clothes I give MEASUREMENTS because anything else is ridiculous and I wish sellers would give “lay flat” measurements as well as the “drop” so I know just HOW “high waisted” a pair of jeans will be on ME. So I DO need to know what the measurement from the crotch to the top of the button is as well as the back side. Same goes with the width of the ankle. Makes me nuts. So, I shall NOT do that to you because these are great jeans. The only issue is that now my husband has had to spend HOURS squeezing each grommet so it grabs the material and does not fray out of the grommet (QUALITY CONTROL AGAIN!!!) they are great but in hindsight as I did this with my denim Free People ones that are this style and it worked well so I should have known. I put the grommet top of jean (remove string) into a delicate bag and use clips to hold it shut so it is not banged around like crazy in the dryer or the washer. BOTH are front loads if that matters. I do NOT dry them 100% in dryer. I only get dampness out and then hang to dry. Just FYI and I suggest you do that as well. They do need to be washed INSIDE OUT as the print is NOT on the inside which is CHEAP and I should take a star off for that alone. It takes NOTHING to put the same print on the inside. NOTHING. Ok, here we go, get ur tape measure. Jeans Laying flat: Waist is 17 and 1/4” without me stretching them as there are stretch insterts. Inseam is 26 1/2 “ but remember that these sit lower on the waist so depending on how you wear them will depend on where they sit at your ankle. DROP: front is from the “x” where the crotch is to the top of the material above the button is 12” BACK DROP: 17 1/2” but the way one of my photos shows, it appears as if the back is longer than that as it looks higher and I have a BOOTY and no plumbers crack when I bend over so YAAAAHHH! Now MY measurements: Waist: 38” and yes at my actual waist.the skinnny part, not the low or the mom jean high, the actual TAILORS mark of where a waist is. Inseam from crotch to floor:28 1/2” and they sit on me so I CAN wear a 1” to a 1 1/2” boot no problem with them looking like floods but that is how I wear them. Drop…can’t really do that one if u get my drift. There is some stretch to them AFTER you wash them. If u do NOT want ANY shrinkage from ur first wear or u need some “stretchage” the DO NOT PLACE IN DRYER AT ALL AND DRY THEM INSIDE OUT FOR 1/2 the time then right side out for the rest. HOPEFULLY photos will help… NOT that it matters as yes, on the BMI I am “obese” but in the Dunk Tank when competing, I am not so that tells you how dense my bones are and my “fat” is GREY and not adipose or white (bad) fat. I am 5’ 3” and 178lbs. I’ll have lost 10 -15 by end of March because of training but this is how they fit NOW and how I am NOW. Yes I have my 1 1/2” boot on with them. I REALLY hope this helps you all. REMEMBER TO TAKE OUT STRING and I am changing mine to a LONG sneaker string that is the exact green in Taffeta and it looks FAB when I am thinner and wear a short shirt. Just saying. *These JUST came out of the wash as I got them FILTHY dirty at the Range so I HAD to wash them as I do not wash Denim every time I wear them so they have not “given” anywhere and right now are TIGHT but the XL would have fit another 1/2 of me and my GF took the L she received which were PERFECT On me so remember, if you CAN, do get two of same size or one of two sizes as there isn’t any continuity. All in all when they “give” and are not so tight at the dang hips and hang properly after an hour of wearing, I get compliments galore so there MUST Be something to them plus I warned you, I DO have a BOOTY! IF the XL would have been what it was supposed to be, I MIGHT have gone for them and had them taken in as it appears that no matter the size ALL of the jeans were the same inseam, just an FYI…… Good luck and hopefully I will remember to put up a photo when my training is done so u can see the PROPER fit. Again, watch those grommets. My apologies about placing the cheetah on a patterned cut carpet LOL, realized after that it’s almost camo LOL!!! PS: I have NEVER put photos of my body (in good, great or horrific shape) up on ANY site so I hope this helps cuz it is HARD to hit send but I can not complain if I am not part of the solution. So here goes……

I am 5ft 4inches and 161 lbs. I wear a Large in most brands, and ordered a large inch these pants. I barely squeezed into them! These absolutely run atleast one size small, maybe 2! I can't get over how tight these are in the waist! At any rate, I got them on. Immediately noticed another issue I've seen in some other jeans. The zipper comes unzipped at the top, as well as opens a bit. I have found that typically has to do with the make of the pants. No doubt the snug fit does not help the issue though. I do not like the look that gives. You can see in some of my photos. The pants are stretchy. The length is nice. As a short gal it is hard to find pants that are not ridiculously long, so that was a pleasant find with these. The ripped look is actually a bit hard to even see. Unless I bend my legs, the rips are so tight they stay closed, and because they are black they are not easily seen. Overall I like the way they look on me. I just wish they weren't so small because it makes them extremely uncomfortable due to the tightness.

I was relieved these fit, and so pleasantly surprised by these jeans! I wear them several times a week now. I am a size 14 currently, with Old Navy 14 getting a little loose (but Old Navy runs big). These fit me perfectly and don't dig into the waist too much, but I'm also not pulling them up all the time. I like that there is very little stretch (maybe none), but I would say they are on the thinner side. Not structured enough to do much for my lower belly, but to be honest not many pants do. I'm an apple shape right now, and a lot of pants fit well in one area but not another. These have worked well for me overall. If you have a tiny waist and wider hips, I'm guessing they would gape more. I like that they are not wide and not skinny, have interesting pockets, and I did not have to hem them (I'm 5'3''), which I generally have to do.
reviewer_name_raw: Nothing fancy, but a win overall!

I’m not typically a ‘reviewer’, but I love it! Perfect fit (XXL). Accurate color, sail blue. Nice weight and quality denim, not bulky or thin denim-like cotton. Love the details. Well made, tight seams/stitching. Flowed well, not boxy and unflattering or too huggy on my curves (5’5”, 220lbs).

I absolutely love this denim dress. The L fit perfectly. 5’3” & 168 lbs. It wasn’t boxy and not real heavy so I felt comfortable in it at a birthday party we attended. It did stretch out a bit through the evening. The only negative, was how bad it smelled when I received it. I had to wash it 3 times to remove the strong odor.

I absolutely love love love these pants! I know everyone's saying that they're short but I'm a shorty, I'm 5'4 and they fit me perfectly. They are so fun and whenever I wear them I get a ton of compliments.

*Update - I have worn this shirt a few times now. It washes easily (hang to dry) and does not lose its shape nor shrink. I was pleasantly surprised by this shirt! I’m 5’6”, 130 lbs, and the medium fits perfectly. Even though my three children are grown, I’m still self-conscious about my lingering mom belly and outtie bellybutton. I usually avoid tops that are too clingy because they tend to highlight that area. When I first pulled this shirt out of the bag, it looked way too small and tight, but I was completely wrong. The fabric is thick, soft, and stretchy in all the right ways. It conceals my tummy and even flatters that area, which is a huge win for me. The cute cutout detail adds just the right amount of flair to an otherwise basic black shirt, making it easy to dress up or down. I can see myself wearing this with slacks for work or pairing it with jeans for a casual look. For under $10, this shirt is a total steal!

I very rarely make Amazon reviews but these jeans are just too good not to say something! They truly feel like real jean material, not see through, and are nice and thick to hide any cellulite- which I appreciate. With the colder months here I’m confident they are thick enough to keep me warm, and once the temps REALLY drop (since I live in the northern U.S.) I’m sure a pair of fleece lined leggings will fit underneath well. At the same time, they are also very stretchy and comfortable. I am 25 weeks pregnant, 5’6 and currently 197lbs (185 pre pregnancy) I typically carry most of my weight in my butt and thighs. I got the large and they fit perfectly and are stretchy enough to allow me room to grow. It feels amazing to try on a pair of jeans and just smile bc everything fits so well after so many weeks/months of feeling like a frumpy blob lol pre-pregnancy I was used to buying brands like Bridge by Glee and Flying Monkey, which are designed to really lift and flatter the booty. These jeans are definitely not *that* flattering on the butt, but they don’t do me a disservice in that area either. I’m just glad to have something that fits and is on trend at this point. Giving bonus points bc they have real front pockets too! These jeans are definitely worth the cost and are something I plan to keep to wear through future pregnancies as well. Overall, they function well and will serve their purpose well through the rest of my pregnancy. I imagine they will even be nice to wear in my post-partum period also. The light wash that I have here is great for casual, everyday wear and I’m going to go and buy a second pair in a darker wash as soon as I’m done writing this post. If you’re curvy with hips and thighs like me, don’t hesitate to buy these jeans. They’re perfect! P.S. ignore what appears to be discoloration in one of the pictures. I took these photos in dim lighting and I think my phone was just trying to auto-adjust to make the photo appear brighter. These jeans are not discolored :)

Levi's Women's Ribcage Bell Bottom Jeans UPDATE: The sizing chart shows that if you are normally a size 28 to order their size 27. I got the black in a 27 and they fit PERFECT!! They just have a weird jean smell since they're dyed. But there is no gaps with the 27. They are still baggy though, so if you have thin legs, these will be more of a wide leg jean rather than a bell. I don't have a pic but i wore them today and yesterday and they are comfortable and staying up just fine, not tight around my waist/hips at all. Very glad I stuck with a 27 for these ones. ORIGINAL REVIEW: Not sure why everyone is saying to size up.. I stuck with my normal size (28) and they left a huge gap. I have never bought anything smaller than a 28. I normally wear a size 6/7 jean. 28W/32L, my hips are 33", I am roughly 5'7" 125lbs. These were big. If you are built like me and have a tiny waist, huge hips, and no butt, you should probably stick to your normal size or size down. If I would've went with the review/Amazon suggestions, they would've been 3 sizes too big. Luckily, I was able to shrink them just a hair to make them not look gigantic on me. Length was perfect though! They aren't as shaping in the butt as I would like them to be, I will be ordering these in black in a size 27 to see if they fit better.

I bought the oatmeal heather in size small for $11.90. I am 5’7 and 125 lbs, and it fits like a glove, which might not be too great for a hoodie. It’s not tight, it’s just right, but I am wanting a roomier fit, so I’m going to return the small and order a medium. I’m also worried that if it shrinks in the wash, it won’t fit anymore, but it has 40% polyester in it so I don’t think it’s going to shrink. I was pleasantly surprised that the quality is nice, it’s not too thin, and it’s soft. The hood portion has two layers of fabric. Very good value for the money. Side note: seems like the person who tried it on before me owns a black cat, there’s lots of little black cat hairs on the inside. I’m not too upset about it, but Amazon should be a little more thorough in checking returns. UPDATE: Sweater didn’t shrink in the wash. Wore it multiple times, very comfortable. Unfortunately, a big downside is the soft interior lining sheds A LOT. If you wear a black shirt underneath, you will get sweater fluff on your shirt. Maybe it will diminish with each wash, I don’t know yet, but that also means the sweater is losing its fibers.

Levi Strauss Signature Gold Womens Maternity Baby Bump Skinny Jeans I adore this pair of jeans!!!! In this pic I’m 30 weeks along and weigh roughly 150lbs and got a medium. I love that they fit snug and are form flattering but are also stretchy and extremely soft and comfortable. My husband said my butt looks great and let’s be honest, anything that makes you feel remotely normal and confident when pregnant is a must have! The back pocket fit my phone just fine. Other reviews mentioned having to pull them up periodically but I didn’t notice that as an issue personally and I’m moderately active. That might be more of an issue if you need to size up for a looser fit, but I couldn’t say. Great purchase in my opinion!

Levi Strauss Signature Gold Womens Maternity Baby Bump Skinny Jeans I usually never buy clothes online because I'll never know if they will fit, but I'm so glad I took a chance with these jeans. Amazon was recommending I go with a size small because I typically wear a size 28 (size 6 or 7 in Old Navy), and I'm so glad I went with a medium based on existing reviews and the minimal difference between size small and medium according to the size chart. I'm a runner, just shy of 5'4'', and my pre-pregnancy weight was around 138-139 pounds. I'm now 18 weeks and my weight has only gone up about 4 pounds, but I was desperate to find some maternity jeans as I was getting increasingly uncomfortable from the pressure resting on my lower abdomen, especially when sitting in a car. I normally wear size 28 (size 6 or 7 in some brands like Old Navy). The inseam length of this size medium is perfect. No area on the jeans feels too tight or too loose. I love how high these sit on the hips because it feels like they aren't going to slip down. I expect there may be some minor shrinkage when I wash them so it may feel a little bit tighter post-wash, but they seem stretchy enough to continue feeling comfortable. I think these will also be awesome postpartum jeans. One thing I was concerned about was that the waistband wouldn't be supportive enough, especially as I get closer to the third trimester, but I simply decided to purchase a separate maternity band for extra support and to wear with workout pants or dresses. Overall, this is a great buy and I recommend for any mamas that are feeling uncomfortable!

Looks exactly like the pic. This denim shirt is REALLY cute overall, however, it's a little tight in the chest and the elastic around the arms was a bit tight as well. I ordered a size large (I'm 5'6" & 175 lbs), but after trying it on I decided to also order an XL to see which one fits me better. I plan to keep whichever one fits best and return the other. Unfortunately shipping was NOT quick, but I really do think the shirt is flattering, so it's worth the wait. The denim feels like good quality, fairly thick, like a pair of jeans. Only CON is that I noticed that the top string on one side is sewn slightly lower the one on the other side (see pics). Not a huge deal, but I removed 1 star for lack of attention to detail. With that said, I would absolutely recommend to others.

I got the wide leg jeans in color, Z Dark Blue with a frayed hem. I chose this style because it doesn’t have the line on the back like the other jeans. I went off the size chart and chose a size 14. I am 5'8" and 165 pounds. I feel these jeans run a little bigger than the size chart, but after washing and drying them, they shrank to a better fit that I’m happy with. I could have maybe gone down to a size 12. The jeans have some stretch, and I found them comfortable to wear, plus they look chic. The jeans are in a length different than I'm used to, but they still look cute. I may fold the hem up to see if I like that better. I think if you’re probably 5'6" or below, these would be normal size jeans for shorter legs. Overall, I think these are cute for summertime.

I've been looking for some comfortable jeans, and some wide leg jeans, and these looked like they fit the bill. Unfortunately, they do not fit this body that well! This is the first time ever I've tried something on and my husband commented that it wasn't flattering and he didn't think I should keep it. Usually he likes everything. He said: "This makes everything that should look good look bad, from a flat butt to hiding your waist." It might be my fault. I am 5'9", I weigh 150 pounds and I purchased size Large to make sure they fit my hips, but they do seem to large in the waist/hips on me. But these pants ARE oddly cut - they look wide legged in the promo photos, but they do go in a little below the knees, making them more of a flare or bootcut effect than a pure wide or stovepipe leg. These jeans were also about 2-3" too short on me, so it's possible the cut/flare is just all wrong for my proportions and they would look better on a shorter woman - possibly someone 5'4-5'6". Quality wise they are fine. The material feels reasonable, like a stretch denim, and the sewing and construction is good. There ARE pockets, front and back, which I like. I will say, when I read "sweatpant jeans" I imagine a soft, fuzzy material on the inside and an overall soft fit. These were more your standard stretch denim with an elasticated waistband. They feel like stretchy jeans, not sweatpants. That's fine but I do think that maybe "Sweatpant" is misleading, that and the fit are why I give these three stars instead of five.

5 MONTH OLD REVIEW two months ago these would have been 5 star. Today, after less than ten washes these are basically trash. I’m not a tall person, 5’3”. These things have gone from a good fit to no longer wearable. They are so short! You can see how dorky they look in the photo. There’s just no way I can make these wearable. They’ve shrunk beyond what fits now and are basically trash, not to mention the back pocket has ripped as well. When I first got these I loved them, it was nice baggy fit. They’re easily 2” above my ankle now and the waist has shrunk as well. I don’t expect $30 pants to last years but a good year or two would have been nice. Save your money and invest in a good quality denim.

These things have gone from a good fit to no longer wearable. They are so short! You can see how dorky they look in the photo. There’s just no way I can make these wearable. They’ve shrunk beyond what fits now and are basically trash, not to mention the back pocket has ripped as well. When I first got these I loved them, it was nice baggy fit. They’re easily 2” above my ankle now and the waist has shrunk as well. I don’t expect $30 pants to last years but a good year or two would have been nice. Save your money and invest in a good quality denim.

They look cuter in the Amazon pic compared to real life. They are very comfortable and true to size - I’m a us size 8, usually size medium, 155lbs 5’6 and these fit, but I wish they were a little bit tighter to help suck it all in. I definitely would not size up. The thing I dislike most about these shorts is the fake rips in the back, it looks like the butt is dirty. Not sure if I’ll keep em, I might if I can get them to shrink a little

I will specially wear these with a sweatshirt and maybe an oversized tee shirt. Can’t say they would be cute with anything else at least on me. I am 5’8 and 150lbs wearing a large in the picture. The shorts are extremely comfortable, soft and nothing is tight. I also ordered a medium because these are bordering on too loose. The way I intend to wear them I still think they will be fine.Update: I could not even button the . A drastic size difference.

Pros: They snuggly fit my super slender frame and are very flattering. I've tried 6 different brands of maternity jeans and this is the one and only type that has fit me. Cons: my only complaint so far is that they fall down a LOT!! I'm constantly pulling them up. For reference: I am 5'5", 98lbs pre pregnancy, 117lbs now at 30 weeks, with hips around 34 inches.

My dimensions are 5'3", 130 lbs, waist 28 -29", hips 40" and I ordered the green in medium (US 8 - 10) . The shorts have a 7" inseam which offers a modest coverage when sitting or bending over. The material is 98% Cotton, 2% Elastane which makes the shorts breathable and flexible when sitting, walking or relaxing on the couch. The high waisted cut is figure slimming, but also does not cut into my waist when bending due to the Elastane content. I mention this because sometimes high waisted garments can be restrictive. The size I ordered has a loose fit but is not baggy as the material remains close to your figure, as shown in the advertisement. Given that these are 98% cotton I plan on cold washing and cool drying to prevent shrinking. I find the medium true to size and the color green as shown in the advertisement. I wear these shorts with flip flops or tennis shoes, but I imagine they would pair well with boots too. Although these shorts come with 5 pockets there's not a lot of room to store objects in them. Perhaps, a credit card or a key would fit. I ordered the medium due to my measurements and I wanted a form fitting look. My hip width is at the limit for medium, and I could have also ordered the large for a looser fit. I highly recommend reviewing the seller's size chart. I have an athletic build and find that wearing vertical stripes can visually elongate my body, making me appear taller and slimmer. They help draw the eye downward, which can create a more streamlined look. Vertical stripes have benefits for apple, hourglass, and pear shape figures too. Apple Shape: For those with a fuller midsection, vertical stripes help divert attention from the waist. Hourglass Shape: Individuals with an hourglass figure can wear vertical stripes to enhance their natural curves. Pear Shape: Vertical stripes on the lower half can help balance wider hips by elongating the legs. I would consider these shorts a Summer wardrobe staple.

The denim on this dress is a heavyweight denim. It is quality construction, and the stitching is great. It has pockets on each side and they are fairly big and fully functional, which is really a plus for me. I gave it four stars because it is kind of boxy shaped. There isn’t much indentation of the waist so it just hangs like a bag. Also, I feel like it is really oversized. I tried to style it with a belt, but it really didn’t help much. I am 5 foot seven and 120 pounds and I ordered a small and it is way too big. I would definitely size down unless you have an apple shaped figure.

These jeans are super trendy and comfortable with a nice wide-leg fit and soft denim. I love the casual, relaxed vibe, and they’re great for all-day wear. However, there was no sizing chart, so I had to guess—and I ended up with the wrong size, so likely they are using Asian sizing. They also aren’t the most flattering from the back. Still a stylish option if you can get the right fit! Please note the lengthy - 5’3” and plenty long - I’d say the XL is probably about a US size 12, and probably a 30” I seam.
reviewer_name_raw: Cute Style but Sizing Requires Guesswork

user_comment: For reference and sizing: I have a 28-29 inch waist and 36DDD bust, so I ordered an XL. The fabric has some nice stretch, and I probably could have sized down to a Large for a closer fit overall. In the photos, I'm holding the back to show there's no gapping at the chest when buttoned—huge win! The front buttons are real metal (not cheap snaps), and I was thrilled that it actually fit comfortably across the bust without any fussing or worries. For larger-bosomed women, this is a definite plus-the chest sizing feels spot-on. It was a little loose around the waist, but honestly, that made it super comfortable and easy to wear all day. I paired it with my tie-waist blue jeans, and the colors matched perfectly. The peplum hem is a nice touch-it covers the tie without it showing, and it gives the vest a more polished, feminine shape. I love the cap sleeves-they elevate the look and make it feel dressier than a basic vest. Living in a warmer climate, I can wear this almost year-round (great for layering or standalone). When I wore it out today, I got multiple compliments and was told I looked "ready for the office”-in the best way! Like the model photos, I plan to style it with white pants next. Very cute, well-made, sized correctly for the bust, and super versatile. I give it five stars!

I love this top and I am sorry if my dimly lit bathroom photo is not doing it justice! I will try to take a better photo later. I love this denim top so much, if I could give it more stars I would! I am a bit busty, and I was worried that there would be gaping in between the buttons and the bust might be too tight while the waist was too loose, this was not the case at all. It definitely accommodates larger busts while also accentuating the waist. For reference I am 5'5", 115 lbs, and a 32DD bust, and I am wearing the size small. I would say this is more of a cap sleeve top than a vest. It is a medium weight strechy denim that is soft and breathable. The button holes were a little tight at first, but I would rather that then have them popping open. Overall, this top far surpassed my expectations and if you are thinking about getting this, you absolutely should!

These pants are perfect for shorties! I’m 5’0” and was able to wear them with a pair of heels without having to get them altered. They are a matte black faux leather. No smell. High rise and comfortable. I’m typically a 00/24 in pants and these were slightly too big on me, but still looked decent. Highly recommend.

luvamia Denim Vest Women Trendy Button Down Braided Adjustable Waist Western Casual Sleeveless Jean Waistcoat Vests Top Luvamia is quickly becoming my favorite brand. They produce a lot of different styles and sizes, and this trendy vests no exception. It is very well made with denim that is not too thick and not too thin. It's quite comfortable... not heavy at all. The braidwork on it is a really nice touch. It is a bit on the smaller side. I am 5'3" 150 pounds. I usually fit in a medium with a bit extra room, but I could not button my medium sized vest. I suggest sizing up. This vest would look great with jeans or a skirt. It would also go very well with cowboy boots.

luvamia Denim Vest Women Trendy Button Down Braided Adjustable Waist Western Casual Sleeveless Jean Waistcoat Vests Top This is a really nice denim vest! It's well made of 88.4% cotton, 6.2% regenerated fibre, 5.4% polyester. There is little to no stretch in the fabric. It's soft and of a slightly less than medium thickness. I did not find any flaws in the sewing or materials. The 2 front pockets are small, but will easily hold tissues, keys, or a credit card. It has a buckle strap on the lower back for adjusting the fit. The style and the braided trim look great. I got the Wategos Blue color and it matches the color of the images on my laptop. I ordered this vest in a size XXL, with XL being my regular size. Since there wasn't any elastane or spandex listed as part of the fabric composition, I wanted to be sure it wouldn't be too tight. I prefer airflow room with my tops. It fits a bit loosely, but I have yet to adjust the buckle strap on the back to improve how it fits me. There was a bit of extra room at the side bust area for me, so hopefully with adjustment that will take up the slack. I'm okay with the XXL because if I had ordered the XL, it likely would gap between the buttons at the chest area, on me. For reference: I'm 5' 4" tall, 42 B or C (depends on the bra), 33-ish waist, 44" hips, and 182 lbs. Vests of this type are not something I usually wear, but the design and trim on this one made it irresistible, so I had to try it. I was pleasantly surprised to find that I like it a lot! I'm so glad that I decided to try this vest and I highly recommend it! The vest is good quality and really comfortable to wear. There are shades of blue, a candy pink, washed black, and cream white available for color choices. The current price (2-8-2026) of $29.99 is a fair value, I think. At this time, there is also a coupon being offered to reduce the price and make it an even better value! I would certainly order one of these vest again, without hesitation.

These fit perfectly. I read reviews that suggested I should size down and I am so glad I did. I bought the 4 (small). For reference, I am 133 lbs, waist 32-33”, hips 38-39” and I am 5’4”. Sizing is a little weird for me because my waist is fluffier than I would like and I usually have to pick between waist size or hip size on a size chart. These jeans are very forgiving for mom tummies. The high waist is perfect. Keeps everything snug but not uncomfortably tight. I decided to go with the small because I am shorter at 5.4”. The length of perfect. I put a bootie (maybe 3” heel) and the length is perfect. I am very happy with these jeans. They are snug but stretchy and the fabric is very comfortable. Love the designs on the pant legs. Super happy with this purchase. I recommend sizing down especially if you are short. However, the thighs are a little tight. I looked at the size chart and actually measured my thigh before I ordered. If you have bigger or muscular thighs, consider measuring or stay with your normal size. Also pics aren’t great and I was only wearing one shoe

I love these jeans! They’re exactly what I was looking for when I hear the word baggy. I’m 5’2 and 195 pounds, typically these types of jeans run small around the waist but no, they’re actually loose. I sized up to XXL when I’m typically a L or XL, so I did size up a bit. You might need a belt if you do size up, but otherwise I’d say to get your regular size or whatever Amazon recommends you! They’re super comfortable, and I cuffed the bottom of the jeans because it was a tad too long so I reccomend you wear platforms with them. For the shape of my body, these make my hips look wider which I love because my hips aren’t small, but I have hip dips, and these are perfect for hiding them. I’d style these with a fitted shirt! They’re totally worth the money, fabric doesn’t feel cheap at all, and is very well made. I bought the black and blue one, they’re both XXL. However, I do notice that the black one is a bit looser on the waist by a tiny bit. Not complaining though, but I know it might be important to some. I wear these all the time, everywhere I go, they’re perfect!

Gap Women's High Rise Wide Leg Cropped Jeans I really wanted to like these, but they aren’t the right fit for me. I’m 5’2" and 125lbs. In all fairness, I have no waist, very straight and short torso, so I knew these were a risk. First I bought a 4 Short and could barely breath (pictures included). I know these are intended to be a crop, but the stiffness of the jeans just looked funny to me with such a wide leg (I’m all about a wide leg jean though). There is some stretch despite the stiffness, which I attribute mostly to the thickness of the denim. I decided to try a 6 Regular hoping for a looser fit that would hit the ground (no pics from this fitting, wasn’t worth taking pictures). The fit was looser, yes, but the length still didn’t hit the floor for me. I just didn’t love it enough to try for a long length, and I wasn’t confident the stiff demon would pool correctly at my feet even if I did get a longer length. I went ahead and returned them. These are cute and I hope they work out for folks taller than myself, however I do recommend sizing up regardless or your height or length you choose.
reviewer_name_raw: Just doesn’t work for short people.

user_comment: First, an admission: I am much bigger than I need to be because of a pre-denture diet. I am 5'5" and 230lbs (up from 150 18months ago!)I was already "curvy" but have moved into the the "much-too-swurvy" lane. These pants are in the 2X size. I wanted something comfortable & flattering that I can wear on a daily basis. Surprisingly, these fit the bill. I liked this first pair so much that I ordered 2 others. The last pair must have had to crawl from China because they took forever to get here. PROS: I am STUNNED by these leggings. When I opened the package, I laughed so hard and long that I think I lost about a pound. Which I need to do more of because I have put on weight. These are so narrow-looking that I didn't think there was any way I could get them past my knee. You can see from the photo of these next to a pair of my yoga pants that things were looking quite impossible. But... WOW. Not only do these fit but they are a lot more flattering than I expected. The material stretches like some magical polymer from another world but doesn't make my fat thighs look misshapen. They look awesome with both ankle-high boots and boots that come up to mid calf. If I wear a top covering my belly, the pants give me a slimmer illusion. Also, these flatten my belly a lot more than expected & I have a LOT of belly right now. If I keep them pulled up high enough, there is no muffin top or unflattering lines under a top. That's amazing. (I won't be gracing any BBW magazine covers, but I feel more presentable.) When I am sitting, there are no embarrassing rolls showing. Wild! There are no real negatives with these because of the price. I can tell that the stitching in the crotch area might be the first to give way. If they only last 3 months each, I am fine with that. For now, I will be living in these leggings. When I lose the weight, I will have to order more of them. Wait - there is one potential negative: I look so good in these that I might be less inclined to lose the extra weight! J/K

If I keep them pulled up high enough, there is no muffin top or unflattering lines under a top. That's amazing. (I won't be gracing any BBW magazine covers, but I feel more presentable.) When I am sitting, there are no embarrassing rolls showing. Wild! There are no real negatives with these because of the price. I can tell that the stitching in the crotch area might be the first to give way. If they only last 3 months each, I am fine with that. For now, I will be living in these leggings. When I lose the weight, I will have to order more of them. Wait - there is one potential negative: I look so good in these that I might be less inclined to lose the extra weight! J/K

I recently picked up these women’s high-waisted cargo shorts and they’ve quickly become a go-to in my warm-weather wardrobe. The high waist is flattering and comfortable. It smooths the midsection without feeling tight, and pairs perfectly with everything from cropped tops to tucked-in tees. The cargo style is both trendy and practical. The pockets are generously sized and actually usable. I can fit my phone, keys, and wallet without the shorts looking bulky. The fabric feels durable but not stiff, and has just enough stretch to move with me whether I’m walking around town or heading out on a weekend adventure. Fit-wise, they hit at a flattering length that’s not too long or too short, and the cut flatters my legs while still feeling relaxed and casual. I'm 5'5" and they fit just as pictured. I also appreciate that they haven’t faded or lost shape after a few washes. The construction feels solid and holds up well. Overall, these high-waisted cargo shorts are comfortable, stylish, and versatile. Perfect for running errands, travel, or casual outings. If you want a pair of shorts that combine fashion with everyday functionality, these are definitely worth adding to your closet!

All of their jeans run large (i’ve tried three different styles). I was a sz 6/8 before pregnancy. I’m 17 week/154 lbs and have a decent sized bump and I was swimming in the mediums. I returned them for a small, and I still have a ton of room to grow into. In fact, I wondered for a moment if I should try an extra small, but that seems crazy given I’m only going to get bigger. The pants are extremely stretchy. Even with the button fly and the drawstring, it would be very easy for someone to pants me. They are definitely not as cute as they are on the model — there’s a lot of a loose fabric between the back of the waistband and the top of your butt (even after I sized down). They want to slide down my body, and I have to hike them up a lot. But they’re comfortable and they look a lot better than most other maternity pants. And unlike some of the other maternity pants that I’ve bought, I believe these ones will last me through my third trimester. My other ones are already feeling a little snug in the belly band.

WAX JEAN Women's Mom Jean with Blown Out Knee First red flag. The pants arrived in the clear warehouse plastic bag. No Amazon packaging or added protection. Tried them on- they’re at least a full size too big. I’m a size 6, 7, or 28. 150lbs hourglass shape. Tiny waist big hips. The waist actually fit alright. The pockets, zipper area and thighs were baggy. Thought maybe I could wash them to tighten up. But then I saw a HUGE HOLE on the inner thigh… if I do replace I would get a size 5. And make sure it comes in Amazon packaging. Genuinely disappointed

I own several pieces from luvamia at this point, and they’ve consistently proven to be a solid, reliable brand. This long-sleeve, lightweight, denim button-down is another strong example of that consistency. The fabric is soft for denim and comfortable enough for all-day wear. The seam work and stitching are clean and well finished. I ordered a size medium in Indigo Dark Blue. For reference, I’m 5’3” and 154 lbs. The color is accurate to the listing, a rich, classic dark indigo that works year-round. The sizing is exactly true to size. And I mean exactly. It fits well, but it’s a more tailored fit than I expected with very little give or stretch. If you’re planning to layer it over tees or tanks, I would recommend sizing up for a bit more room. I personally wish I had gone up one size to give myself more layering flexibility. The Western style is subtle without leaning into costume territory. The two flap chest pockets are fully functional. The cuff buttons are a little tight, but not impossible. The sleeve length is spot on for my height, and they look great worn down or rolled up for a more casual feel. Styling-wise, this is versatile. It looks just as good with jeans for a classic denim-on-denim look as it does with a short or long skirt. It would work for casual days, concerts, smart-casual office settings, or layered open as a lightweight overshirt. I washed and dried it normally with no special settings and it did not fade or shrink in the wash. At the time of my review, the price is $31.99. Here’s where I hesitate. The quality is very good for fast fashion but this isn’t a premium heritage denim brand. The construction is solid, but the pricing is pushing into territory where you start comparing it to higher-end options. For low-to-mid $20s, this would feel like a no-brainer. At just under $32, it’s priced overconfidently.

My kiddo has longer legs for her height (5’3”), but these jeans are still pretty long. She does love them though. The fit is right for her waist and she thinks they look girly without being over the top. She wears those 2-3 days a week, so they’re pretty comfortable and have held up well to the wear and washings. There are no words for how much I love this top!!! It is so pretty! Just like the seller’s photos in every way. It is insanely soft and the fabric feels sturdy and well made, it’s not a flimsy loose knit at all. I purchased size Large and I’m 5’7” - 168 lbs - 36B and it fits perfectly!! The way it falls is just amazing and just like the photos show. The color is the most stunning shade of orange. I love it and I need more colors!!! Get this one you’ll be so glad you did!

Angels Forever Young Women's Forever Wide High-Rise Patch Pockets Jeans Just like wearing your favorite comfy sweatpants! For reference, I am around 5’6” and 125lbs. I bought the 6. Photo (excuse the cat toys) shows length which is spot on. Perhaps a bit long for some but it works for me. I have two thick shirts tucked into the waistband, yet it still feels amazing! And bonus points for any of my fellow eczema sufferers, there are no rivets! Woohoo! The only metal is the closure button/zipper. All pockets are rivet-free. What a fabulous find!

CAKE THE LABEL Women's Mid Rise Regular Straight Jeans Classic Pants I’ve lost weight in the last year and I guess it all came off my butt 🤦‍♀️😩 because the butt of all my jeans ends up sagging like a loaded diaper after only wearing them an hour or so. I’ve tried SO many pairs off of Amazon recently, most of them Levi’s and all of them returned with a hard pass. I finally ordered these and wish I had sooner because these are PERFECTION in denim form. I’m apple shaped, with narrow hips and big boobs. I’m a Torrid size 2 on top, and wear a 33 in most jeans (Abercrombie/Agolde) depending on how much stretch they have. I ordered a 33 in these and it’s like they were tailor made for me. The waist is perfect and flattering and not high but medium, hitting me right at my belly button and the top of my hip bones. (I’m super short waisted) These manage to flatten my stomach AND flatter my butt, which is nearly impossible to do. Usually if jeans fit me in the waist they’re too big in the hips/butt and visa versa. The material is soft and stretchy without having so much stretch that you’re basically just wearing unflattering denim leggings… you know the ones. 😒 I’m 5’6 and they’re slightly long on me, but since the leg opening isn’t super wide it’s not an issue. They are a TRUE straight leg, which is why I’ve returned so many pairs lately… they’ll be described as straight but be huge and wide leg on me OR they’ll be described as straight and be more like skinny jeans. I know this is par for the course when buying jeans online, though. Thankfully my search is over! I’d give these 20 stars if I could, highly HIGHLY recommend! 🙌🏻🙌🏻🙌🏻

I wasn’t sure how the quality or fit would be, as I feel like Amazon can be hit or miss. However, these were totally a winner for me. I got beige and it’s really a pretty color. The material seems really thin at first... like “can you see what color underwear I’m wearing” thin. However, they're not see through and don’t feel too fragile. I’m 5’0” and these were wayyyy too long for me. I cut probably 5-7 inches of length off the bottom and they are still just above the ground. They don’t have much stretch at all, but are still very comfortable. Even with those things considered, I love the fit and the style!!! I get so many compliment. They’re arguably my favorite pants. I just hope they hold up overtime.

I’m 5’2” and about 165–168 lbs for reference. The denim is very heavy and structured with minimal stretch, but it feels high quality. I ordered a L and 2XL to compare. The L was too small in the thighs (couldn’t get them past my butt), and the 2XL had way too much extra room. The XL ended up being the perfect fit. They were a little snug across the front of the seat when sitting at first, but I got used to it. Once I was settled into them, they were pretty comfortable. Overall they worked perfectly for my event and I would definitely buy them again.

This denim babydoll top surprised me in the best way. For reference, I ordered a size Medium and wear a DDD cup, and it was still very flattering. The structured denim gives it shape without pulling awkwardly across the chest, which can be hard to find with this style. The fabric is slightly stiff, but in a good way, it helps the puff sleeves and peplum shape hold their structure so the top looks polished instead of flimsy. It doesn’t collapse or wrinkle easily, and the silhouette feels intentional and styled. The overall aesthetic is very cute and outdoorsy. It feels perfect for casual daytime activities like apple picking, visiting a farm, going to an outdoor market, or even just walking around an outlet mall on a nice day. It has that effortless, slightly vintage-inspired summer vibe. Overall, I was genuinely impressed with how good it looked on and how flattering the cut was. Definitely 5 stars for fit, structure, and style.

EVALESS Womens Boyfriend Washed Crop Denim Jean Jacket Coat I have this jacket in black, medium blue and the lighter blue and am seriously considering the grey and white! There is nothing so versatile as this denim jacket to add to my collection. I love the distressed look because it gives the jacket a “edgy” look and feel. Once out of the package and washed the material settles in to a nice softness. They fraying is done well enough that it doesn’t unravel or deconstruct after wearing or laundering. I am 5”11 170lbs and wear an XL. I could get away with a L but I love to layer a hoodie underneath quite often. I would definitely size according to how you wear your jackets as there is a slight stretch to it. The overall fit is comfortable and gives you that long loved jean jacket feel. A great value for the money and highly recommended. Another winner from the Evaless brand…versatile, stylish, and on point. I always get compliments on my clothing and my ability to mix brand names with my Amazon FAVORITES and this jacket is ✨.

These high-waisted, wide-leg cropped jeans are amazing! At 5'3", they hit right at my ankle, which I love. The stretchy denim makes them super comfortable, and I wear them year-round. Lately, I’ve been pairing them with black kitten-heel boots for a chic look, and they also look great with my navy sweater that has matching buttons. Stylish, versatile, and flattering, highly recommend!

My dimensions are 5'3", 125 lbs, shoulder width 15", bust 36" to 38", waist 28" to 29" and I ordered medium in Almond Brown. The fit is true to size and the color is as shown in the advertisement. (Color in photo's is not an accurate depiction do to lighting.) The fit is a relaxed fit with a mild stretch; if a looser fit is preferred I recommend ordering up one size. The material is a soft, medium weight, denim that is comprised mostly of cotton with a touch of Viscose. Usually, I would not be a fan of polyester (21%) in denim with this weight, however I find it helps with wrinkle prevention and the durability of the material. I have washed this top several times and if removed quickly from the dryer I am not burdened with too many wrinkles. I do have to use a low heat iron on the pocket flaps and collar area for a more crisp appearance. The double button cuffs, traditional collared V-neck and finished seam detail offer the wearer versatility; Hence, I have worn this top to work in a "business casual," manner and with shorts to tool around town. "Luvamia" continues to impress me with the quality of their products. This is the fourth product I have received from them and once again they deliver on style, comfort, fit, color and quality. If you prefer to invest in pieces that can provide more than one use this is an excellent investment.

Size stats: pre pregnancy- 5’2”, 216lbs, size 16/18, XL, 1X. Current size-26 weeks pregnant. 5’2”, 212lbs, making my 18’s work but needed maternity jeans for sureee. These are great! I sized down because my pants size is determined by my stomach and less so my hips/legs. (I was a 18/XL pre-pregnancy and bought the large.) The over the stomach band isn’t too tight and I think I’ll be able to wear them the whole pregnancy, the only issue I see happening is that the band of the “jeans” that buttons is a big tight already, but I’ll just have to have it under the belly and not trying to stretch up as much as I can. The thighs and calves are snug without feeling skin tight. No bagginess. I’m 5’2”, and the length is perfect. I could roll them up once for a more tailored fit but I’m not going to get my hem wet by accident either way. I like the wash and the subtle wear on them. Butt is very flattering which is hard to find in maternity jeans. Real front pockets that would fit more than half a hand! Lol Worth the money.

Size stats: pre pregnancy- 5’2”, 216lbs, size 16/18, XL, 1X. Current size-26 weeks pregnant. 5’2”, 212lbs, making my 18’s work but needed maternity jeans for sureee. These are great! I sized down because my pants size is determined by my stomach and less so my hips/legs. (I was a 18/XL pre-pregnancy and bought the large.) The over the stomach band isn’t too tight and I think I’ll be able to wear them the whole pregnancy, the only issue I see happening is that the band of the “jeans” that buttons is a big tight already, but I’ll just have to have it under the belly and not trying to stretch up as much as I can. The thighs and calves are snug without feeling skin tight. No bagginess. I’m 5’2”, and the length is perfect. I could roll them up once for a more tailored fit but I’m not going to get my hem wet by accident either way. I like the wash and the subtle wear on them. Butt is very flattering which is hard to find in maternity jeans. Real front pockets that would fit more than half a hand! Lol Worth the money.

I have been a sucker for camo for years. I'm so happy they haven't gone out of style. I found a cute way to dress them up with my jewelry . Got my inspiration from Kira and came right here on Amazon to make a purchase. I'm only 5 ft tall so they're pretty darn long That didn't diminish it for me though. I just bought some iron on quick hem and that did the trick so they're no longer dragging. The material is not very heavy or thick. The quality isn't bad, material isn't thick at all. These won't keep you warm in the winter if that's what you're looking for.

Love these, but sizing is way off. Very small, order at least one size up. I love the choice of colors, the tapered leg is nice too. They have a little bit of stretch, but not too much that they will be falling off after you wear them for a while. Not as heavy as a denim, but heavy enough that they’re not see-through. I am typically a size 9, but the 11 fit me much better. I could get the nines on, but they were tight and clingy. The ones pictured in me are size 11. I liked them so much, I bought two more pairs. Hopefully the sizing is consistent. For reference, I am 5’6 and around 140lbs, I am in my 50’s.
